attributes.description: Total fat content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory nutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Includes saturated fat, trans fat, polyunsaturated fat, and monounsaturated fat.

attributes.name: total_sugars_g
attributes.description: Total sugars content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory sub-component of total carbohydrate on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Includes both naturally occurring and added sugars.

attributes.name: trans_fat_g
attributes.description: Trans fat content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory sub-component of total fat on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Regulatory threshold: items with less than 0.5g may declare 0g per FDA rounding rules.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number of this nutrition profile for the associated menu item. Incremented each time a recipe reformulation, ingredient substitution, or regulatory re-analysis results in a new nutrition profile. Supports historical audit trail and regulatory change management.

attributes.name: vitamin_d_mcg
attributes.description: Vitamin D content per declared serving size in micrograms. Mandatory micronutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel per 2016 labeling rule update. Expressed as micrograms (mcg) with percent Daily Value (%DV) calculated separately.

products.name: allergen_declaration
products.description: Regulatory master record capturing the allergen profile for each menu item per FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA) requirements. Tracks the presence, may-contain, and absence status for the 9 major FDA allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame), cross-contact risk level, declaration date, and regulatory review status.
products.reference: FALCPA Allergen Declaration / FDA Major Food Allergens

attributes.name: allergen_declaration_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each allergen declaration record. Primary key for the allergen_declaration data product in the menu domain.

attributes.name: created_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Allergen declarations are authored by a qualified employee; tracking origin supports regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the menu item for which this allergen declaration applies. Links the allergen profile to the specific sellable item in the menu master.

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.recipe. Business justification: An allergen declaration is assessed against a specific recipe version. allergen_declaration currently carries recipe_version as a STRING denormalization. Adding recipe_id FK to recipe normalizes this

attributes.name: sop_document_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.sop_document. Business justification: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and local health codes require allergen declarations to reference the governing SOP (e.g., cross-contact prevention, allergen handling protocol). Food safety compliance offi

attributes.name: superseded_by_allergen_declaration_id
attributes.description: Reference to the allergen_declaration_id of the newer declaration that supersedes this record when a recipe change or regulatory update requires a revised allergen profile. Null if this is the current active declaration. Enables full version history traversal.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized approver (e.g., Director of Food Safety, QA Manager) who granted final approval for this allergen declaration. Required for regulatory audit compliance.

attributes.name: channel_applicability
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of service channels for which this allergen declaration is applicable (e.g., DT, OLO, 3PD, dine-in, catering). Supports channel-specific menu publishing and allergen disclosure requirements across Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dt|olo|3pd|dine_in|catering|kiosk|mobile_app — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this allergen declaration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance documentation requirements.

attributes.name: cross_contact_risk_level
attributes.description: Assessed risk level for unintentional allergen cross-contact during food preparation in the restaurant kitchen (BOH). Determined through HACCP analysis of shared equipment, preparation surfaces, and ingredient handling procedures. Drives operational protocols and guest advisories.

attributes.name: cross_contact_source
attributes.description: Free-text description of the identified source(s) of potential allergen cross-contact risk in the kitchen environment (e.g., shared fryer oil, shared prep surfaces, shared utensils, proximity to allergen-containing ingredients). Supports HACCP documentation and staff training.

attributes.name: daypart_applicability
attributes.description: Daypart(s) during which this allergen declaration is applicable (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, all_day). Relevant when the same menu item has different ingredient formulations by daypart, resulting in different allergen profiles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: breakfast|lunch|dinner|late_night|snack|all_day — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: declaration_date
attributes.description: The business date on which this allergen declaration was formally issued or last certified by the food safety team. Used for regulatory compliance tracking and HACCP documentation.

attributes.name: declaration_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for this allergen declaration record, used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-system references. Format: ALGN-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.

attributes.name: declaration_status
attributes.description: Current regulatory review and approval lifecycle status of this allergen declaration. Controls whether the declaration is active and publishable to guests and regulatory bodies. Valid values: draft, pending_review, approved, superseded, withdrawn.

attributes.name: declaration_type
attributes.description: Classification of the allergen declaration by menu item category context. Standard declarations apply to core menu items; LTO (Limited Time Offer) declarations apply to temporary promotional items; seasonal and regional declarations apply to market-specific or time-limited variants; custom applies to made-to-order or catering items.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: The date from which this allergen declaration is binding and must be communicated to guests across all channels (DT, OLO, 3PD, in-restaurant). Aligns with menu item launch or recipe change effective date.

attributes.name: eggs_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of eggs allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The date on which this allergen declaration expires and must be reviewed or renewed. Null indicates the declaration remains valid until superseded. Supports periodic review cycles mandated by food safety SOPs.

attributes.name: fish_species
attributes.description: Specific fish species present in the menu item (e.g., salmon, tuna, tilapia, cod) when fish_status is 'contains'. FDA FALCPA requires species-level disclosure for fish allergens on food labels.

attributes.name: fish_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of fish allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: gluten_free_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether this menu item has been certified as gluten-free per FDA gluten-free labeling rule (21 CFR Part 101.91), requiring less than 20 ppm gluten. Distinct from wheat allergen status — an item may be wheat-free but not certified gluten-free.

attributes.name: guest_advisory_text
attributes.description: Standardized guest-facing allergen advisory statement for this menu item, suitable for display on menus, digital ordering platforms (OLO, 3PD), and in-restaurant signage. Drafted in compliance with FDA menu labeling and FTC advertising regulations.

attributes.name: haccp_ccp_reference
attributes.business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier for the HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) associated with allergen management for this menu item. Links the allergen declaration to the formal HACCP plan documentation maintained by the food safety team.

tag_set.value: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Reference

attributes.name: internal_notes
attributes.description: Internal food safety team notes regarding this allergen declaration, including rationale for cross-contact risk assessments, supplier-provided allergen data caveats, or pending ingredient change investigations. Not for guest-facing use. Classified as confidential business information.

attributes.name: may_contain_allergen_count
attributes.description: Count of the 9 FDA major allergens declared as 'may_contain' (cross-contact risk) status for this menu item. Supports risk stratification, guest advisory communications, and HACCP reporting.

attributes.name: milk_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of milk (dairy) allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: peanuts_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of peanuts allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: regulatory_submission_date
attributes.description: The date on which this allergen declaration was formally submitted to the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, local health department). Null if regulatory_submission_required is false or submission is pending.

attributes.name: regulatory_submission_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether this allergen declaration requires formal submission to a regulatory body (e.g., FDA, local health department) as part of menu labeling compliance. Drives workflow routing to the regulatory affairs team.

attributes.name: restaurant_format_applicability
attributes.description: Restaurant format(s) for which this allergen declaration applies. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Supports multi-format operations where the same menu item may have different preparation methods and allergen profiles by format.

attributes.name: review_date
attributes.description: The date on which this allergen declaration was last reviewed by the food safety or regulatory compliance team. Supports audit readiness and HACCP critical control point documentation.

attributes.name: reviewed_by
attributes.description: Name or employee identifier of the food safety officer or regulatory compliance specialist who last reviewed and certified this allergen declaration. Supports audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: sesame_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of sesame allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements (sesame added as the 9th major allergen effective January 1, 2023 under FASTER Act). Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: shellfish_species
attributes.description: Specific shellfish species present in the menu item (e.g., shrimp, crab, lobster, crayfish) when shellfish_status is 'contains'. FDA FALCPA requires species-level disclosure for crustacean shellfish allergens.

attributes.name: shellfish_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of shellfish (crustacean shellfish) allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: soybeans_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of soybeans allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: total_allergen_count
attributes.description: Count of the 9 FDA major allergens declared as 'contains' status for this menu item. Used in menu engineering, guest-facing allergen filtering tools, and regulatory reporting to quickly identify high-allergen items.

attributes.name: tree_nut_varieties
attributes.description: Specific tree nut varieties present in the menu item (e.g., almonds, cashews, walnuts, pecans, pistachios) when tree_nuts_status is 'contains'. FDA FALCPA requires variety-level disclosure for tree nut allergens.

attributes.name: tree_nuts_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of tree nuts allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this allergen declaration record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any allergen status, cross-contact risk assessment, or regulatory review field. Supports change management and audit compliance.

attributes.name: wheat_status
attributes.description: Declares the presence status of wheat allergen in this menu item per FDA FALCPA major allergen requirements. Values: contains (intentionally present), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (absent and no cross-contact risk).

attributes.name: zenput_audit_task_code
attributes.description: Reference identifier for the Zenput food safety audit task or operational compliance checklist item associated with the verification of this allergen declaration in restaurant operations. Supports traceability between the master declaration and field-level compliance verification.

products.name: modifier_group
products.description: Master record for groups of customization options (modifiers) applicable to menu items — e.g., Protein Choice, Sauce Selection, Size Upgrade, Add-Ons. Captures group name, selection type (single/multi-select), min/max selections, display name, and item applicability rules (which menu items this group attaches to, display order, required/optional flag, channel-specific overrides). Drives POS and OLO customization logic and item-to-modifier mapping.
products.reference: Modifier Group / Condiment Group / POS Modifier Set

attributes.name: modifier_group_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the modifier group record. Primary key for the modifier_group data product in the menu domain. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

attributes.name: approved_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Modifier group approval is part of menu governance — the approving employee is required for POS configuration change audits and franchise menu compliance reporting. 'approved_by' plain text is denorma

attributes.name: allergen_relevance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether any modifier options within this group may introduce allergen variations that require disclosure to guests. True = allergen-relevant group requiring allergen declaration logic at POS/OLO; False = no allergen impact. Supports FDA food labeling compliance and HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) protocols.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier group configuration was formally approved for production deployment by the authorized menu engineering or operations team. Supports menu governance workflows and audit compliance.

attributes.name: calorie_impact_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether modifier selections within this group materially affect the calorie count of the parent menu item, triggering calorie range display requirements on menus and digital ordering platforms. Supports FDA menu labeling compliance (21 CFR Part 101).

attributes.name: channel_applicability
attributes.description: Defines which ordering channels (DT = Drive-Thru, OLO = Online Ordering, 3PD = Third-Party Delivery, POS = in-store Point of Sale) this modifier group is active on. Enables channel-specific menu configuration and supports omnichannel menu engineering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|pos_only|olo_only|dt_only|3pd_only|pos_olo|pos_dt — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this modifier group record was first created in the system of record. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.

attributes.name: daypart_applicability
attributes.description: Indicates which daypart(s) this modifier group is available during. Dayparts are standard foodservice time segments (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, snack) used for menu engineering, PMIX analysis, and POS time-based menu activation.

attributes.name: default_modifier_code
attributes.description: The business code of the modifier option that is pre-selected by default when a guest views this modifier group on POS or OLO. Supports speed of service (SOS) optimization by pre-populating the most common selection. Null if no default applies.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the modifier group's purpose, scope, and applicable customization context. Used by menu engineers and franchise operators for documentation and training materials.

attributes.name: display_name
attributes.description: Customer-facing label shown on POS screens, KDS (Kitchen Display System), OLO (Online Ordering) interfaces, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) menus. May differ from the internal group_name for marketing or localization purposes. Example: 'Choose Your Protein', 'Pick a Sauce'.

attributes.name: display_sequence
attributes.description: Numeric ordering position that controls the sequence in which this modifier group is presented to the guest on POS screens, KDS, OLO, and 3PD interfaces. Lower values appear first. Enables menu engineers to control the customization flow and upsell sequencing.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this modifier group configuration is no longer valid. Null for open-ended configurations. Supports scheduled menu deactivation, LTO (Limited Time Offer) lifecycle management, and menu version control.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this modifier group configuration is considered active and valid for use in POS, OLO, and 3PD channels. Supports menu lifecycle management, scheduled menu changes, and NRO (New Restaurant Opening) menu activation.

attributes.name: free_modifier_limit
attributes.description: Number of modifier selections within this group that are included at no additional charge. Selections beyond this limit may incur an upcharge per the individual modifier's pricing. A value of 0 means all selections are charged; null means unlimited free selections. Supports COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) management and pricing strategy.

attributes.name: group_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the modifier group, used in POS configuration, OLO platform integration, and 3PD channel mapping. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Example: 'PROT-CHOICE', 'SAUCE-SEL', 'SIZE-UPG'.

attributes.name: group_name
attributes.description: Internal business name for the modifier group used in back-office systems, menu engineering reports, and POS configuration. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Example: 'Protein Choice', 'Sauce Selection', 'Size Upgrade'.

attributes.name: group_type
attributes.description: Categorical classification of the modifier group by its culinary or operational function. Drives menu engineering analysis (PMIX), reporting by customization category, and POS screen layout. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|sauce|size|add_on|side|beverage|preparation|topping|crust|dietary — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: image_url
attributes.description: URL pointing to the visual asset (image) representing this modifier group on digital ordering platforms (OLO, kiosk, 3PD). Used to enhance the guest customization experience on digital channels. Null if no image is assigned.

attributes.name: is_collapsible
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier group can be collapsed or hidden in the guest-facing UI when not interacted with. True = group can be collapsed to reduce screen clutter on OLO and kiosk interfaces; False = always fully expanded. Supports UX optimization for digital ordering channels.

attributes.name: is_franchise_configurable
attributes.description: Indicates whether franchise operators are permitted to modify the configuration of this modifier group (e.g., adjust min/max selections, add local modifiers) within the bounds of the franchise agreement. True = franchisee may configure; False = corporate-locked configuration. Supports franchise management and brand standards compliance.

attributes.name: is_kds_displayed
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier group and its selected options are displayed on the KDS (Kitchen Display System) in the BOH (Back of House). True = modifier group selections are shown to kitchen staff; False = suppressed from KDS display (e.g., packaging or FOH-only modifiers). Supports BOH operational efficiency.

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier group is associated with a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotional campaign. True = LTO modifier group with defined start/end dates; False = permanent menu modifier group. Supports marketing campaign execution and menu lifecycle management.

attributes.name: is_printed_on_receipt
attributes.description: Indicates whether the modifier group's selected options are printed on the guest receipt. True = selections appear on receipt for guest reference; False = suppressed from receipt printing. Supports guest transparency and order accuracy.

attributes.name: is_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the guest must make at least one selection from this modifier group before the parent menu item can be added to the order. True = mandatory selection; False = optional. Derived operationally from min_selections > 0 but stored explicitly for POS/OLO rendering logic.

attributes.name: localization_key
attributes.description: Reference key used to look up translated display names and descriptions for this modifier group in multi-language markets. Supports international franchise operations and localized guest-facing menu presentation across global markets.

attributes.name: lto_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier group is deactivated as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion. Null for permanent modifier groups. Used by POS and OLO systems for automated menu deactivation scheduling.

attributes.name: lto_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier group becomes available as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion. Null for permanent modifier groups. Used by POS and OLO systems for automated menu activation scheduling.

attributes.name: max_selections
attributes.description: Maximum number of modifier options a guest may select from this group. For single-select groups this is typically 1. For multi-select groups this caps the number of add-ons or customizations. Enforced at POS and OLO order validation.

attributes.name: min_selections
attributes.description: Minimum number of modifier options a guest must select from this group to complete an order. A value of 0 indicates the group is optional. A value of 1 or more makes the group mandatory. Enforced at POS and OLO order validation.

attributes.name: modifier_group_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the modifier group. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. 'active' = currently in use on POS/OLO; 'inactive' = temporarily disabled; 'draft' = under menu engineering review; 'archived' = retired from all channels; 'suspended' = paused due to supply or compliance issue.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for operational notes, menu engineering commentary, or franchise-specific instructions related to this modifier group. Used by menu engineers, franchise business consultants, and operations teams for context that does not fit structured fields.

attributes.name: olo_group_ref
attributes.description: Native identifier assigned to this modifier group within the Olo Digital Ordering Platform. Used for synchronization between the enterprise menu master and the OLO channel, ensuring consistent customization options across in-store and digital ordering.

attributes.name: pmix_tracking_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether modifier selections within this group are tracked in PMIX (Product Mix) reporting for menu engineering analysis. True = modifier selection data flows into PMIX analytics; False = excluded from PMIX reporting (e.g., administrative or packaging modifiers). Supports menu engineering and revenue management decisions.

attributes.name: pos_group_ref
attributes.business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Modifier Group Reference
attributes.description: Native identifier or reference code assigned to this modifier group within the Oracle MICROS POS system. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the POS system of record during menu synchronization and audit processes.

tag_set.value: Point of Sale (POS) Modifier Group Reference

attributes.name: restaurant_format_applicability
attributes.description: Specifies which restaurant format(s) — QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, or fine dining — this modifier group applies to. Supports multi-format menu governance across the enterprise's diverse restaurant portfolio.

attributes.name: selection_type
attributes.description: Defines whether a guest may select only one modifier (single-select, e.g., Size Upgrade) or multiple modifiers (multi-select, e.g., Add-Ons, Sauce Selection) from this group. Drives POS and OLO customization logic and guest-facing UI rendering.

attributes.name: sort_order_method
attributes.description: Defines how individual modifier options within this group are sorted when displayed to the guest. 'manual' = display_sequence on each modifier; 'alphabetical' = A-Z by modifier name; 'price_asc/desc' = by price; 'popularity' = by historical selection frequency from PMIX data. Supports menu engineering and upsell optimization.

attributes.name: upcharge_basis
attributes.description: Defines how additional charges are applied when a guest selects modifiers beyond the free_modifier_limit. 'per_item' = each additional modifier is charged individually; 'flat_group' = a single flat charge applies to the group regardless of count; 'none' = no upcharges apply. Drives POS pricing logic and revenue management.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this modifier group record was last modified in the system of record. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance in the lakehouse silver layer.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Monotonically incrementing version counter for this modifier group record. Incremented each time the group configuration is updated. Supports menu change management, audit trails, and rollback capabilities in the lakehouse silver layer.

products.name: menu_modifier
products.description: Master record for each individual modifier option within a modifier group — e.g., 'Extra Cheese', 'No Onions', 'Large Size', 'Gluten-Free Bun'. Captures modifier name, modifier group reference, price delta (upcharge or discount), calorie delta, default selection flag, availability status, and channel applicability. Feeds Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform for guest customization. [SSOT] Authoritative owner for concept 'modifier'.

attributes.name: menu_modifier_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each modifier option record in the master modifier catalog. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references from Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform.

attributes.name: created_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Modifiers (e.g., add‑ons) are defined by a chef or manager; tracking creator aids cost and allergen management.

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent menu item to which this modifier is applicable. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) analysis and menu engineering decisions at the item-modifier level.

attributes.name: modifier_group_id
attributes.description: Reference to the modifier group to which this modifier option belongs (e.g., 'Cheese Options', 'Bun Type', 'Size'). Establishes the parent-child grouping used by Oracle MICROS POS for menu customization display.

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.recipe. Business justification: Menu modifiers (e.g., Extra Cheese, Grilled Chicken, Avocado Add-On) often have their own standardized preparation recipes — how to prepare and apply the modifier. Currently menu_modifier carries prep

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Modifiers (e.g., extra cheese, add bacon) consume physical stock items. Linking menu_modifier to stock_item enables modifier-level inventory deduction, cost delta validation, and stock availability ch

attributes.name: allergen_flags
attributes.description: Pipe-delimited list of major food allergens introduced or removed by this modifier, per FDA's 9 major allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree_nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame). Example: 'milk|wheat'. Critical for guest safety, HACCP compliance, and allergen declaration on digital ordering platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or employee identifier of the menu engineering or culinary team member who approved this modifier for inclusion in the menu catalog. Supports governance, audit trail, and change management for menu updates.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier was formally approved by the menu engineering team for deployment to production POS and digital ordering systems.

attributes.name: available_channels
attributes.description: Pipe-delimited list of ordering channels where this modifier is available (e.g., 'dine_in|drive_thru|olo|3pd'). Supports channel-specific menu configuration for DT (Drive-Thru), OLO (Online Ordering), and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dine_in|drive_thru|olo|3pd|kiosk|catering — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: available_dayparts
attributes.description: Pipe-delimited list of dayparts during which this modifier is available (e.g., 'breakfast|lunch|dinner|late_night'). Enables daypart-specific menu engineering and ensures modifiers are only offered during operationally appropriate time windows.

attributes.name: calorie_delta
attributes.description: The incremental calorie change (in kilocalories) added to or subtracted from the base menu item's calorie count when this modifier is selected. Positive for additions, negative for removals. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance and nutritional transparency on digital ordering channels.

attributes.name: carbohydrate_delta_g
attributes.description: Incremental change in total carbohydrate content (in grams) when this modifier is applied. Required for FDA nutritional labeling and supports guest dietary needs (e.g., low-carb, diabetic-friendly options).

attributes.name: cogs_delta
attributes.business_glossary_term: Modifier Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Delta
attributes.description: The incremental raw ingredient cost (COGS) associated with applying this modifier, expressed in the local currency. Used for menu engineering profitability analysis, CoGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculations, and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting at the modifier level.

tag_set.value: Modifier Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Delta

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this modifier record was first created in the menu management system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the price_delta (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the modifier option for internal documentation, menu engineering analysis, and digital channel display (e.g., online ordering, third-party delivery apps).

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this modifier record is no longer valid in the menu catalog. Null for currently active modifiers. Enables historical menu reconstruction for PMIX analysis, audit, and regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this modifier record is valid and active in the menu catalog. Supports temporal menu versioning, NRO (New Restaurant Opening) menu rollouts, and historical PMIX (Product Mix) analysis.

attributes.name: fat_delta_g
attributes.description: Incremental change in total fat content (in grams) when this modifier is applied to the base menu item. Required for FDA nutritional labeling compliance on menus and digital ordering platforms.

attributes.name: image_url
attributes.description: URL pointing to the digital image asset for this modifier, used on kiosk displays, OLO (Online Ordering) platforms, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) integrations to visually represent the modifier option to guests.

attributes.name: is_allergen_free
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier introduces zero major allergens (True) or introduces at least one allergen (False). Used for rapid allergen filtering on digital ordering platforms and guest-facing allergen transparency tools.

attributes.name: is_available
attributes.description: Real-time availability flag indicating whether this modifier is currently orderable (True) or temporarily unavailable due to ingredient stockout, 86'd item, or operational hold (False). Distinct from status, which reflects the lifecycle state; this flag reflects operational availability at a point in time.

attributes.name: is_default
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier is pre-selected by default when a guest orders the parent menu item (True) or must be explicitly chosen (False). Controls default state in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform order flows.

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.business_glossary_term: Modifier Is Limited Time Offer (LTO) Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier is part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion (True) or is a permanent menu option (False). LTO modifiers are subject to promotional campaign timelines and require coordinated activation/deactivation across all channels.

tag_set.value: Modifier Is Limited Time Offer (LTO) Flag

attributes.name: is_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a selection from this modifier's group is mandatory before the order can be completed (True) or optional (False). For example, selecting a size may be required for a beverage item. Enforced at POS and digital ordering checkout.

attributes.name: lto_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier's LTO availability expires. Null for permanent modifiers. Triggers automatic deactivation in POS and digital ordering systems upon expiry.

attributes.name: lto_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this modifier becomes available as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) campaign. Null for permanent modifiers. Used to schedule activation in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform.

attributes.name: max_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum number of times this modifier can be applied to a single menu item in one order (e.g., a guest can add up to 3 extra cheese slices). Enforced at POS and digital ordering checkout to control portion and cost.

attributes.name: menu_modifier_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the modifier option. 'active' indicates available for ordering; 'inactive' is temporarily unavailable; 'discontinued' is permanently removed; 'pending_approval' is awaiting menu engineering sign-off; 'seasonal' is available only during specific periods.

attributes.name: modifier_type
attributes.description: Categorical classification of the modifier's operational intent. 'addition' adds an ingredient (e.g., Extra Cheese), 'removal' removes an ingredient (e.g., No Onions), 'substitution' replaces an ingredient (e.g., Gluten-Free Bun), 'size_upsize'/'size_downsize' changes portion size, 'preparation' specifies cooking instruction (e.g., Well Done). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: addition|removal|substitution|size_upsize|size_downsize|preparation — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Human-readable display name of the modifier option as it appears on POS terminals, KDS (Kitchen Display System), and digital ordering channels (e.g., 'Extra Cheese', 'No Onions', 'Gluten-Free Bun', 'Large Size').

attributes.name: plu_code
attributes.description: Price Look-Up code assigned to this modifier in Oracle MICROS POS for transaction processing and sales reporting. Used for PMIX (Product Mix) reporting and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) tracking at the modifier level.

attributes.name: pos_button_color
attributes.description: Hexadecimal color code for the modifier's button on Oracle MICROS POS touchscreen terminals. Used to visually differentiate modifier categories (e.g., additions in green, removals in red) to improve FOH (Front of House) order accuracy and speed of service (SOS).

attributes.name: price_delta
attributes.description: The incremental price adjustment applied to the base menu item price when this modifier is selected. Positive values represent upcharges (e.g., +1.50 for Extra Cheese); negative values represent discounts or removals (e.g., -0.50 for No Protein). Zero indicates no price change. Used by Oracle MICROS POS for real-time transaction pricing.

attributes.name: protein_delta_g
attributes.description: Incremental change in protein content (in grams) when this modifier is applied. Supports FDA nutritional labeling requirements and enables protein-focused menu analytics for health-oriented menu engineering.

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: Identifies the restaurant format(s) for which this modifier is applicable: QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Supports format-specific menu configuration across the franchise and company-owned portfolio.

attributes.name: short_name
attributes.description: Abbreviated display name used on KDS (Kitchen Display System) screens and printed receipts where space is constrained (e.g., 'XTR CHSE', 'NO ONI', 'GF BUN').

attributes.name: sku
attributes.description: SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) code linking this modifier to the inventory and supply chain systems (MarketMan Inventory Management, SAP S/4HANA MM module) for ingredient-level cost tracking and PAR Level management.

attributes.name: sodium_delta_mg
attributes.description: Incremental change in sodium content (in milligrams) when this modifier is applied. Critical for FDA menu labeling compliance and guest dietary transparency, particularly for health-conscious consumers and those managing sodium intake.

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: Numeric sequence controlling the display order of this modifier within its modifier group on POS terminals, KDS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Supports menu engineering decisions on modifier prominence.

attributes.name: tax_category_code
attributes.description: Tax category code assigned to this modifier for POS tax calculation purposes. Certain modifiers (e.g., alcoholic additions, specific food categories) may carry different tax treatment than the base item. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for tax compliance.

attributes.name: unavailability_reason
attributes.description: Reason code explaining why this modifier is currently unavailable (is_available = False). Supports operational reporting, supply chain issue tracking, and guest communication on digital ordering platforms.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this modifier record was most recently modified in the menu management system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.

products.name: item_cost
products.description: Transactional record capturing the theoretical COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) for each menu item at a point in time, derived from recipe BOM and current ingredient purchase prices. Tracks theoretical food cost amount, theoretical CoGS%, actual CoGS% (from inventory management systems like MarketMan), variance amount, variance percentage, cost calculation date, ingredient price snapshot reference, and cost approval status. Updated when ingredient prices change (weekly/daily frequency) — distinct from pmix_record which captures periodic sales performance. Feeds menu engineering decisions, pricing optimization, P&L reporting, and franchise profitability benchmarking.
products.reference: Theoretical Food Cost / COGS per Item

attributes.name: item_cost_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each item cost record. Primary key for the item_cost data product in the menu domain.

attributes.name: approved_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Item cost approval is a formal financial control process — the approving employee is required for P&L accountability, food cost variance reporting, and internal audit trails. 'approved_by' plain text

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the menu item for which this theoretical cost record is calculated. Links to the menu.menu_item master product.

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific recipe version (Bill of Materials) used to derive the theoretical cost. Ensures cost traceability to the exact ingredient composition at time of calculation.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Food cost variance reports and theoretical vs actual COGS analysis are run at the unit level. Franchise vs corporate cost benchmarking and regional ingredient cost tracking require item_cost→unit. No

attributes.name: actual_cogs_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Actual Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Actual CoGS%)
attributes.description: Actual realized Cost of Goods Sold percentage sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management, reflecting real purchase costs, waste, and yield losses. Compared against theoretical_cogs_pct to compute variance and identify operational inefficiencies.

tag_set.value: Actual Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Actual CoGS%)

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this item cost record was approved by the authorized reviewer. Used for audit trail and to establish the effective start of this cost record in menu engineering and P&L reporting.

attributes.name: base_selling_price
attributes.description: The menu item's base selling price at the time of cost calculation, used as the denominator for CoGS% computation. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to ensure historical cost percentage accuracy even when prices change.

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: The sales channel for which this cost record applies: dine-in, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), or all channels. Packaging and portion costs may differ by channel, resulting in channel-specific cost records.

attributes.name: cogs_pct_variance
attributes.business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percentage Variance (CoGS% Variance)
attributes.description: Difference between actual_cogs_pct and theoretical_cogs_pct (actual minus theoretical). Positive variance indicates actual costs exceeded theoretical expectations, signaling waste, theft, or pricing issues. Critical metric for P&L reporting and operational control.

tag_set.value: Cost of Goods Sold Percentage Variance (CoGS% Variance)

attributes.name: cost_calculation_date
attributes.description: The business date on which the theoretical item cost was calculated. Used to track cost trends over time and align with menu engineering review cycles and P&L reporting periods.

attributes.name: cost_calculation_method
attributes.description: Method used to derive the item cost: 'theoretical_bom' = derived from recipe Bill of Materials and price snapshot; 'actual_marktman' = sourced directly from MarketMan actual cost data; 'blended' = weighted combination of theoretical and actual. Ensures analytical transparency.

attributes.name: cost_per_gram
attributes.description: Theoretical cost per gram of the menu item (theoretical_cost_amount / portion_size_grams). Enables normalized cost comparison across items of different portion sizes and supports portion engineering decisions.

attributes.name: cost_status
attributes.description: Lifecycle status of this item cost record. 'draft' indicates pending review; 'approved' indicates validated and active for menu engineering; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer calculation; 'archived' indicates retired from active use.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this item cost record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit creation trail for the Silver layer record.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cost amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The daypart for which this cost record applies (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, or all). Ingredient compositions and portion standards may vary by daypart, resulting in daypart-specific cost records for the same menu item.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this item cost record ceases to be active, typically when superseded by a new calculation. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports bi-temporal cost history and trend analysis.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this item cost record is considered active and applicable for menu engineering, pricing, and P&L reporting. Supports bi-temporal modeling of cost history.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal accounting period (e.g., '2024-P04') to which this cost record is aligned for P&L reporting. Follows the company's internal fiscal calendar as managed in SAP S/4HANA. Enables period-over-period cost trend analysis.

attributes.name: ingredient_count
attributes.description: Total number of distinct ingredients in the Bill of Materials (BOM) used for this cost calculation. Provides a quick indicator of recipe complexity and the number of price inputs contributing to the theoretical cost.

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether this cost record applies to a Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item. LTO items often have unique cost structures due to specialty ingredients and require separate cost tracking for promotional P&L analysis.

attributes.name: marktman_cost_record_code
attributes.description: The native record identifier from MarketMan Inventory Management for the actual cost data used in this calculation. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit and reconciliation purposes.

attributes.name: menu_engineering_class
attributes.description: Menu engineering quadrant classification based on the item's profitability (CoGS%) and popularity (PMIX). 'star' = high popularity, high margin; 'plow_horse' = high popularity, low margin; 'puzzle' = low popularity, high margin; 'dog' = low popularity, low margin. Drives pricing and promotional strategy.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes from the menu engineering or finance team explaining the rationale for this cost calculation, any manual adjustments made, or context for significant variances. Supports audit review and knowledge transfer.

attributes.name: packaging_cost
attributes.description: Cost contribution of packaging materials (containers, wrappers, bags, cups) to the total theoretical item cost. Tracked separately from food ingredients to support packaging procurement decisions and sustainability reporting.

attributes.name: portion_size_grams
attributes.description: Standard portion size in grams for the menu item as defined in the recipe BOM at the time of cost calculation. Used to normalize cost-per-gram comparisons across menu items and to validate portion compliance with HACCP standards.

attributes.name: price_basis
attributes.description: The pricing basis applied to ingredient costs in this calculation: 'last_purchase' = most recent purchase price; 'moving_average' = weighted average of recent purchases; 'contract' = contracted supplier price from Coupa; 'standard' = standard cost set by finance. Affects cost comparability.

attributes.name: price_snapshot_date
attributes.description: The effective date of the ingredient purchase prices used in this cost calculation. Distinct from cost_calculation_date — the snapshot may use prices from a prior period (e.g., last purchase price, rolling average). Critical for cost audit and variance explanation.

attributes.name: price_snapshot_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the ingredient price snapshot used in this cost calculation. Captures the specific point-in-time purchase prices from MarketMan that were applied to the Bill of Materials to derive theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).

attributes.name: primary_protein_cost
attributes.description: Cost contribution of the primary protein ingredient(s) to the total theoretical item cost. Proteins (beef, chicken, seafood) are typically the highest-cost BOM component and are tracked separately for commodity price sensitivity analysis and USDA-aligned procurement reporting.

attributes.name: recipe_version
attributes.description: Version identifier of the recipe / Bill of Materials (BOM) used in this cost calculation (e.g., 'v3.2'). Enables traceability of cost changes driven by recipe reformulations versus ingredient price movements.

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: The restaurant format for which this cost record applies: Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Cost structures differ by format due to labor, portion, and ingredient standards.

attributes.name: target_cogs_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Target Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Target CoGS%)
attributes.description: The budgeted or target CoGS% for this menu item as set by the finance and menu engineering teams. Used as the benchmark against which theoretical_cogs_pct and actual_cogs_pct are evaluated. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2800 = 28.00%).

tag_set.value: Target Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Target CoGS%)

attributes.name: target_variance_pct
attributes.description: Acceptable tolerance band for CoGS% variance (theoretical vs actual) before an alert or review is triggered. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = ±2.00%). Supports automated exception reporting in menu engineering workflows.

attributes.name: theoretical_cogs_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%)
attributes.description: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold expressed as a percentage of the menu item's base selling price (theoretical_cost_amount / base_price). Key menu engineering metric used to classify items and set pricing strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2850 = 28.50%).

tag_set.value: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%)

attributes.name: theoretical_cost_amount
attributes.business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount
attributes.description: The theoretical food cost amount in the operating currency for one unit of the menu item, derived by multiplying each Bill of Materials (BOM) ingredient quantity by its point-in-time purchase price. Core input to menu engineering and pricing decisions.

tag_set.value: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount

attributes.name: theoretical_cost_variance_amount
attributes.description: Absolute monetary variance between actual cost and theoretical cost per unit (actual cost amount minus theoretical_cost_amount). Expressed in the operating currency. Used alongside cogs_pct_variance for financial impact quantification in P&L reporting.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this item cost record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent update for change management and audit trail purposes in the Databricks Silver layer.

attributes.name: waste_pct
attributes.description: Theoretical food waste percentage factored into the cost calculation, representing expected yield loss during preparation (e.g., trim waste, cooking shrinkage). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%). Sourced from recipe yield standards in MarketMan.

attributes.name: yield_pct
attributes.description: Expected usable yield percentage of the primary ingredient after preparation (e.g., 0.8500 = 85% yield after trimming). Used in yield management calculations to adjust raw ingredient quantities in the BOM to achieve the target portion size.

products.name: combo_meal
products.description: Master record for bundled combo meal offerings — pre-defined combinations of menu items sold together at a bundle price (e.g., Value Meal, Family Pack, Kids Meal). Captures combo name, component items, bundle price, individual item price sum, bundle discount amount, applicable channels, daypart availability, and active status. Distinct from LTOs in that combos are permanent or semi-permanent menu fixtures.
products.reference: Combo Meal / Value Meal / Bundle Offer

attributes.name: combo_meal_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the combo meal record. Primary key for the combo_meal data product in the menu domain.

attributes.name: approved_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Combo meal launch approval is a formal marketing and culinary governance step in restaurant operations. The approving employee must be traceable for menu change management, franchise compliance, and L

attributes.name: menu_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Combo meals are defined within a specific menu; adding menu_id ties the combo to its parent menu.

attributes.name: primary_menu_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu_item. Business justification: Every combo meal has a primary or 'hero' menu item (e.g., the burger in a Burger Combo, the chicken sandwich in a Chicken Combo). This FK identifies the featured/primary menu item that defines the com

attributes.name: allergen_flags
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of major allergens present in the combo meal based on its default component items (e.g., 'milk,wheat,soy'). Supports FDA major food allergen labeling requirements and guest advisory communications. Detailed per-allergen declarations are managed in the allergen_declaration product.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date on which the combo meal was formally approved for publication by the designated menu authority. Supports audit trail and menu governance compliance.

attributes.name: bundle_discount_amount
attributes.description: The monetary discount provided to the guest by purchasing the combo meal versus buying each component item individually (individual_items_price_sum minus bundle_price). Key metric for menu engineering and promotional value analysis.

attributes.name: bundle_price
attributes.description: The retail selling price of the combo meal as a bundle, charged to the guest at POS or digital ordering channels. This is the price the guest pays for the full combo, distinct from the sum of individual item prices.

attributes.name: calorie_range_max
attributes.description: Maximum calorie count for the combo meal when customizable components are at their highest-calorie configuration. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance when combos offer substitution options.

attributes.name: calorie_range_min
attributes.description: Minimum calorie count for the combo meal when customizable components are at their lowest-calorie configuration. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance when combos offer substitution options.

attributes.name: combo_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the combo meal, used in Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and 3PD integrations for menu lookup and order capture. Serves as the canonical cross-system reference code.

attributes.name: combo_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the combo meal contents and value proposition, used for digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) channel listings. Supports guest decision-making and marketing copy.

attributes.name: combo_name
attributes.description: Human-readable marketing name of the combo meal as displayed on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering channels (e.g., 'Classic Value Meal', 'Family Pack', 'Kids Meal'). Used for guest-facing display and reporting.

attributes.name: combo_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the combo meal offering. Controls whether the combo is available for sale across channels. 'test' indicates a market-test combo not yet in full rollout.

attributes.name: combo_type
attributes.description: Classification of the combo meal by bundle format. Drives menu engineering analysis, PMIX reporting, and promotional targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: value_meal|family_pack|kids_meal|party_pack|snack_combo|premium_combo — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this combo meal is offered. Supports global menu management, regulatory compliance, and multi-currency pricing.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the combo meal record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage in the Silver layer.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this combo meal record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise markets.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Daypart(s) during which this combo meal is available for sale. Daypart segmentation is a core foodservice operational concept used in PMIX analysis, labor scheduling, and revenue management.

attributes.name: discontinue_date
attributes.description: The date on which the combo meal was or is scheduled to be removed from the menu. Nullable for permanent menu fixtures. Used for menu lifecycle management and supply chain wind-down planning.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the current version of the combo meal record ceases to be operationally effective. Nullable for open-ended records. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which the current version of the combo meal record (including pricing, components, and availability) is operationally effective. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.

attributes.name: food_cost_pct
attributes.description: Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of the bundle price (item_cost / bundle_price * 100). Core menu engineering KPI used to classify combo meals and drive pricing decisions. Aligns with CoGS% jargon in foodservice P&L management.

attributes.name: image_url
attributes.description: URL of the primary product image for the combo meal used on digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD channel listings. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.

attributes.name: individual_items_price_sum
attributes.description: The sum of the base prices of all individual component menu items if purchased separately. Used to calculate the bundle discount amount and communicate value to guests. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS item pricing.

attributes.name: is_3pd_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Controls 3PD channel menu syndication via Olo integration.

attributes.name: is_customizable
attributes.description: Indicates whether guests can substitute or modify component items within the combo meal (e.g., swap side item, upsize drink). Drives modifier group display logic in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.

attributes.name: is_dine_in_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available for dine-in service. Relevant for casual and fine-dining formats where combo availability may differ by service mode.

attributes.name: is_dt_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available for sale through the Drive-Thru (DT) channel. Controls channel-specific menu display in Oracle MICROS POS and KDS routing.

attributes.name: is_national_launch
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is part of a national rollout versus a regional or test-market offering. Drives marketing campaign scope, supply chain planning, and franchise communication.

attributes.name: is_olo_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available through the Olo Digital Ordering Platform (OLO) for web and mobile ordering. Controls digital menu availability.

attributes.name: is_taxable
attributes.description: Indicates whether the combo meal bundle price is subject to sales tax. Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA AR module. Tax applicability may vary by jurisdiction.

attributes.name: item_cost
attributes.description: Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the combo meal, representing the combined food cost of all component items. Used to calculate COGS% and support P&L and menu engineering decisions. Sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management and SAP S/4HANA CO module.

attributes.name: launch_date
attributes.description: The date on which the combo meal became or is scheduled to become available for sale. Used for menu lifecycle management, supply chain lead time planning, and marketing campaign alignment.

attributes.name: menu_engineering_class
attributes.description: Menu engineering quadrant classification for the combo meal based on profitability and popularity: 'star' (high profit, high popularity), 'plow_horse' (low profit, high popularity), 'puzzle' (high profit, low popularity), 'dog' (low profit, low popularity). Core menu engineering decision-support field.

attributes.name: olo_item_code
attributes.description: Olo Digital Ordering Platform external identifier for this combo meal. Used for menu synchronization between the internal menu master and the Olo platform for web, mobile, and 3PD channel ordering.

attributes.name: ownership_model
attributes.description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available at franchise-operated locations, company-owned locations, or both. Supports franchise compliance tracking via FranConnect and royalty reporting.

attributes.name: pmix_target_pct
attributes.description: Target Product Mix (PMIX) percentage representing the expected share of total transactions this combo meal should represent within its category. Used for menu performance benchmarking and sales forecasting.

attributes.name: pos_item_code
attributes.description: Oracle MICROS POS-specific item code used to ring up the combo meal at the point of sale. This is the PLU/button code used by FOH (Front of House) staff and KDS (Kitchen Display System) routing.

attributes.name: region_code
attributes.description: Geographic region code where this combo meal is available (e.g., 'NORTHEAST', 'WEST', 'SOUTH'). Supports regional menu variation, pricing strategy, and PMIX analysis across the restaurant network.

attributes.name: restaurant_format
attributes.description: Restaurant format(s) for which this combo meal is applicable. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) combos differ structurally from casual or fine-dining bundles. Drives menu engineering and franchise compliance.

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: Display sequence order for the combo meal on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Controls guest-facing menu presentation.

attributes.name: tax_category_code
attributes.description: Tax category code assigned to the combo meal for sales tax calculation in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA. Determines applicable tax rates by jurisdiction and product type.

attributes.name: total_calories
attributes.description: Total calorie count for the combo meal as configured with default component items. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance on menus with 20+ locations. Displayed on menu boards and digital ordering platforms.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the combo meal record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver layer.

products.name: item_assignment
products.description: This association product represents the Assignment (Menu Line) between menu and menu_item. It captures the operational record of which menu items appear on which menus, under what display conditions, and for what effective date range. Each record links one menu to one menu_item and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this specific assignment — sort order, featured flag, channel display name, category override, and effective dates. Menu engineers actively create, update, and retire these records as part of the menu publishing and versioning workflow.. Existence Justification: In restaurant operations, menu items are actively assigned to multiple menus simultaneously — a Cheeseburger appears on the Drive-Thru menu, the OLO menu, the dine-in menu, and the 3PD menu at the same time. Conversely, each menu contains many items. Menu engineers explicitly manage these assignments, controlling sort order, featured placement, channel-specific display names, and effective date windows for each item-on-menu combination. This is a first-class operational business concept called 'menu item assignment' or 'menu line' that humans create, update, and delete as part of the menu publishing workflow.

attributes.name: item_assignment_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Menu Item Assignment - Menu Item Assignment Id
attributes.description: Primary key for the menu_item_assignment association

tag_set.value: Menu Item Assignment - Menu Item Assignment Id

attributes.name: menu_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking this assignment record to the parent menu that contains the item.

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Menu Item Assignment - Menu Item Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking this assignment record to the menu item being placed on the menu.

tag_set.value: Menu Item Assignment - Menu Item Id

attributes.name: category_override
attributes.description: An optional menu-specific category label that overrides the menu item's default category classification for display purposes on this menu. For example, a 'Crispy Chicken Sandwich' may appear under 'Sandwiches' on the dine-in menu but under 'Chicken' on the drive-thru menu. Belongs to the assignment because it is context-specific.

attributes.name: channel_display_name
attributes.description: The channel-specific display name for this menu item as it appears on this particular menu's channel (e.g., a shorter name for drive-thru boards vs. a longer name for OLO). Overrides the item's default item_name for this specific menu context. Belongs to the assignment, not the item master.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which this menu item assignment expires and the item is removed from this menu. Null indicates the assignment is open-ended. Enables LTO and seasonal item management at the assignment level without modifying the item master record.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which this menu item assignment becomes active and the item begins appearing on this menu. Enables time-bounded menu engineering — an item can be pre-assigned to a menu before its launch date. Belongs to the assignment, not the item or the menu.

attributes.name: is_featured
attributes.description: Indicates whether this menu item is highlighted or promoted on this specific menu (e.g., shown in a featured section, callout badge, or hero image slot). A single item may be featured on one menu but not another, so this flag belongs to the assignment relationship.

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: The display sequence position of this menu item within its category on this specific menu. Controls the order in which items appear on digital menu boards, OLO, and 3PD channels. Belongs to the assignment, not the item or the menu, because the same item may appear in different positions on different menus.

domains.name: guest
domains.description: Single source of truth for customer identity, profiles, preferences, demographics, segments, loyalty membership, and guest engagement across all channels (dine-in, drive-thru, online ordering). Manages CSAT, NPS, lifetime value, and consent/privacy management. Master record for WHO the business serves.

products.name: profile
products.description: Master record for every guest identity across all service channels (dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, 3PD). Single source of truth for WHO the business serves — captures full identity and demographic attributes including name, contact details, date of birth, language preference, age band, gender identity, household income band, education level, employment status, geographic market classification, demographic data source (self-declared vs. third-party enrichment), enrichment provider and date. Also owns digital account attributes: username, account status (active/suspended/deactivated), registration date, registration channel, last login timestamp, device type, app version, two-factor authentication status, and account tier. Sourced primarily from Salesforce CRM, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and brand mobile app. This is the anchor entity for the entire guest domain.

products.name: preference
products.description: Captures all guest-stated and inferred preferences, dietary restrictions, and food allergen declarations. Covers FDA major allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame) with severity levels (intolerance vs. allergy), dietary restriction types (vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free), declaration source (self-declared, healthcare provider), cuisine preferences, favorite menu items, preferred service channel (dine-in, DT, OLO), preferred daypart, communication channel preferences (email, SMS, push), and marketing opt-in/opt-out flags. Sourced from Salesforce CRM, Olo guest data, and guest self-declaration. Drives personalization, targeted marketing, and HACCP-aligned guest food safety compliance including FDA allergen labeling requirements.

products.name: satisfaction_survey
products.description: Records guest satisfaction survey instances with full question-level response detail. Captures survey type (CSAT, NPS, post-delivery, post-visit), delivery channel (email, SMS, in-app, receipt QR), delivery timestamp, completion status, NPS score (0-10), CSAT score, restaurant unit, daypart, and respondent profile. Includes granular question-level data: question text, question type (rating, open-text, multiple-choice), response value, response timestamp, and sentiment classification for open-text responses. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Olo guest feedback flows. Enables CSAT/NPS driver analysis at both survey and question level, supporting operational improvement across FOH and BOH.

products.name: complaint
products.description: Operational record of a guest complaint or service recovery case raised through any channel (in-restaurant, phone, digital, social media). Captures complaint category (food quality, speed of service/SOS, order accuracy, cleanliness, staff behavior), severity level, channel of receipt, restaurant unit, associated order reference, resolution status, resolution type (refund, replacement, apology), resolution timestamp, and escalation flag. Managed in Salesforce CRM service cloud.

attributes.name: complaint_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the complaint record.

attributes.name: brand_id

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Complaint volume and resolution time by channel (drive-thru, delivery app, phone, in-store) is a core restaurant operations report. The plain text 'channel' column on complaint is a denormalization of

attributes.name: ingredient_lot_id

attributes.name: shift_id

attributes.name: stock_item_id

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supplier accountability tracking: when a guest complaint is traced to a specific supplier's ingredient (contamination, foreign object, spoilage), restaurant operations and regulatory reporting require

attributes.name: visit_id

attributes.name: category

attributes.description: Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the complaint (e.g., C‑20231234).

attributes.name: complaint_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the complaint.

attributes.name: complaint_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the complaint was initially recorded.

attributes.name: consent_given
attributes.description: Indicates whether the guest consented to store and process their personal data for this complaint.

attributes.name: csat_score

products.name: interaction
products.description: Unified event stream capturing every recorded touchpoint between the brand and a guest across all channels. Covers inbound interactions (app sessions, loyalty check-ins, drive-thru visits, dine-in visits, OLO sessions, 3PD orders) and outbound communications (marketing emails, SMS messages, push notifications, direct mail). Captures interaction type, direction (inbound/outbound), channel, timestamp, restaurant unit (if applicable), campaign or trigger reference, subject/content reference, delivery status, open status, click-through status, unsubscribe action, and interaction outcome. Sourced from Salesforce CRM marketing automation, Oracle MICROS POS, and Olo. Provides the raw engagement timeline for RFM modeling, communication frequency capping, suppression list management, and guest engagement scoring.

attributes.name: interaction_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the interaction event.

attributes.name: brand_id

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Guest interaction channel (phone, web chat, in-app, in-store) must reference the canonical channel registry for omnichannel CRM reporting and contact center analytics. The plain text 'channel' column

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: In‑Restaurant Interaction Log tracks the employee handling each guest interaction, required for training effectiveness and KPI reporting.

attributes.name: guest_order_id

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.pos_terminal. Business justification: Kiosk/POS terminal-level interaction tracking enables channel analytics (self-service vs. cashier), PCI audit trails, and terminal throughput reporting. A restaurant IT/operations expert expects guest

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the interaction record was first created in the lakehouse.

attributes.name: device_code
attributes.description: Identifier of the device or terminal that recorded the interaction (e.g., POS terminal, kiosk).

products.name: demographic
products.description: Stores structured demographic attributes for a guest profile including age band, gender identity, ethnicity (where permitted and consented), household income band, education level, employment status, and geographic market classification. Captures data source (self-declared vs. third-party enrichment), enrichment provider, and enrichment date. Supports targeted marketing, menu development, and site selection analytics in conjunction with the realestate domain.

attributes.name: demographic_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the demographic record.

attributes.name: demographic_guest_profile_id

attributes.name: ethnicity

attributes.name: ethnicity_source
attributes.description: Origin of the ethnicity information.

attributes.name: full_name
attributes.description: Legal full name of the individual.

attributes.name: gender_identity
