attributes.name: geographic_market
attributes.description: Market classification based on the guest's primary location.

attributes.name: household_income_band

attributes.name: language_preference
attributes.description: Preferred language for communications.

attributes.name: marital_status
attributes.description: Current marital status of the guest.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free‑form comments or observations about the demographic record.

products.name: visit
products.description: Captures each confirmed guest visit to a restaurant unit across all service modes (dine-in, DT, OLO, 3PD). Distinct from the order domain's order transaction — this is the guest-centric visit record that may span multiple orders or be a zero-spend visit (e.g., complaint resolution visit). Captures visit date, daypart, service mode, restaurant unit, party size (cover count), table number (dine-in), DT lane (drive-thru), visit duration, and whether the visit was incentivized by a promotion. [SSOT: authoritative owner is loyalty.loyalty_visit] establish single authoritative owner

attributes.name: visit_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the guest_visit data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: Visit-level channel analytics (dine-in vs. drive-thru vs. delivery vs. app) require a normalized channel reference. visit_channel is a plain text denormalization of order.channel. Channel-level visit

attributes.name: host_employee_id

attributes.name: member_id

attributes.name: menu_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Menu engineering and LTO performance analysis require knowing which menu version (daypart, channel, LTO) was active during a guest visit. Restaurant analysts use this to correlate menu changes with vi

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.pos_terminal. Business justification: POS terminal-level visit/transaction reconciliation is a named operational process: each visit's check is tendered on a specific terminal, enabling terminal throughput analysis, cash reconciliation, a

attributes.name: profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: guest_visit must reference the guest profile to associate each visit with a guest.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Labor efficiency reporting: linking guest visits to the shift during which they occurred enables core restaurant KPIs — covers per shift, revenue per labor hour, and guest throughput per shift. Restau

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Visit analytics require linking each guest visit to the exact restaurant unit for performance dashboards and service‑speed KPI calculations.

attributes.name: duration_minutes

attributes.description: Total spend amount

attributes.name: visit_time

products.name: digital_account
products.description: Represents a guest's registered digital account on the brand's owned digital channels (mobile app, website, OLO platform). Captures account username, account status (active, suspended, deactivated), registration date, registration channel, last login timestamp, device type, app version, two-factor authentication status, and account tier. Distinct from the guest profile (identity) — this is the digital access credential and session management record sourced from Olo Digital Ordering Platform.
products.reference: Digital Account / Online Account / App Account

attributes.name: digital_account_id
attributes.description: System-generated unique identifier for the digital account record.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: In multi-brand restaurant enterprises, a digital account belongs to a specific brand's app/platform (e.g., Brand A loyalty app vs. Brand B app). Brand-level digital account management, app analytics,

attributes.name: member_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Required for app login to map to loyalty member for point accrual and redemption; the restaurant's loyalty program uses digital accounts to identify members during orders.

attributes.name: profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: digital_account must be linked to the guest profile to associate digital activity with the correct guest.

attributes.name: account_number
attributes.description: External account number assigned to the digital account, used for customer service and reporting.

attributes.name: account_tier
attributes.description: Membership tier that determines benefits and loyalty program eligibility.

attributes.name: app_version
attributes.description: Software version of the mobile or web app used during the last login.

attributes.name: consent_marketing
attributes.description: Guest's consent to receive marketing communications.

attributes.name: consent_third_party

attributes.name: created_timestamp

attributes.name: device_type

attributes.name: digital_account_status

attributes.name: effective_until

attributes.name: email

attributes.name: lockout_timestamp

attributes.name: phone_number

attributes.name: username

domains.name: order
domains.description: Core transactional domain managing order capture, fulfillment, and delivery across all service channels including POS (Oracle MICROS), drive-thru (DT), online ordering (OLO), third-party delivery (3PD), and catering. Tracks order lifecycle, KDS routing, ticket time, speed of service (SOS), average transaction count (ATC), and average check value (ACV).

products.name: guest_order
products.description: Core transactional header record for every guest order placed across all service channels (POS/MICROS, drive-thru, OLO, 3PD, catering, kiosk). Captures order-level attributes: ticket number, order type, service channel, daypart, order status, guest count, total amount, tax total, discount total, timestamps (placed, promised, completed), and source system reference. This is the authoritative SSOT for all order transactions — every order_item, order_payment, order_status_event, and kds_ticket FKs back to this record.
products.reference: Oracle MICROS POS Order Header / Olo Order

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each guest order record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the guest_order data product.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_channel. Business justification: guest_order currently stores channel as a string; linking to order_channel lookup provides referential integrity and removes redundant column.

attributes.name: loyalty_member_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Needed for loyalty points accrual and member order history report linking each order to the member who placed it.

attributes.name: loyalty_program_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the loyalty program associated with this order, if the guest is enrolled. Used to link loyalty point accrual and redemption events to the order transaction.

attributes.name: menu_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Required for Menu Performance Reporting: each order must be linked to the specific menu version presented to the guest to analyze sales per menu and ensure compliance with menu engineering.

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.pos_terminal. Business justification: POS audit trail and PCI compliance reporting require linking each guest order to the specific terminal it was rung on. Terminal performance reports, shift reconciliation, and PCI audit logs all depend

attributes.name: primary_guest_profile_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the guest (customer) who placed the order. Nullable for anonymous walk-in transactions where no loyalty or digital profile is associated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header.

attributes.name: server_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Front of House (FOH) server or cashier employee who took or managed the order. Used for labor performance tracking, tip allocation, and service quality analysis.

attributes.name: server_shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Server tip allocation and IRS tip reporting (Form 8027) require linking each order to the server's specific shift. Per-shift server sales productivity reporting also depends on this. Role-prefixed 'se

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed or fulfilled. Links to the restaurant master record.

attributes.name: cancelled_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when the order was cancelled or voided. Null for orders that were fulfilled. Used for cancellation rate reporting and operational analysis.

attributes.name: created_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when this guest_order record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this transaction header. Distinct from placed_at which captures the business event time.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary amounts in this order (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Required for multi-currency international restaurant operations. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET currency component.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Operational daypart segment during which the order was placed. Dayparts are defined by the restaurant's operating schedule and used for Product Mix (PMIX) analysis, labor scheduling, and sales reporting. Standard segments: breakfast, lunch, afternoon, dinner, late_night.

attributes.name: delivery_address_line1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for delivery orders. Contains personally identifiable location information for the guest. Null for non-delivery order types.

attributes.name: delivery_postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code of the delivery address. Used for delivery zone analysis, geographic sales reporting, and logistics optimization. Contains guest location data classified as PII.

attributes.name: delivery_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party delivery (3PD) provider fulfilling the delivery order. Null or 'none' for non-delivery orders. Used for 3PD commission cost analysis and delivery performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value of all discounts, promotions, coupons, and loyalty redemptions applied to the order. Represents the adjustment component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for promotional effectiveness and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) analysis.

attributes.name: fulfilled_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when the order was handed to the guest or dispatched for delivery, marking the completion of the service transaction. Used for end-to-end Speed of Service (SOS) measurement.

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether the order contains at least one Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item. Used for LTO performance tracking, promotional sales attribution, and menu engineering analysis.

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Indicates whether the order was voided by a manager after tender. Voided orders are retained for audit and loss prevention purposes but excluded from revenue reporting. Distinct from cancelled orders (cancelled before preparation).

attributes.name: item_count
attributes.description: Total number of individual menu items (including modifiers and add-ons) included in the order. Used for throughput analysis, kitchen capacity planning, and Average Transaction Count (ATC) benchmarking.

attributes.name: kds_routed_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when the order was routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for preparation. Used to calculate kitchen queue time and Back of House (BOH) throughput metrics.

attributes.name: loyalty_points_earned
attributes.description: Number of loyalty reward points earned by the guest for this order transaction. Used for loyalty program accrual tracking and guest engagement analytics.

attributes.name: loyalty_points_redeemed
attributes.description: Number of loyalty reward points redeemed by the guest as part of this order transaction. Used for loyalty liability accounting and promotional cost analysis.

attributes.name: olo_order_ref
attributes.description: External order reference number assigned by the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for orders placed through digital channels (web, mobile app). Null for in-store POS orders.

attributes.name: order_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the guest order. Tracks the order from placement through fulfillment or cancellation. 'placed' = order received; 'in_preparation' = KDS routing active; 'ready' = food ready for pickup/delivery; 'fulfilled' = order handed to guest; 'cancelled' = cancelled before preparation; 'voided' = voided post-tender by manager.

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Fulfillment type of the order indicating how the guest intends to receive their food. Distinct from order_channel (which captures the ordering interface). Used for kitchen routing, packaging, and service time analytics.

attributes.name: party_size
attributes.description: Number of guests in the dining party associated with this order. Applicable for dine-in orders. Used for cover count reporting, table turn analysis, and per-cover revenue calculations.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current status of the payment transaction associated with this order. Distinct from order_status which tracks fulfillment lifecycle. Used for financial reconciliation and exception management.

attributes.name: placed_at
attributes.description: The principal real-world event timestamp when the guest order was placed and confirmed in the POS or digital ordering system. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this transaction header. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) calculations and daypart assignment.

attributes.name: pos_transaction_ref
attributes.description: Native transaction reference or check number generated by the Oracle MICROS POS system. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the POS system of record.

attributes.name: ready_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when the order was marked ready for pickup or delivery by kitchen staff. Used to calculate ticket time (time from order placement to food ready) and Speed of Service (SOS) metrics.

attributes.name: sos_seconds
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in seconds from order placement (placed_at) to order fulfillment (fulfilled_at). Represents the end-to-end guest experience time. A primary operational KPI for Quick-Service Restaurants (QSR) and Drive-Thru (DT) performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: special_instructions
attributes.description: Free-text field capturing guest-specific preparation instructions, allergy notes, or customization requests entered at the time of ordering. Used by kitchen staff for order fulfillment and food safety compliance (HACCP allergen awareness).

attributes.name: subtotal_amount
attributes.description: Gross pre-tax, pre-discount monetary value of all items in the order. Represents the base order value before adjustments. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction header.

attributes.name: table_number
attributes.description: Table or seat identifier assigned to the dine-in order within the restaurant floor plan. Null for takeout, drive-thru, and delivery orders. Used for table turn analysis and Front of House (FOH) service management.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total sales tax amount applied to the order based on applicable local, state, and federal tax rates. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment. Used for tax remittance reporting and financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: tender_type
attributes.description: Primary payment instrument used to settle the order. Supports payment mix analysis and PCI DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|mobile_pay|gift_card|loyalty_points|voucher|comp — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: ticket_number
attributes.description: Human-readable, externally visible order ticket number assigned by the Point of Sale (POS) system (Oracle MICROS). Printed on receipts and displayed on the Kitchen Display System (KDS). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction. Format varies by restaurant configuration (e.g., sequential daily number).

attributes.name: ticket_time_seconds
attributes.description: Elapsed time in seconds from order placement (placed_at) to order ready (ready_at). Represents the kitchen preparation time, a key Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput KPI in restaurant operations. Stored as a raw operational field captured by the POS/KDS system.

attributes.name: tip_amount
attributes.description: Gratuity amount added by the guest at the time of payment. Applicable for casual and fine-dining service models. Used for labor compensation reporting and financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: total_amount
attributes.description: Final net monetary amount charged to the guest after applying discounts and adding taxes (subtotal_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount). Represents the Average Check Value (ACV) for this transaction. Net total component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.

attributes.name: updated_at
attributes.description: Timestamp when this guest_order record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this transaction header. Used for incremental data processing and change data capture (CDC) pipelines.

attributes.name: void_reason
attributes.description: Reason code for why the order was voided. Null for non-voided orders. Used for loss prevention, operational exception reporting, and manager accountability tracking.

products.name: order_item
products.description: Line-item detail for each menu item within a guest order. Captures the ordered menu item, quantity, unit price, modifiers applied, line-level discounts, PMIX contribution, preparation instructions, KDS station routing, and item-level fulfillment status. Supports PMIX analysis, COGS% calculation, and kitchen throughput reporting. [SSOT] Aliases canonical owner menu.menu_item for concept 'item'. SSOT: authoritative source is menu.menu_item. SSOT: authoritative owner is menu.menu_item for concept 'menu_vs_order_item'. [SSOT: authoritative owner is menu.menu_item] menu_item is master; order_item references menu_item_id
products.reference: Oracle MICROS POS Order Detail / Olo Order Item

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each line item within a guest order. Primary key for the order_item entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

attributes.name: applied_item_price_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.item_price. Business justification: Price audit trail and franchise compliance reporting require knowing which item_price record (channel-specific, daypart-specific, unit-specific) was active when each order item was placed. Enables POS

attributes.name: discount_id
attributes.description: Reference to the promotional discount, coupon, or loyalty reward applied to this line item. Null when no discount is applied. Links to the marketing domain for promotional effectiveness analysis.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order (TRANSACTION_HEADER) to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header/detail relationship for order fulfillment and revenue reporting.

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: REQUIRED: Ingredient‑level sales and nutrition reporting needs each order_item linked to its ingredient for cost, menu analysis, and regulatory nutrition disclosure.

attributes.name: ingredient_lot_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient_lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Food‑safety traceability and recall reports must map each sold item to the specific ingredient lot used in its preparation.

attributes.name: kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) station to which this line item was routed for preparation. Supports kitchen throughput and ticket time analysis.

attributes.name: menu_modifier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the modifier applied to this line item (e.g., extra cheese, no onions, upsize). Nullable when no modifier is applied. Supports modifier-level PMIX and upsell analysis.

attributes.name: prep_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Labor costing & food‑safety audit need to know which employee prepared each menu item; supports per‑item labor allocation and accountability.

attributes.name: prepared_recipe_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.recipe. Business justification: Food safety traceability and HACCP compliance require linking each ordered item to the recipe version active at time of preparation. If a foodborne illness incident occurs, operators must identify whi

attributes.name: primary_order_canonical_menu_item_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Primary Order Canonical Menu Item Id
attributes.description: FK to canonical SSOT owner menu.menu_item for concept 'item'.

tag_set.value: Primary Order Canonical Menu Item Id

attributes.name: primary_order_combo_combo_meal_id
attributes.description: Reference to the combo meal or bundle to which this line item belongs. Null when is_combo_component is False. Groups combo components for bundle-level revenue and PMIX reporting.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Kitchen productivity and food cost vs. labor cost analysis require linking prep work to the shift. Shift-level waste tracking, throughput reporting, and COGS-per-shift calculations depend on this. pre

attributes.name: tertiary_order_menu_item_menu_item_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Tertiary Order Menu Item Menu Item Id
attributes.description: SSOT link to authoritative menu.menu_item for concept item

tag_set.value: Tertiary Order Menu Item Menu Item Id

attributes.name: allergen_override_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a manager or authorized staff member overrode an allergen warning for this line item at the Point of Sale (POS). True triggers an audit trail for food safety compliance and liability management.

attributes.name: calorie_count
attributes.description: Total calorie count for this line item as required by FDA menu labeling regulations. Reflects the calorie value at the ordered quantity and modifier combination. Supports regulatory compliance and guest nutrition transparency.

attributes.name: cost
attributes.description: Standard cost of goods for this line item at the time of the transaction, sourced from the recipe/Bill of Materials (BOM). Used to calculate Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (COGS%) and gross margin at the item level.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this order item record was first captured in the source system (Oracle MICROS POS or Olo). Represents the moment the line item was added to the order. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Silver Layer.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this line item (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global restaurant markets.

attributes.name: daypart_code
attributes.description: Operational daypart during which this line item was ordered (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Supports daypart-level PMIX analysis, labor scheduling alignment, and revenue management by time segment.

attributes.name: is_combo_component
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is a component of a combo meal or bundle (True) or a standalone item (False). Supports combo meal PMIX analysis and bundle pricing validation.

attributes.name: is_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ordered menu item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO) at the time of the transaction. Supports LTO performance tracking, PMIX contribution analysis, and promotional campaign reporting.

attributes.name: item_status
attributes.description: Current fulfillment lifecycle status of this line item as tracked through the Kitchen Display System (KDS) and Point of Sale (POS). Drives kitchen throughput reporting and Speed of Service (SOS) measurement.

attributes.name: line_discount_amount
attributes.description: Total discount applied at the line-item level, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, loyalty rewards, and employee meal discounts. Supports promotional effectiveness and margin analysis.

attributes.name: line_gross_amount
attributes.description: Gross extended amount for this line item before discounts and taxes, calculated as quantity × (unit_price + modifier_price). Used as the basis for COGS% and revenue reporting.

attributes.name: line_net_amount
attributes.description: Net revenue amount for this line item after all line-level discounts, before tax. Equals line_gross_amount minus line_discount_amount. Core metric for Average Check Value (ACV) and P&L reporting.

attributes.name: line_sequence
attributes.description: Sequential position of this line item within the parent order, starting at 1. Used to preserve the order of items as entered at the Point of Sale (POS) and for receipt printing.

attributes.name: loyalty_points_earned
attributes.description: Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this line item, based on the loyalty program rules in effect at the time of the transaction. Supports loyalty program accrual tracking and CRM analytics.

attributes.name: modifier_price
attributes.description: Incremental price charged for the applied modifier (e.g., upsize surcharge, premium add-on). Zero or null when no modifier price applies. Contributes to line-level revenue and Average Check Value (ACV) analysis.

attributes.name: pmix_category
attributes.description: Menu engineering classification of this item within the Product Mix (PMIX) framework (e.g., Star, Plow Horse, Puzzle, Dog per the BCG matrix). Supports menu engineering decisions and item lifecycle management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: star|plow_horse|puzzle|dog — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: preparation_instructions
attributes.description: Free-text special preparation instructions entered by the guest or cashier for this line item (e.g., 'well done', 'no salt', 'extra crispy'). Transmitted to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for Back of House (BOH) execution.

attributes.name: promo_code
attributes.description: Alphanumeric promotional or coupon code entered at the Point of Sale (POS) or Online Ordering (OLO) platform that triggered the line-level discount. Supports campaign attribution and redemption tracking.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: Number of units of the menu item ordered on this line. Supports decimal precision for weight-based or fractional items (e.g., bulk catering). Used in PMIX analysis and COGS% calculation.

attributes.name: refund_amount
attributes.description: Monetary amount refunded for this line item when refund_flag is True. May be a partial or full refund of line_net_amount. Used for financial reconciliation and guest recovery reporting.

attributes.name: refund_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item has been refunded (True). Supports refund rate tracking, guest satisfaction (CSAT) analysis, and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.

attributes.name: service_channel
attributes.description: The service channel through which this line item was ordered: dine-in, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), catering, or self-service kiosk. Supports channel-level PMIX and revenue analysis.

attributes.name: source_system_item_ref
attributes.description: Native item identifier from the originating Point of Sale (POS) or Online Ordering (OLO) system (e.g., Oracle MICROS detail line ID, Olo order item UUID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Tax amount assessed on this line item based on applicable jurisdiction tax rules and menu item tax category. Supports tax remittance reporting and compliance with local tax authorities.

attributes.name: tax_exempt_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is exempt from sales tax (True), such as for qualifying food items under state/local tax law or institutional catering orders. Supports tax compliance and remittance reporting.

attributes.name: ticket_time_seconds
attributes.description: Elapsed time in seconds from kds_sent_timestamp to kds_bump_timestamp, representing the preparation time for this line item. Key Speed of Service (SOS) metric for kitchen throughput and operational performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: Selling price per single unit of the menu item at the time of order, before modifiers, discounts, or taxes. Reflects the menu price in effect during the transaction for historical accuracy.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this order item record, such as a quantity change, void, or status update. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit trail requirements in the Silver Layer.

attributes.name: void_reason_code
attributes.description: Coded reason for voiding this line item (e.g., GUEST_CHANGE, PREP_ERROR, OUT_OF_STOCK, MANAGER_COMP). Null when item is not voided. Supports waste tracking and operational exception reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GUEST_CHANGE|PREP_ERROR|OUT_OF_STOCK|MANAGER_COMP|ALLERGY|DUPLICATE — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: waste_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item was recorded as food waste (True) rather than served to a guest. Supports Food Waste Percentage (Waste%) tracking, inventory reconciliation, and sustainability reporting.

attributes.name: waste_reason_code
attributes.description: Coded reason for food waste on this line item when waste_flag is True. Supports root-cause analysis for Waste% reduction initiatives and HACCP compliance reporting.

products.name: order_modifier
products.description: Records all customizations and modifiers applied to individual order items, including add-ons, substitutions, removals, and special preparation requests. Captures modifier name, modifier group, price delta, and whether the modifier was guest-initiated or system-defaulted. Essential for accurate COGS% tracking and kitchen execution fidelity. [SSOT] Aliases canonical owner menu.menu_modifier for concept 'modifier'. SSOT: authoritative source is menu.menu_modifier. SSOT: authoritative owner is menu.menu_modifier for concept 'modifier'. [SSOT: authoritative owner is menu.menu_modifier] menu_modifier is master; order_modifier references

attributes.name: order_modifier_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each order modifier record applied to an order line item. Primary key for the order_modifier data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

attributes.name: applied_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance audit requires recording which employee applied a modifier (e.g., discount, special request) to ensure authorization and traceability.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent order transaction header. Enables direct order-level aggregation and reporting without requiring a join through the order item. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS transaction records.

attributes.name: kds_ticket_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.kds_ticket. Business justification: order_modifier has kds_routed (BOOLEAN), kds_station_name (STRING), and kds_acknowledged_timestamp — indicating modifiers are routed to KDS tickets. Adding kds_ticket_id FK normalizes the KDS routing

attributes.name: modifier_group_id
attributes.description: Reference to the modifier group to which this modifier belongs (e.g., 'Protein Options', 'Sauce Selection', 'Temperature'). Supports modifier group-level reporting and menu engineering analysis. Links to the menu domain modifier_group catalog.

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent order line item to which this modifier is applied. Establishes the header-detail relationship between the order item and its customizations. Aligns with Oracle MICROS POS order item detail records.

attributes.name: primary_order_menu_modifier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the menu modifier master record defining the base modifier configuration, pricing rules, and preparation instructions. Links to the menu domain modifier catalog.

attributes.name: quaternary_order_menu_modifier_menu_modifier_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Quaternary Order Menu Modifier Menu Modifier Id
attributes.description: SSOT link to authoritative menu.menu_modifier for concept modifier

tag_set.value: Quaternary Order Menu Modifier Menu Modifier Id

attributes.name: source_modifier_menu_modifier_id
attributes.description: The native modifier transaction identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS modifier line ID, Olo modifier ID). Enables exact record traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the system of record.

attributes.name: tertiary_order_menu_modifier_id
attributes.description: FK redirect to authoritative SSOT owner menu.menu_modifier.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where this modifier was applied. Enables location-level modifier adoption analysis, Product Mix (PMIX) reporting, and kitchen execution fidelity tracking.

attributes.name: allergen_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier introduces or removes an allergen from the order item (True). Triggers allergen advisory workflows and supports FDA food labeling compliance and HACCP allergen control point tracking.

attributes.name: allergen_notes
attributes.description: Descriptive notes identifying the specific allergen(s) introduced or removed by this modifier (e.g., 'Contains: Tree Nuts', 'Removes: Gluten'). Supports FDA FALCPA compliance, HACCP Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points documentation, and guest safety communication.

attributes.name: applied_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this modifier was applied to the order item at the Point of Sale (POS) or digital ordering channel. Principal business event timestamp for Speed of Service (SOS) and Ticket Time analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: calorie_delta
attributes.description: The incremental calorie change (positive or negative) contributed by this modifier to the parent order item. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance (Section 4205 of the ACA) and supports guest nutritional transparency.

attributes.name: cogs_delta
attributes.business_glossary_term: Modifier Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Delta
attributes.description: The incremental raw food cost impact of this modifier on the order item, expressed in the local currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) calculation, menu engineering, and P&L reporting. Sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management BOM costing.

tag_set.value: Modifier Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Delta

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this order modifier record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail timestamp for data lineage and ETL reconciliation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the price delta of this modifier (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise and company-owned restaurant locations.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The meal period or daypart during which this modifier was applied (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Supports daypart-level PMIX analysis, modifier adoption trends, and targeted menu engineering decisions.

attributes.name: group_name
attributes.description: Name of the modifier group as displayed at the time of order capture (e.g., 'Add-Ons', 'Sauce', 'Cooking Preference'). Snapshot value for historical reporting and PMIX analysis independent of menu restructuring.

attributes.name: initiation_source
attributes.description: Indicates whether the modifier was explicitly requested by the guest, automatically applied as a system default (e.g., standard condiment), suggested by crew, or triggered by a loyalty or promotional rule. Critical for distinguishing guest-driven customization from operational defaults in PMIX and CoGS% analysis.

attributes.name: is_comped
attributes.description: Indicates whether the price delta for this modifier was comped (complimentary, zero-charged) as part of a guest recovery, promotional offer, or manager override. Supports comp tracking for P&L and guest satisfaction (CSAT) analysis.

attributes.name: is_default
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier was pre-selected as the system default configuration for the menu item (True) or was explicitly chosen/changed by the guest or crew (False). Used to distinguish standard preparation from customization in PMIX and kitchen execution analysis.

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier was voided after initial capture (True). Supports void analysis, kitchen error tracking, and accurate financial reconciliation. Distinct from modifier_status to enable fast boolean filtering in analytics.

attributes.name: kds_acknowledged_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the kitchen crew acknowledged this modifier on the Kitchen Display System (KDS). Used to calculate kitchen execution latency and Ticket Time for Speed of Service (SOS) reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: kds_routed
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier was successfully routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for preparation execution (True). Supports kitchen execution fidelity tracking and Speed of Service (SOS) analysis.

attributes.name: loyalty_redemption_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier was applied as part of a loyalty program redemption (True), such as a free add-on reward. Supports loyalty program ROI analysis and modifier-level redemption tracking.

attributes.name: lto_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this modifier is associated with a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion (True). Supports LTO performance tracking, promotional PMIX analysis, and campaign effectiveness measurement.

attributes.name: modifier_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the modifier as displayed on the Point of Sale (POS) screen, guest receipt, and Kitchen Display System (KDS) at the time the order was placed. Captured as a snapshot to preserve historical accuracy independent of menu changes.

attributes.name: modifier_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the modifier on the order item. 'applied' indicates the modifier was successfully executed; 'voided' indicates it was removed post-capture; 'pending' indicates awaiting kitchen confirmation; 'rejected' indicates the modifier could not be fulfilled.

attributes.name: modifier_type
attributes.description: Categorical classification of the modifier action applied to the order item. Drives Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) impact analysis and kitchen execution routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: add_on|substitution|removal|upsize|downsize|special_request — promote to reference product if additional types are required]

attributes.name: olo_modifier_code
attributes.description: The modifier code assigned by the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for online and third-party delivery orders. Enables cross-channel modifier reconciliation between POS and digital ordering channels.

attributes.name: order_channel
attributes.description: The service channel through which the order containing this modifier was placed. Supports channel-level modifier adoption analysis across Point of Sale (POS), Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), kiosk, and catering channels.

attributes.name: pos_modifier_code
attributes.description: The native modifier code assigned by the Oracle MICROS POS system. Used for reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the POS system of record, and for menu management synchronization.

attributes.name: prep_instruction
attributes.description: Free-text or structured preparation instruction associated with this modifier as displayed on the KDS and printed order ticket (e.g., 'Extra crispy', 'No ice', 'On the side'). Captures special preparation requests for kitchen execution fidelity and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) compliance.

attributes.name: price_delta
attributes.description: The incremental price adjustment applied to the order item as a result of this modifier. Positive values represent upcharges (e.g., premium add-ons); negative values represent discounts or removals that reduce item price. Essential for accurate revenue and Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) calculation.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of the modifier applied to the order item (e.g., 2 extra shots of espresso, 0.5 for half portion). Supports fractional quantities for portion-based modifiers and accurate COGS% tracking.

attributes.name: sequence_number
attributes.description: Ordinal position of this modifier within the set of modifiers applied to the parent order item. Used to preserve the display and preparation order on the Kitchen Display System (KDS) and printed receipts.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the modifier quantity (e.g., 'each' for discrete add-ons, 'oz' for liquid modifiers, 'g' for weight-based modifiers). Supports accurate Bill of Materials (BOM) costing and inventory depletion via MarketMan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|oz|ml|g|portion|pump|slice|scoop — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this order modifier record was last updated in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports change data capture (CDC) and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse pipeline. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: voided_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this modifier was voided, if applicable. Null if the modifier was not voided. Supports void rate analysis and kitchen error tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

products.name: payment
products.description: Captures all payment tender records associated with a guest order, supporting split-tender scenarios. Includes tender type (cash, credit, debit, gift card, loyalty redemption, 3PD settlement), amount tendered, change due, authorization code, PCI-compliant masked card data, payment processor reference, and payment status. Authoritative SSOT for order-level payment capture; financial settlement lives in the finance domain.
products.reference: Oracle MICROS POS Payment / PCI DSS Payment Record

attributes.name: payment_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each payment tender record associated with a guest order. Serves as the primary key for the order_payment data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

attributes.name: cashier_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the FOH (Front of House) employee who processed this payment transaction at the POS terminal. Used for cash accountability, tip reporting, and labor performance analysis.

attributes.name: discount_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.discount. Business justification: payment.discount_amount captures the discount applied at payment time, but there is no FK linking the payment to the specific discount record that drove that amount. Adding discount_id to payment enab

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order for which this payment tender was captured. Supports split-tender scenarios where multiple payment records link to a single order.

attributes.name: member_id
attributes.description: Reference to the guest loyalty account from which points or rewards were redeemed as a payment tender. Applicable when tender_type is loyalty_redemption. Links to the loyalty domain for redemption tracking.

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: The identifier of the specific POS terminal or payment device on which this transaction was processed. Used for terminal-level reconciliation, hardware fault analysis, and PCI DSS scope management.

attributes.name: profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: Required for payment reconciliation reports linking each payment to the guest profile; essential for fraud detection and loyalty crediting.

attributes.name: redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: Payments using points-as-tender must reference the specific redemption event for payment reconciliation, tender-type reporting, and processor settlement. loyalty_points_redeemed is a denormalized scal

attributes.name: refund_id
attributes.description: The identifier of the original payment transaction being refunded or reversed. Populated only when payment_status is refunded or voided. Enables linkage between the original charge and its corresponding credit for reconciliation.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: End-of-shift cash drawer reconciliation and tip pooling require linking each payment to the cashier's shift. Shift-level revenue vs. labor cost reporting and IRS tip reporting (Form 8027) depend on th

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the payment was captured. Used for site-level financial reconciliation and AUV (Average Unit Volume) reporting.

attributes.name: applied_amount
attributes.description: The net amount actually applied from this tender toward the order balance. In split-tender scenarios, this may be less than the tendered_amount. Represents the actual financial obligation satisfied by this tender line.

attributes.name: authorization_code
attributes.description: The alphanumeric approval code returned by the payment processor or card network upon successful authorization of a card-based tender. Used for chargeback resolution, settlement reconciliation, and audit purposes.

attributes.name: captured_timestamp
attributes.description: The real-world date and time when the payment tender was captured at the Point of Sale (POS) or digital ordering channel. Principal business event timestamp for the payment record. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).

attributes.name: card_entry_method
attributes.description: The method by which card data was captured at the point of interaction. Impacts interchange rates, fraud liability, and PCI DSS compliance scope. Contactless includes NFC/tap-to-pay and mobile wallet transactions.

attributes.name: card_type
attributes.description: The card network or brand associated with the payment card used for this tender (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex). Used for interchange fee analysis and payment mix reporting.

attributes.name: change_due_amount
attributes.description: The amount of change to be returned to the guest when the tendered_amount exceeds the applied_amount. Applicable primarily to cash tender transactions. Zero for card and digital payment types.

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: The service channel through which the payment was initiated. Distinct from tender_type (the instrument). Enables channel-level revenue analysis across POS (Point of Sale), DT (Drive-Thru), OLO (Online Ordering), 3PD (Third-Party Delivery), and catering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pos|drive_thru|online|mobile_app|kiosk|3pd|catering — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp recording when this payment record was first written to the data platform. Distinct from captured_timestamp which reflects the real-world payment event time.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international restaurant markets.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The meal period or daypart during which the payment was captured. A key dimension in foodservice analytics for revenue distribution, staffing optimization, and promotional performance measurement across breakfast, lunch, dinner, and late-night periods.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: The total monetary discount applied to this payment tender, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, and employee meal discounts. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component for net revenue calculation.

attributes.name: gift_card_number_masked
attributes.description: Masked identifier for the restaurant-issued gift card used in this tender transaction. Retains only partial digits for identification while protecting the full card value. Applicable when tender_type is gift_card.

attributes.name: interchange_fee_amount
attributes.description: The fee charged by the card-issuing bank for processing this card payment, expressed in the transaction currency. A key component of payment processing cost analysis and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculations.

attributes.name: is_split_tender
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this payment record is part of a split-tender transaction where multiple payment instruments were used to satisfy a single order. True when the order has more than one associated payment tender record.

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this payment tender record has been voided prior to settlement. A voided transaction was authorized but cancelled before batch close, distinguishing it from a post-settlement refund.

attributes.name: masked_card_number
attributes.description: PCI DSS-compliant masked representation of the payment card number, retaining only the first 4 and last 4 digits (e.g., 4111********1111). Full PANs (Primary Account Numbers) are never stored in the lakehouse per PCI DSS Requirement 3.3.

attributes.name: offline_authorization_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this payment was authorized in offline mode due to network connectivity loss at the restaurant. Offline authorizations carry elevated fraud and chargeback risk and require special reconciliation handling.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the payment tender record. Tracks the payment workflow from authorization through settlement or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|settled|declined|voided|refunded|pending — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]

attributes.name: pos_transaction_number
attributes.description: The externally-known transaction number assigned by the Oracle MICROS POS system at the time of payment capture. Used for end-of-day reconciliation, chargeback research, and audit trails.

attributes.name: processor_name
attributes.description: The name of the payment processing service provider that handled authorization and settlement for this tender (e.g., Worldpay, Heartland, Stripe, Square). Used for processor-level performance and fee analysis.

attributes.name: processor_reference_code
attributes.description: The unique transaction identifier assigned by the external payment processor (e.g., Stripe, Worldpay, Heartland) for this tender. Used for cross-system reconciliation between POS and payment gateway records.

attributes.name: promo_code_applied
attributes.description: The promotional or discount code entered by the guest at the time of payment, if applicable. Used to link payment records to marketing campaign performance and LTO (Limited Time Offer) redemption analytics.

attributes.name: refund_reason
attributes.description: Categorical reason code explaining why a refund or void was issued for this payment. Used for operational quality analysis, CSAT (Customer Satisfaction) root cause investigation, and fraud monitoring.

attributes.name: response_code
attributes.description: The ISO 8583 or processor-specific response code returned by the payment network for this authorization attempt (e.g., 00 = Approved, 05 = Do Not Honor, 51 = Insufficient Funds). Used for decline analysis and payment success rate monitoring.

attributes.name: settlement_batch_code
attributes.description: The batch identifier assigned by the payment processor during end-of-day settlement processing. Groups all captured transactions submitted together for bank funding. Used for daily cash reconciliation and SAP S/4HANA AR posting.

attributes.name: settlement_date
attributes.description: The date on which the payment processor settled funds to the restaurant's bank account. Distinct from captured_timestamp. Used for cash flow forecasting, bank reconciliation, and financial close processes in SAP S/4HANA.

attributes.name: split_tender_sequence
attributes.description: Sequential line number identifying the order of tender application within a split-tender transaction. For example, a guest paying part cash and part credit card would have sequence 1 (cash) and sequence 2 (credit card). Enables correct reconstruction of multi-tender payment scenarios.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The total sales tax or VAT amount included in this payment tender, as calculated by the POS system based on applicable jurisdiction tax rules. Required for tax remittance reporting and SAP S/4HANA tax posting.

attributes.name: tender_type
attributes.description: Classification of the payment instrument used for this tender. Supports split-tender scenarios. Key dimension for payment mix analysis and financial settlement routing. 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) settlement represents aggregated remittance from delivery partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|loyalty_redemption|3pd_settlement|mobile_wallet|voucher — promote to reference product if additional tender types are needed]

attributes.name: tendered_amount
attributes.description: The gross amount presented by the guest for this specific tender line. In cash scenarios, this may exceed the order total, resulting in change due. In card scenarios, this equals the charged amount. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for payment-level financial analysis.

attributes.name: third_party_delivery_partner
attributes.description: Identifies the 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) aggregator platform responsible for payment settlement when tender_type is 3pd_settlement. Used for partner-level remittance reconciliation and commission tracking.

attributes.name: third_party_order_reference
attributes.description: The order identifier assigned by the 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) partner platform (e.g., DoorDash order ID, Uber Eats order ID). Used to cross-reference restaurant POS records with delivery partner settlement reports.

attributes.name: tip_amount
attributes.description: Gratuity amount added by the guest to this payment tender. Tracked separately from the order total for labor distribution, tax reporting, and server tip-out calculations. Applicable in casual and fine-dining service models.

attributes.name: token
attributes.description: A network-issued or processor-issued token that replaces the actual card PAN (Primary Account Number) for secure storage and recurring payment processing. Enables PCI DSS scope reduction through tokenization.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp recording when this payment record was last modified in the data platform, such as when a status transitions from authorized to settled or a refund is processed.

products.name: status_event
products.description: Immutable event log tracking every lifecycle state transition of a guest order from placement through fulfillment or cancellation. States include: received, acknowledged, in_preparation, ready, dispatched, delivered, cancelled, voided. Captures the state, prior state, transition timestamp, triggering actor (POS, KDS, OLO, 3PD webhook), and any exception reason. Enables SOS (Speed of Service) and ticket time analytics.
products.reference: Oracle MICROS KDS Status / Olo Order Webhook Event

attributes.name: status_event_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each immutable order lifecycle state transition event record in the Silver layer. Primary key of this append-only event log.

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee (FOH cashier, BOH cook, or manager) who manually triggered this state transition. Null when the transition was system- or device-initiated. Supports labor accountability and SOS coaching.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order for which this status transition event was recorded. Links all lifecycle events back to the originating order.

attributes.name: kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.kitchen_station. Business justification: Station-level SOS breach analysis and kitchen throughput reporting — core QSR operational reports — require joining status_event to kitchen_station. status_event.kds_station_code is a denormalized sta

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Oracle MICROS POS terminal or kiosk that captured or processed this state transition event. Used for terminal-level performance diagnostics and audit trails.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Speed-of-service (SOS) reporting by shift is a primary QSR operational KPI. status_event records SOS metrics per order state transition; linking to shift enables shift-level SOS performance dashboards

attributes.name: source_status_event_id
attributes.description: The native event or transaction identifier assigned by the originating source system (e.g., MICROS transaction ID, OLO event UUID, KDS bump ID). Enables exact-match deduplication and cross-system reconciliation during Silver layer ingestion.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where the order was placed and fulfilled. Used to segment Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time analytics by location.

attributes.name: business_date
attributes.description: The operational business date to which this event is attributed for financial and operational reporting purposes. May differ from event_date for late-night orders that cross midnight but belong to the prior business day's P&L period.

attributes.name: cumulative_elapsed_seconds
attributes.description: Total number of seconds elapsed from the initial 'received' event to this event, representing the running ticket time for the order up to this point. Used to measure end-to-end Speed of Service (SOS) at any point in the lifecycle.

attributes.name: current_state
attributes.description: The order lifecycle state that this event transitions the order INTO. Drives Speed of Service (SOS) analytics, KDS routing, and fulfillment tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|acknowledged|in_preparation|ready|dispatched|delivered|cancelled|voided — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: data_quality_flag
attributes.description: Silver layer data quality classification for this event record. Values: valid (passed all checks), suspect (failed one or more quality rules), duplicate (identified as a duplicate of another event), late_arriving (received significantly after event_timestamp), corrected (overrides a prior suspect record).

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The operational daypart segment during which this event occurred (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Used to segment Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput analytics by time-of-day business period.

attributes.name: delivery_zone
attributes.description: Geographic delivery zone or radius tier assigned to this order for third-party delivery (3PD) or direct delivery fulfillment. Used for delivery time SOS benchmarking and zone-level fulfillment analytics.

attributes.name: drive_thru_lane
attributes.description: Identifies the specific drive-thru (DT) lane associated with this order event for multi-lane drive-thru configurations. Null or 'none' for non-drive-thru channels. Enables per-lane SOS and throughput analytics.

attributes.name: elapsed_seconds_in_prior_state
attributes.description: Number of seconds the order spent in the prior_state before transitioning to the current_state. Computed at ingestion time from consecutive event timestamps. Core input for Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time analytics.

attributes.name: event_date
attributes.description: Calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which this event occurred, derived from event_timestamp. Stored as a discrete DATE column to support efficient date-based partitioning, daily SOS reporting, and Same-Store Sales (SSS) period alignment.

attributes.name: event_sequence
attributes.description: Monotonically increasing integer representing the ordinal position of this event within the full lifecycle of a single order. Enables reconstruction of the complete state machine path (e.g., 1=received, 2=acknowledged, 3=in_preparation).

attributes.name: event_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the order transitioned into the current state. The principal real-world event time used for Speed of Service (SOS) and ticket time calculations.

attributes.name: exception_reason_code
attributes.description: Structured code indicating why an exceptional transition occurred (e.g., ITEM_OUT_OF_STOCK, GUEST_CANCEL, PAYMENT_FAILED, EQUIPMENT_DOWN, DUPLICATE_ORDER). Null for normal lifecycle transitions. Drives exception reporting and root-cause analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full code list]

attributes.name: exception_reason_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative describing the exception or anomaly that caused this state transition, as entered by staff or returned by the triggering system. Supplements the structured exception_reason_code for operational context.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal reporting period (e.g., '2024-P04-W02') to which this event belongs, aligned to the company's internal fiscal calendar. Enables period-over-period SOS and throughput comparisons in financial reporting.

attributes.name: fulfillment_mode
attributes.description: Indicates whether the order was placed for immediate fulfillment, scheduled for a future time (advance order), or ASAP (as-soon-as-possible with a requested window). Affects SOS target calculation and kitchen scheduling.

attributes.name: ingestion_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time this event record was first written into the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse. Distinct from event_timestamp (real-world event time). Used for data latency monitoring, pipeline SLA tracking, and audit trail.

attributes.name: is_cancellation_event
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this event represents an order cancellation (current_state = 'cancelled'). Enables rapid filtering for cancellation rate analytics, refund processing triggers, and guest recovery workflows.

attributes.name: is_sos_breach
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the cumulative elapsed time at this event exceeded the configured SOS target for the channel and daypart. True = SOS breach detected. Drives SOS compliance dashboards and operational alerts.

attributes.name: is_terminal_state
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the current_state is a terminal (end-of-lifecycle) state for the order (i.e., delivered, cancelled, or voided). Simplifies filtering for completed order analytics without requiring state enumeration logic.

attributes.name: is_void_event
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this event represents an order void (current_state = 'voided'). Voids differ from cancellations in that they are typically manager-initiated post-tender corrections. Supports POS audit and loss prevention reporting.

attributes.name: manager_override
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this state transition required a manager authorization override (e.g., void, post-close cancellation, or exception state). Supports loss prevention, audit compliance, and operational accountability reporting.

attributes.name: olo_order_reference
attributes.description: External order reference number assigned by the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for online and mobile orders. Populated only for OLO-originated events. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and the OLO platform.

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the order by business purpose. Values: standard (regular guest order), lto (Limited Time Offer promotional order), catering, group, loyalty_redemption, comp (complimentary). Enables type-specific SOS benchmarking and PMIX analysis.

attributes.name: partition_date
attributes.description: The date value used as the physical partition key for this table in the Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake storage layer. Typically aligned to business_date to optimize time-range query performance for SOS and throughput analytics.

attributes.name: prior_state
attributes.description: The order lifecycle state immediately before this transition event. Null for the first event (initial placement). Enables detection of invalid state transitions and rollback analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|acknowledged|in_preparation|ready|dispatched|delivered|cancelled|voided — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: promise_time_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time communicated to the guest as the expected ready or delivery time at the moment of order placement. Used to measure promise-time accuracy and guest satisfaction (CSAT) correlation.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time this event record was last modified in the Silver layer (e.g., due to late-arriving corrections or enrichment). For a truly immutable event log this should rarely change; tracked for data quality auditing.

attributes.name: scheduled_fulfillment_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time the guest requested or the system scheduled for order fulfillment. Populated only for scheduled/advance orders. Used to calculate on-time fulfillment rate and schedule adherence.

attributes.name: service_channel
attributes.description: The order fulfillment channel through which the guest placed the order. Drives channel-specific SOS benchmarks and throughput analytics. Values: dine_in, drive_thru (DT), online (OLO), third_party_delivery (3PD), catering, kiosk, curbside. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dine_in|drive_thru|online|third_party_delivery|catering|kiosk|curbside — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sos_target_seconds
attributes.description: The configured Speed of Service (SOS) target in seconds for this service channel and daypart combination at the time of the event. Enables real-time and historical SOS compliance measurement against operational benchmarks.

attributes.name: third_party_delivery_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party delivery (3PD) aggregator that submitted the webhook triggering this event (e.g., doordash, uber_eats, grubhub). Null or 'none' for non-3PD channels. Enables 3PD-specific SOS and fulfillment analytics.

attributes.name: third_party_event_reference
attributes.description: External event or webhook identifier provided by the third-party delivery (3PD) provider for this specific state transition. Used for cross-system reconciliation and dispute resolution with 3PD partners.

attributes.name: triggering_actor
attributes.description: The system or human actor that initiated this state transition. Values include POS (Oracle MICROS Point of Sale), KDS (Kitchen Display System), OLO (Online Ordering platform), 3PD (Third-Party Delivery webhook), CATERING, STAFF (manual override), SYSTEM (automated rule), or GUEST (self-service cancellation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: POS|KDS|OLO|3PD|CATERING|STAFF|SYSTEM|GUEST — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: channel
products.description: Reference master defining all service channels through which guest orders are placed: POS (dine-in, counter), drive-thru (DT), online ordering (OLO), third-party delivery (3PD — DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub), catering, kiosk, and mobile app. Captures channel code, channel category, integration platform, fulfillment mode (dine-in, takeout, delivery, curbside), and active status. Serves as the FK target for guest_order channel attribution, channel-mix reporting, and ACV benchmarking.

attributes.name: channel_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the order channel. Primary key for the order_channel reference master.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Connect restaurant.channel by adding column brand_id (BIGINT) with FK to restaurant.brand.brand_id because order channels should be scoped to brands. P9: connect_table: restaurant.channel** - add column brand_id (BIGINT) with FK to restaurant.brand.b

attributes.name: activation_date
attributes.description: Date when this order channel was first activated and made available for order placement across the restaurant network.

attributes.name: average_ticket_time_seconds
attributes.description: Benchmark average ticket time (order placement to fulfillment) in seconds for this channel, used for Speed of Service (SOS) performance tracking. Null if not applicable.

attributes.name: category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the channel by service model: dine_in (FOH counter, table service), takeout (DT, counter pickup), delivery (3PD, own delivery), catering (bulk orders), hybrid (multi-mode channels like kiosk).

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: Ownership classification of the channel: owned (company-operated channels like POS, DT, proprietary OLO), third_party (external platforms like 3PD aggregators), hybrid (co-branded or white-label solutions).

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the order channel (e.g., POS, DT, OLO, 3PD, KIOSK, MOBILE, CATERING). Used as the business identifier for integration and reporting.

attributes.name: commission_rate_percent
attributes.description: Commission or transaction fee percentage charged by the channel provider (applicable to 3PD channels). Null for owned channels. Used for COGS and P&L analysis.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this order channel record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deactivation_date
attributes.description: Date when this order channel was deactivated or retired. Null if the channel is still active.

attributes.name: default_daypart
attributes.description: Default daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night) typically associated with this channel. Null if the channel operates across all dayparts equally.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the order channel, including its purpose, typical use cases, and any special operational considerations.

attributes.name: display_order
attributes.description: Numeric sort order for displaying this channel in reporting dashboards and user interfaces. Lower numbers appear first.

attributes.name: fulfillment_mode
attributes.description: Primary method by which orders placed through this channel are fulfilled: dine_in (guest consumes on-premise), takeout (guest picks up), delivery (delivered to guest location), curbside (pickup at designated area), catering (scheduled bulk delivery).

attributes.name: integration_platform
attributes.description: Name of the technology platform or system that powers this channel (e.g., Oracle MICROS, Olo, DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary mobile app). Null for native POS channels.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is currently active and available for order placement. False indicates the channel has been retired or temporarily disabled.

attributes.name: is_digital
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is a digital channel (true for OLO, mobile app, kiosk, 3PD; false for traditional POS, DT counter service).

attributes.name: kds_routing_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel are routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for BOH preparation tracking. True for most channels; may be false for catering orders prepared off-site.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the order channel (e.g., Point of Sale, Drive-Thru, Online Ordering, Third-Party Delivery, Kiosk, Mobile App, Catering).

attributes.name: requires_restaurant_assignment
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel must be assigned to a specific restaurant location at order time (true for delivery, catering; false for walk-in POS).

attributes.name: supports_guest_data_capture
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel captures guest identification and contact information (true for OLO, mobile, 3PD; false for anonymous POS transactions).

attributes.name: supports_loyalty_integration
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports integration with the customer loyalty program for points accrual and redemption.

attributes.name: supports_payment_at_order
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether payment is collected at the time of order placement (true for OLO, 3PD, mobile; false for traditional counter service where payment occurs at pickup).

attributes.name: supports_scheduled_orders
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this channel allows customers to schedule orders for future fulfillment (true for catering, some OLO platforms; false for immediate-service channels like DT).

attributes.name: target_sos_seconds
attributes.business_glossary_term: Target Speed of Service (SOS) in Seconds
attributes.description: Target Speed of Service (SOS) threshold in seconds for this channel, representing the operational goal for order fulfillment time. Used for performance benchmarking and SOP compliance.

tag_set.value: Target Speed of Service (SOS) in Seconds

attributes.name: third_party_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party delivery service provider if this channel is a 3PD channel (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, Postmates). Null for non-3PD channels.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this order channel record was last modified.

products.name: kds_ticket
products.description: Represents a kitchen display system (KDS) production ticket generated from a guest order, routed to a specific BOH station (grill, fryer, assembly, expo). Captures ticket creation time, station assignment, bump time, re-fire events, ticket time (seconds), SOS target compliance flag, and fulfillment handoff timestamp. Core operational record for kitchen throughput, ticket time compliance, BOH performance management, and FOH handoff coordination.

attributes.name: kds_ticket_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the KDS ticket. Primary key for the kds_ticket product.

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the BOH employee who was assigned to or completed this ticket. Used for individual performance tracking and labor productivity analysis.

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent guest order that generated this KDS ticket. Links the ticket to the originating transaction captured at the POS.

attributes.name: kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific BOH kitchen station (grill, fryer, assembly, expo, beverage) to which this ticket was routed. Critical for station-level performance tracking.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Reference to the workforce shift during which this ticket was processed. Enables shift-level throughput and labor efficiency analysis.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where this KDS ticket was generated. Enables location-level throughput and SOS analysis.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp indicating when this record was first inserted into the data platform. Used for data lineage and ETL tracking.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Time-of-day segment when the ticket was created. Used for daypart-specific throughput analysis and labor planning. Common values: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack.

attributes.name: item_count
attributes.description: Total number of menu items included on this KDS ticket. Used to analyze ticket complexity and its impact on preparation time.

attributes.name: modifier_count
attributes.description: Total number of modifiers (customizations, substitutions, add-ons) applied to items on this ticket. Higher modifier counts typically increase ticket time.

attributes.name: order_channel
attributes.description: Service channel through which the originating order was placed. Values include dine_in (FOH table service), drive_thru (DT), online (OLO direct), third_party_delivery (3PD platforms), catering, kiosk.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Priority classification assigned to the ticket. Rush and VIP orders receive expedited handling. Catering orders may have extended preparation windows.

attributes.name: re_fire_count
attributes.description: Number of times this ticket was re-fired. Multiple re-fires indicate recurring quality or accuracy issues. Zero indicates no remakes were required.

attributes.name: re_fire_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this ticket was re-fired (sent back to the kitchen for remake) due to quality issues, order errors, or customer complaints. True indicates a remake event occurred.
