attributes.name: re_fire_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description of why the ticket was re-fired. Common reasons include incorrect preparation, quality defect, missing items, customer request, order entry error.

attributes.name: sos_met_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether the ticket was completed within the SOS target time. True if ticket_time_seconds <= sos_target_seconds, False otherwise. Critical for operational compliance reporting.

attributes.name: sos_target_seconds
attributes.description: Target ticket time in seconds defined by restaurant operations standards for this station and order channel. Used to determine SOS compliance.

attributes.name: special_instructions
attributes.description: Free-text field containing customer special requests, preparation notes, or allergy warnings that BOH staff must follow when preparing this ticket.

attributes.name: ticket_bumped_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the ticket was bumped (cleared) from the KDS screen by BOH staff, indicating the order was sent to the customer or next station.

attributes.name: ticket_completed_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when all items on the ticket were finished and ready for service or pickup. Used to calculate total ticket time.

attributes.name: ticket_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Exact date and time when the KDS ticket was generated and routed to the station. Start point for ticket time calculation and SOS measurement.

attributes.name: ticket_number
attributes.description: Human-readable ticket number displayed on the KDS screen and printed on kitchen receipts. Used by BOH staff to identify and track production items.

attributes.name: ticket_sequence_number
attributes.description: Sequential order number of this ticket within the parent order. Multi-station orders generate multiple tickets; this field tracks the sequence.

attributes.name: ticket_started_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when BOH staff began actively preparing the items on this ticket. Marks transition from new to in_progress status.

attributes.name: ticket_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the KDS ticket. Tracks progression from new (just routed) through in_progress (being prepared), completed (finished), bumped (cleared from screen), voided (cancelled), or re_fired (sent back for remake).

attributes.name: ticket_time_seconds
attributes.description: Total elapsed time in seconds from ticket creation to ticket completion. Core KPI for kitchen throughput and speed of service (SOS) performance measurement.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp indicating when this record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.

attributes.name: void_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this ticket was voided (cancelled) before completion. True indicates the ticket was cancelled and items were not prepared.

attributes.name: void_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description of why the ticket was voided. Common reasons include customer cancellation, order entry error, system error, duplicate ticket.

products.name: delivery_order
products.description: Manages the delivery fulfillment record for orders dispatched via third-party delivery (3PD) platforms (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub) or first-party delivery. Captures 3PD platform reference ID, courier assignment, estimated and actual delivery times, delivery distance, delivery fee, platform commission rate, delivery status, and any delivery exception (late, missing item, cancelled). Bridges the Olo digital ordering platform data with internal order records.

attributes.name: delivery_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the delivery order record. Primary key.

attributes.name: employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the courier or driver assigned to deliver the order. May be null if not yet assigned.

attributes.name: guest_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.address. Business justification: Delivery operations must track which saved guest address was used per delivery order for address reuse analytics, delivery accuracy reporting, and address validation audits. Plain-text address columns

attributes.name: guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent order being fulfilled via delivery. Links to the core order transaction.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Delivery labor cost per shift and driver productivity reporting require linking each delivery to the shift it occurred in. Shift-level delivery SOS analysis and driver performance reviews depend on th

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_time_minutes
attributes.description: Actual time in minutes taken by the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, calculated from picked up timestamp to actual delivery timestamp.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when the order was delivered to the guest, as confirmed by the courier or delivery platform.

attributes.name: actual_prep_time_minutes
attributes.description: Actual time in minutes taken by the restaurant to prepare the order, calculated from order confirmation to ready for pickup.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this delivery order record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery order (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).

attributes.name: customer_feedback
attributes.description: Free-text feedback provided by the guest about the delivery experience. Used for quality improvement and service recovery.

attributes.name: customer_rating
attributes.description: Guest rating for the delivery experience on a scale of 1 to 5. Used for Net Promoter Score (NPS) and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) tracking.

attributes.name: delivery_address_line1
attributes.description: Primary street address line for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_address_line2
attributes.description: Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_city
attributes.description: City name for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).

attributes.name: delivery_distance_km
attributes.description: Calculated distance in kilometers from the restaurant to the delivery destination. Used for delivery fee calculation and logistics analysis.

attributes.name: delivery_exception_type
attributes.description: Type of exception or issue encountered during delivery fulfillment. Used for quality tracking and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|late_delivery|missing_items|wrong_address|customer_unavailable|order_damaged|cancelled_by_customer|cancelled_by_restaurant — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: delivery_fee_amount
attributes.description: Delivery fee charged to the guest for the delivery service. Excludes taxes and tips.

attributes.name: delivery_instructions
attributes.description: Special instructions provided by the guest for delivery (e.g., gate code, building entrance, leave at door). May contain personally identifiable information (PII).

attributes.name: delivery_latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.

attributes.name: delivery_longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.

attributes.name: delivery_postal_code

products.name: catering_order

Distinct from standard guest orders due to advance booking lead time, custom BOM requirements, and dedicated fulfillment workflow.

products.name: discount
products.description: Records all discounts, promotions, coupons, and comp adjustments applied to a guest order or individual order items. Captures discount type (coupon, loyalty redemption, employee meal, manager comp, promotional LTO, bundle/combo deal), discount amount or percentage, promotion reference, authorization employee, and pre-approved vs. exception-based flag. Supports COGS% impact analysis, promotional ROI tracking, and combo savings attribution.

attributes.name: discount_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the order discount record. Primary key.

attributes.name: authorization_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee who authorized or applied the discount, particularly for manager comps and exception-based discounts.

attributes.name: guest_order_id

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific menu item to which this discount was applied. Null if discount applies to entire order.

attributes.name: offer_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.offer. Business justification: Offer performance reporting and redemption cost accounting require tracing each applied discount back to the originating loyalty offer. Restaurant operators need this link to measure offer ROI, valida

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the POS terminal or workstation where the discount was applied.

attributes.name: profile_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: Needed for discount usage analytics per guest, supporting marketing ROI analysis and compliance with promotional eligibility rules.

attributes.name: reward_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.reward. Business justification: A discount line is often the direct materialization of a loyalty reward (free item, percentage off). Linking discount to the specific reward enables reward-level COGS tracking, redemption validation a

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Shift-level discount audit is a standard restaurant loss-prevention control. Managers review discount frequency and value per shift to detect abuse, training gaps, or unauthorized comps. authorization

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant location where this discount was applied.

attributes.name: applied_at
attributes.description: The date and time when the discount was applied to the order or item at the Point of Sale (POS).

attributes.name: authorization_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether manager or supervisor authorization was required to apply this discount.

attributes.description: Specifies the order channel(s) for which this discount is valid (e.g., dine_in, drive_thru, online_ordering, third_party_delivery). Null if no restriction.

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: The coupon code, promo code, or loyalty redemption code entered or scanned at the Point of Sale (POS).

attributes.name: cogs_impact_amount

attributes.description: The calculated impact of this discount on Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for financial and profitability analysis.

attributes.name: created_at
attributes.description: The date and time when this discount record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code

attributes.name: daypart_restriction
attributes.description: Specifies the daypart(s) during which this discount is valid (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Null if no restriction.

attributes.description: Classification of the discount mechanism applied to the order or item.

attributes.name: final_price
attributes.description: The final price after the discount was applied to the item or order.

attributes.name: is_pre_approved
attributes.description: Indicates whether the discount was pre-approved through a promotional campaign (true) or applied as an exception at the Point of Sale (false).

attributes.name: is_stackable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this discount can be combined with other discounts on the same order or item.

attributes.name: is_voided

attributes.name: revenue_impact_amount
attributes.description: The calculated impact of this discount on gross revenue for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.

attributes.description: Indicates whether the discount is applied before tax calculation, after tax calculation, or results in a tax-exempt transaction.

attributes.name: valid_to_date

attributes.name: voided_at
attributes.description: The date and time when the discount was voided or removed from the order. Null if discount was not voided.

products.name: refund
products.description: Captures refund and void transactions issued against a completed or in-progress guest order. Records refund reason (wrong item, quality complaint, missing item, guest dissatisfaction), refund amount, refund method (original tender, gift card, loyalty points), authorizing manager, refund timestamp, and whether the refund was full or partial. Supports CSAT root-cause analysis and exception management.

attributes.description: Identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved and authorized the refund transaction.

attributes.description: Identifier of the guest who received the refund, if known and captured in the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system.

domains.name: supply
domains.description: Manages end-to-end food and non-food supply chain including supplier master data, vendor management, sourcing, purchase orders, inbound logistics, distribution center operations, and ingredient traceability. Tracks COGS, supplier performance, contract compliance, and spend analytics via Coupa Procurement.

products.name: supplier
products.description: Master record for every supplier and vendor in the foodservice supply chain, including food and non-food suppliers, distributors, co-manufacturers, and their key contacts. Captures supplier identity, classification (broadline, specialty, local), approval status, diversity certification, payment terms, lead times, regulatory compliance status (FDA, USDA, HACCP), and primary/secondary contact information. SSOT for supplier identity and contact details across supply chain operations. Sourced from Coupa Procurement supplier master and SAP S/4HANA vendor master (MM). [SSOT] Aliases canonical owner procurement.procurement_supplier for concept 'supplier'. SSOT: authoritative source is procurement.procurement_supplier. products.name: ingredient
products.description: Master catalog of all food ingredients, raw materials, beverages, and packaging SKUs procured across the foodservice supply chain. Captures SKU code, ingredient name, commodity category, unit of measure, allergen flags (Big 9), USDA grade, country of origin, shelf life days, storage temperature requirements (ambient/refrigerated/frozen), and HACCP critical control classification. Serves as the supply-side item master linking to menu domain BOM for recipe costing. SSOT for ingredient identity across supply, inventory, and menu domains. Sourced from SAP MM material master and MarketMan Inventory Management.

products.name: supplier_ingredient_sourcing
products.description: This association product represents the sourcing contract/agreement between a supplier and an ingredient in the foodservice supply chain. It captures which suppliers are approved to provide which ingredients, at what price, under what terms, and for what effective period. Each record links one supply_supplier to one ingredient and carries attributes — unit price, lead time, minimum order quantity, preferred flag, and effective dates — that exist only in the context of this specific supplier-ingredient pairing. This is the operational Vendor Item Master / Approved Supplier List (ASL) used by procurement for spend analytics, supplier rationalization, and COGS management.. Existence Justification: In foodservice procurement, a supplier can provide multiple ingredients (e.g., a broadline distributor like Sysco supplies hundreds of SKUs), and a single ingredient (e.g., Roma tomatoes) can be sourced from multiple approved suppliers at different prices, lead times, and minimum order quantities. This relationship — commonly called an Approved Supplier List (ASL) or Vendor Item Master — is actively managed by procurement teams in systems like Coupa and SAP MM, and carries its own operational data (unit price, lead time, preferred flag, effective dates) that belongs to neither the supplier nor the ingredient alone.

The ingredient-level cost_per_unit may be retained as a standard/benchmark cost for menu costing purposes, but the procurement price lives on the association.]
