attributes.name: preferred_supplier_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this supplier is the preferred (primary) source for this ingredient. Used by procurement to drive sourcing decisions and auto-populate purchase orders.

attributes.name: unit_price
attributes.description: The negotiated purchase price per unit of this ingredient from this specific supplier. Varies by supplier-ingredient combination and cannot be stored on either master record alone.
attributes.references: Sourced from Coupa contract line / SAP MM info record (EINA/EINE)

domains.name: inventory
domains.description: Manages BOH stock levels, SKU tracking, PAR levels (Periodic Automatic Replenishment), waste tracking (Waste%), yield management, receiving, transfers, physical counts, and replenishment orders via MarketMan. Supports COGS% optimization and food cost control across all restaurant units.

products.name: stock_item
products.description: Master record for every SKU tracked in restaurant inventory — food, beverage, packaging, and non-food supplies. Captures SKU code, item name, unit of measure (UOM), item category (protein, produce, dry goods, beverage, paper goods, cleaning), storage class (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), reorder point, reorder quantity, standard cost, vendor item code, shelf life days, HACCP temperature range, allergen flags, daypart applicability, seasonal adjustment flags, and active status. Also owns PAR-level configuration per unit-location: PAR quantity, minimum quantity (reorder point), maximum quantity (shelf capacity), day-of-week overrides, seasonal adjustment flags, and effective date range. This is the SSOT for all stockable items and their replenishment parameters managed through MarketMan.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the stock item. Primary key for the stock_item product.

attributes.name: haccp_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Each high-risk stock item (raw proteins, dairy, shellfish) is governed by a specific HACCP plan defining its CCPs, temperature limits, and handling procedures. This link enables automatic HACCP plan l

attributes.name: ingredient_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: REQUIRED: Ingredient Inventory Valuation Report needs each stock item linked to its ingredient master for nutrition, allergen, and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: primary_kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.kitchen_station. Business justification: Station-level par management and KDS routing configuration: each stock item has a primary prep/consumption station (e.g., beef patties → grill station). Operations managers use this to configure stati

attributes.name: allergen_eggs
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain eggs. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_fish
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain fish. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_milk
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain milk or dairy derivatives. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_peanuts
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain peanuts. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_shellfish
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain crustacean shellfish. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_soybeans
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain soybeans. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_tree_nuts
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain tree nuts. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: allergen_wheat
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains or may contain wheat. Required for menu labeling and allergen disclosure.

attributes.name: case_pack_quantity
attributes.description: Number of individual units contained in one case. Used for ordering and receiving conversions.

attributes.name: cost_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO currency code for the standard cost (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the stock item record was first created in the inventory management system.

attributes.name: discontinuation_date
attributes.description: Date when the item was or will be discontinued from inventory. Used for phase-out planning and historical analysis.

attributes.name: gtin
attributes.description: International product identifier used for supply chain tracking and barcode scanning. May be UPC, EAN, or other GS1 format.

attributes.name: haccp_max_temp_f
attributes.business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Maximum Temperature Fahrenheit
attributes.description: Maximum safe storage temperature in Fahrenheit to maintain product quality and food safety standards.

tag_set.value: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Maximum Temperature Fahrenheit

attributes.name: haccp_min_temp_f
attributes.business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Minimum Temperature Fahrenheit
attributes.description: Minimum safe storage temperature in Fahrenheit required to prevent bacterial growth and ensure food safety compliance.

tag_set.value: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Minimum Temperature Fahrenheit

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Indicates whether the stock item is currently available for ordering and use. Inactive items are retained for historical reporting but not available for new transactions.

attributes.name: is_gluten_free
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item is certified gluten-free or naturally contains no gluten. Critical for dietary accommodation and menu labeling.

attributes.name: is_gmo_free
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item is certified non-GMO. Used for menu transparency and consumer preference alignment.

attributes.name: is_halal
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item meets Islamic dietary law requirements. Used for menu segmentation and cultural accommodation.

attributes.name: is_kosher
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item meets Jewish dietary law requirements. Used for menu segmentation and cultural accommodation.

attributes.name: is_organic
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item is certified organic according to USDA standards. Used for menu marketing and premium pricing.

attributes.name: is_vegan
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains no animal products or by-products. Used for menu filtering and dietary preference alignment.

attributes.name: is_vegetarian
attributes.description: Indicates whether the item contains no meat, poultry, or seafood. Used for menu filtering and dietary preference alignment.

attributes.name: item_category
attributes.description: Primary classification of the stock item by food service category. Used for inventory organization, COGS analysis, and procurement planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|produce|dairy|dry_goods|beverage|paper_goods|cleaning|packaging — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: item_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the stock item including brand, size, packaging, and other distinguishing characteristics.

attributes.name: item_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the stock item as it appears in inventory management and ordering systems.

attributes.name: item_subcategory
attributes.description: Secondary classification providing finer granularity within the item category (e.g., beef, chicken, lettuce, tomato).

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the stock item record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.

attributes.name: par_level
attributes.description: Target inventory quantity that should be maintained at all times to support operational needs without overstocking. Used for automated reorder triggers.

attributes.name: reorder_point
attributes.description: Inventory threshold quantity that triggers a replenishment order. Set below PAR level to account for lead time.

attributes.name: reorder_quantity
attributes.description: Standard quantity to order when inventory falls below reorder point. Calculated to restore inventory to PAR level.

attributes.name: shelf_life_days
attributes.description: Number of days the item remains usable after receipt. Critical for waste management, FIFO rotation, and food safety compliance.

attributes.name: sku_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code identifying the stock item across all restaurant units and inventory systems. This is the business identifier used in MarketMan and POS systems.

attributes.name: standard_cost
attributes.description: Expected unit cost of the item used for COGS calculations and variance analysis. Updated periodically based on procurement contracts.

attributes.name: storage_class
attributes.description: Required storage temperature zone for the item. Determines BOH storage location and HACCP compliance requirements.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: Standard unit in which the stock item is tracked, ordered, and received. Critical for accurate inventory counts and cost calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pound|kilogram|ounce|liter|gallon|box|bag|dozen — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: vendor_item_code
attributes.description: Supplier's unique identifier for this item. Used for purchase order matching and invoice reconciliation.

attributes.name: yield_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of usable product after preparation and trimming. Critical for recipe costing and waste tracking.

products.name: stock_location
products.description: Master record for every physical storage location within a restaurant unit where inventory is held — walk-in cooler, walk-in freezer, dry storage room, BOH prep area, FOH service station, bar storage. Captures location code, location name, location type (refrigerated, frozen, ambient, bar), temperature zone, capacity (cubic feet or shelf count), restaurant unit reference, and active status. Enables granular stock-on-hand tracking by storage zone.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the stock location. Primary key.

attributes.name: manager_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for HACCP temperature monitoring accountability; each storage location has an assigned manager employee who signs compliance reports.

attributes.name: restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where this stock location is physically situated.

attributes.name: stock_equipment_asset_id
attributes.description: Identifier for the refrigeration or climate control equipment serving this location, used for maintenance tracking and temperature monitoring integration.

attributes.name: access_control_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether electronic or physical access control (keycard, PIN, lock) is required to enter this location.

attributes.name: activation_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock location was first activated and made available for inventory storage.

attributes.name: allows_receiving
attributes.description: Indicates whether this location is designated as a receiving point for incoming inventory deliveries.

attributes.name: allows_transfers
attributes.description: Indicates whether inventory can be transferred into or out of this location to other stock locations within the restaurant or across units.

attributes.name: allows_waste_tracking
attributes.description: Indicates whether waste events (spoilage, damage, expiration) can be recorded against inventory in this location for Waste% calculation.

attributes.name: bin_count
attributes.description: Number of individual storage bins or compartments within this location for granular SKU organization.

attributes.name: building_section
attributes.description: Specific section or wing of the building where the location is situated (e.g., Kitchen, Bar, Prep Area, Receiving Dock).

attributes.name: capacity_cubic_feet
attributes.description: Total storage capacity of the location measured in cubic feet.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the stock location record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: cycle_count_frequency_days
attributes.description: Number of days between scheduled physical inventory cycle counts for this location to maintain stock accuracy.

attributes.name: deactivation_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock location was deactivated or decommissioned and is no longer available for inventory storage.

attributes.name: floor_level
attributes.description: Physical floor level where the storage location is situated (e.g., 1 for ground floor, -1 for basement, 2 for second floor).

attributes.name: last_cycle_count_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent physical inventory cycle count was completed for this location.

attributes.name: last_maintenance_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent maintenance or inspection was performed on the storage location or its equipment.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the stock location record was most recently updated.

attributes.name: location_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location within the restaurant unit (e.g., WIC01 for walk-in cooler 1, DRY02 for dry storage room 2).

attributes.name: location_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the stock location (e.g., Walk-In Cooler, Dry Storage Room, Bar Storage, Prep Area Shelf).

attributes.name: location_type
attributes.description: Classification of the storage location based on its primary function and environmental control (refrigerated, frozen, ambient, bar, prep_area, service_station).

attributes.name: next_scheduled_cycle_count_date
attributes.description: Date when the next physical inventory cycle count is scheduled for this location.

attributes.name: next_scheduled_maintenance_date
attributes.description: Date when the next scheduled maintenance or inspection is planned for the storage location or its equipment.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, or operational notes about the stock location.

attributes.name: par_level_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether PAR level inventory management is enabled for this location to trigger automatic replenishment orders.

attributes.name: primary_commodity_category
attributes.description: Primary category of inventory items typically stored in this location (e.g., Proteins, Dairy, Produce, Dry Goods, Beverages, Alcohol).

attributes.name: requires_haccp_monitoring
attributes.business_glossary_term: Requires HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Monitoring
attributes.description: Indicates whether this location requires HACCP monitoring and documentation for food safety compliance.

tag_set.value: Requires HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Monitoring

attributes.name: security_level
attributes.description: Security classification for the location indicating access control requirements (open: unrestricted, restricted: authorized staff only, locked: key/code required, high_value: premium items with enhanced security).

attributes.name: shelf_count
attributes.description: Number of shelves or storage levels available in this location for organizing inventory.

attributes.name: stock_location_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the stock location (active: in use, inactive: temporarily not in use, maintenance: under repair, decommissioned: permanently retired).

attributes.name: storage_area_type
attributes.business_glossary_term: Storage Area Type (Back of House / Front of House)
attributes.description: Indicates whether the location is in Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) area of the restaurant.

tag_set.value: Storage Area Type (Back of House / Front of House)

attributes.name: target_temperature_max_f
attributes.description: Maximum target temperature in Fahrenheit for this storage location to maintain food safety and quality standards.

attributes.name: target_temperature_min_f
attributes.description: Minimum target temperature in Fahrenheit for this storage location to maintain food safety and quality standards.

attributes.name: temperature_monitoring_frequency_hours
attributes.description: Required frequency in hours for temperature checks and logging at this location to ensure food safety compliance.

attributes.name: temperature_zone
attributes.description: Temperature control classification for the location (freezer: below 0°F, cooler: 32-40°F, ambient: room temperature, controlled: climate-controlled but not refrigerated).

products.name: on_hand_balance
products.description: Current stock-on-hand snapshot for each SKU at each storage location within a restaurant unit. Captures quantity on hand, quantity reserved (committed to prep), quantity available, last physical count date, last adjustment date, last received date, unit cost, extended value, and variance from PAR level. Updated by receiving, transfers, waste events, and physical counts. The authoritative real-time inventory position record used for replenishment decisions and food cost reporting.

attributes.name: on_hand_balance_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the on-hand balance record. Primary key for the inventory position snapshot.

attributes.name: last_physical_count_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.physical_count. Business justification: on_hand_balance tracks the last physical count date as a string date field (last_physical_count_date). Replacing this with a FK to physical_count allows the system to JOIN directly to the full count e

attributes.name: primary_on_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where this inventory is held. Links to the restaurant operations master.

attributes.name: primary_on_sku_stock_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this balance is recorded. Links to the inventory item master.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location within the restaurant (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer, prep area). Links to storage location master.

attributes.name: abc_classification
attributes.description: The ABC inventory classification based on value and usage velocity. A items are high-value/high-velocity requiring tight control, C items are low-value/low-velocity.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which unit_cost and extended_value are denominated.

attributes.name: cycle_count_frequency
attributes.description: The frequency at which this SKU should be physically counted as part of the cycle count program. Typically aligned with ABC classification.

attributes.name: days_until_expiration
attributes.description: The number of days remaining until the expiration date. Used to prioritize usage and identify items at risk of waste.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The date by which this inventory must be used or discarded. Critical for food safety, waste management, and FIFO (First In First Out) rotation.

attributes.name: extended_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of the on-hand inventory, calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost. Used for financial reporting and COGS% analysis.

attributes.name: inventory_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of this inventory position. Determines whether the inventory is available for use, held for quality review, or flagged for disposal.

attributes.name: is_perishable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this SKU is a perishable item requiring temperature control and expiration tracking. True for fresh produce, dairy, meat; false for dry goods.

attributes.name: last_adjustment_date
attributes.description: The date when the last inventory adjustment (waste, transfer, or correction) was recorded for this SKU at this location.

attributes.name: last_movement_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time of the last inventory transaction (receipt, transfer, issue, or adjustment) that affected this balance. Used to identify stagnant inventory.

attributes.name: last_received_date
attributes.description: The date when this SKU was last received into inventory at this location. Used to track inventory freshness and identify slow-moving items.

attributes.name: lot_number
attributes.description: The supplier or manufacturer lot number for traceability and recall management. Critical for food safety compliance under HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points).

attributes.name: par_level
attributes.description: The target inventory level for this SKU at this location, used for automatic replenishment decisions. Represents the optimal stock level to maintain service without excess waste.

attributes.name: quantity_available
attributes.description: The net quantity available for use or sale, calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved. Represents the true available-to-promise inventory.

attributes.name: quantity_on_hand
attributes.description: The total physical quantity of the SKU currently in stock at this location. Represents the gross inventory position before reservations.

attributes.name: quantity_reserved
attributes.description: The quantity of the SKU that is committed to prep, production, or pending orders and not available for new allocation. Used in BOH (Back of House) prep planning.

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this on-hand balance record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this on-hand balance record was last updated in the data platform. Audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: reorder_point
attributes.description: The inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered. When quantity_available falls below this threshold, the system generates a purchase order.

attributes.name: safety_stock
attributes.description: The minimum buffer stock maintained to protect against stockouts due to demand variability or supply delays. Part of the PAR level calculation.

attributes.name: sku_code
attributes.description: The business identifier code for the SKU. Denormalized from SKU master for reporting convenience.

attributes.name: sku_description
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the SKU. Denormalized from SKU master for reporting convenience.

attributes.name: snapshot_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this on-hand balance snapshot was captured. Represents the point-in-time inventory position for reporting and reconciliation.

attributes.name: temperature_zone
attributes.description: The required storage temperature zone for this SKU (ambient/dry, refrigerated, or frozen). Critical for food safety compliance and storage location assignment.

attributes.name: unit_cost
attributes.description: The cost per unit of measure for this SKU at this location. Used to calculate extended inventory value and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold).

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which the quantity is expressed (each, pound, kilogram, ounce, gallon, liter, case, box, pack, dozen). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|LB|KG|OZ|GAL|L|CS|BX|PK|DZ — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: valuation_method
attributes.description: The accounting method used to value this inventory (FIFO - First In First Out, LIFO - Last In First Out, weighted average, or standard cost). Determines COGS calculation.

attributes.name: variance_from_par
attributes.business_glossary_term: Variance from Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) Level
attributes.description: The difference between quantity_on_hand and par_level. Positive values indicate overstocking, negative values indicate understocking and trigger replenishment.

tag_set.value: Variance from Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) Level

products.name: physical_count
products.description: Records scheduled or ad-hoc physical inventory count events at a restaurant unit, capturing both count-level header metadata and line-level SKU counts in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: count date, count type (full/spot-check/cycle-count/pre-close/post-close), count status, count period, initiated-by/approved-by employees, total variance value and percentage. Line attributes (one per SKU-location counted): stock item reference, storage location, system quantity (book inventory), counted quantity, variance quantity and value ($), unit of measure, unit cost, count method (manual/scan), counted-by employee reference, and recount flag. Variance lines trigger investigation workflows and feed period-end food cost reconciliation and COGS% reporting.

attributes.name: physical_count_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the physical inventory count event. Primary key.

attributes.name: parent_count_physical_count_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original physical count ID if this count is a recount or correction of a previous count event.

attributes.name: primary_physical_initiated_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee who initiated or started the physical count event.

attributes.name: primary_physical_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where the physical count was performed.

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Physical inventory counts are conducted during a specific shift in restaurant operations. This FK enables labor cost attribution for counting activities, audit traceability by shift, and scheduling an

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: A physical count event can be scoped to a specific storage location within a restaurant unit (e.g., a walk-in cooler count vs. a dry-storage count). Adding stock_location_id as a nullable FK allows lo

attributes.name: actual_end_timestamp
attributes.description: The actual date and time when the physical count was completed and submitted.

attributes.name: actual_start_timestamp
attributes.description: The actual date and time when the physical count was started by the counting team.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the physical count was approved by the manager or supervisor.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation for why the physical count was cancelled or voided.

attributes.name: cancellation_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the physical count was cancelled or voided, if applicable.

attributes.name: count_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the physical inventory count was conducted.

attributes.name: count_method
attributes.description: The method used to perform the physical count: manual (paper-based or manual entry), barcode-scan (handheld scanner), rfid (radio-frequency identification), or hybrid (combination of methods).

attributes.name: count_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the physical count event, formatted as PC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trail.

attributes.name: count_period
attributes.description: The daypart or shift during which the count was performed, aligned with restaurant operating periods.

attributes.name: count_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the count event: scheduled (planned but not started), in-progress (counting underway), submitted (completed awaiting approval), approved (validated by manager), posted (applied to inventory system), or cancelled (voided).

attributes.name: count_type
attributes.description: Classification of the count event: full (complete inventory), spot-check (random sample), cycle-count (scheduled rotation), pre-close (before period end), post-close (after period end), or opening (new restaurant opening).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this physical count record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: is_period_end_count
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this count is a mandatory period-end count for financial close and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculation. True if period-end count, False otherwise.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this physical count record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about the count event, including explanations for significant variances, operational issues during count, or special circumstances.

attributes.name: physical_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of inventory based on the physical count results, calculated by multiplying counted quantities by unit costs.

attributes.name: posted_to_gl_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the count variance was posted to the General Ledger (GL) for financial reporting, typically after approval.

attributes.name: scheduled_start_timestamp
attributes.description: The planned date and time when the physical count was scheduled to begin.

attributes.name: submission_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the physical count was submitted for approval after completion.

attributes.name: system_inventory_value
attributes.description: The total dollar value of inventory according to the system (MarketMan) at the time of count, used as the baseline for variance calculation.

attributes.name: total_sku_counted
attributes.description: The total number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) that were physically counted during this count event.

attributes.name: total_sku_with_variance
attributes.business_glossary_term: Total SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) With Variance
attributes.description: The number of SKUs that showed a variance (difference) between system inventory and physical count.

tag_set.value: Total SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) With Variance

attributes.name: total_variance_amount
attributes.description: The total dollar value variance between system inventory and physical count results, calculated as (physical count value - system inventory value). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.

attributes.name: total_variance_percentage
attributes.description: The total variance expressed as a percentage of system inventory value, calculated as (variance amount / system inventory value) * 100. Key metric for COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) analysis and food cost control.

attributes.name: variance_reason_code
attributes.description: Primary reason code for inventory variance: theft (shrinkage), spoilage (expired/damaged), waste (prep waste), receiving-error (incorrect receipt), transfer-error (incorrect transfer), system-error (data entry mistake), or unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theft|spoilage|waste|receiving-error|transfer-error|system-error|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: waste_log
products.description: Records every food waste event at a restaurant unit — spoilage, overproduction, prep waste, expiration, and quality rejection. Captures waste date, waste time, stock item reference, waste quantity, unit of measure, waste cost ($), waste category (spoilage, overproduction, prep loss, expiration, quality-reject, theft/unknown), waste reason description, responsible station (BOH/FOH), recorded-by employee reference, and manager approval flag. Drives Waste% KPI calculation and supports yield management and COGS% optimization.

attributes.name: waste_log_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the waste log entry. Primary key.

attributes.name: equipment_asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.equipment_asset. Business justification: Equipment failure-driven waste tracking: spoilage events caused by refrigeration or cooking equipment failures must be linked to the specific asset for HACCP documentation, insurance claims, and maint

attributes.name: haccp_plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Waste events flagged as HACCP violations (waste_log.haccp_violation = true) must reference the governing HACCP plan to document which plan was breached and trigger the plan's corrective action procedu

attributes.name: ingredient_lot_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient_lot. Business justification: Food safety and recall impact: waste events must reference the specific ingredient lot discarded to support lot-level waste analysis, recall disposition reporting, and HACCP corrective action document

attributes.name: kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.kitchen_station. Business justification: Station-level waste cost reporting and HACCP accountability: waste events are attributed to the kitchen station where they occurred. `responsible_station` is a denormalized plain string; replacing it

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: Restaurant waste tracking requires linking waste events to the specific order line item that generated them (voided after prep, customer rejection). Enables food cost variance reports, COGS reconcilia

attributes.name: primary_waste_recorded_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who recorded the waste event in the system.

attributes.name: primary_waste_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit where the waste event occurred.

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the recipe or Bill of Materials (BOM) associated with the wasted prepared item, if applicable.

attributes.name: refund_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.refund. Business justification: Customer complaints resulting in refunds often generate waste log entries (food disposed after return). Direct FK enables food safety and financial reports correlating refund events with waste costs,

attributes.name: shift_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the work shift during which the waste event occurred, used for labor and operational analysis.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) that was wasted.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Waste is recorded per storage location; replacing the denormalized storage_location field with a FK enables location‑level waste analytics.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the waste log entry was approved by a manager.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The production or receiving batch identifier for the wasted item, used for traceability and food safety compliance.

attributes.name: corrective_action_taken
attributes.description: Description of any corrective action implemented to prevent recurrence of this type of waste.

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The operational time segment during which the waste event occurred (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night).

attributes.name: disposal_method
attributes.description: The method used to dispose of the wasted item: trash (landfill), compost, donation (food rescue), rendering (animal feed/byproduct), or other.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The use-by or best-before date of the wasted item, critical for expiration-related waste tracking.

attributes.name: haccp_violation
attributes.business_glossary_term: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Violation Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the waste event was associated with a HACCP critical control point violation.

tag_set.value: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Violation Flag

attributes.name: manager_approved
attributes.description: Indicates whether a manager has reviewed and approved this waste log entry.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-form notes or comments about the waste event for operational context.

attributes.name: on_hand_quantity_before_waste
attributes.description: The inventory quantity on hand immediately before the waste event was recorded.

attributes.name: par_level_at_waste
attributes.business_glossary_term: PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) Level at Waste
attributes.description: The PAR level setting for this item at the time of waste, used to analyze whether overstocking contributed to waste.

tag_set.value: PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) Level at Waste

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: The system timestamp when this waste log record was first created in the database.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The system timestamp when this waste log record was last modified.

attributes.name: temperature_at_waste
attributes.description: The temperature in Fahrenheit of the item or storage area at the time of waste, relevant for food safety and spoilage analysis.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit in which the waste quantity is measured (e.g., kilograms, pounds, each, gallons). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lb|oz|g|ea|case|box|gal|ltr|ml — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: waste_category
attributes.description: The classification of the waste event: spoilage (deterioration), overproduction (excess prepared food), prep-loss (trimming/cooking loss), expiration (past use-by date), quality-reject (failed quality standards), theft-unknown (unexplained loss).

attributes.name: waste_cost
attributes.description: The monetary cost of the wasted item in the restaurant's operating currency, calculated as quantity times unit cost.

attributes.name: waste_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the waste event occurred.

attributes.name: waste_prevention_opportunity
attributes.description: Notes on potential process improvements or training opportunities identified from this waste event.

attributes.name: waste_quantity
attributes.description: The numeric quantity of the item wasted, measured in the unit of measure specified.

attributes.name: waste_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description providing additional context and specific reason for the waste event.

attributes.name: waste_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the waste event was recorded.

products.name: stock_transfer
products.description: Records the movement of inventory between restaurant units, between storage locations within a unit, or from a unit back to a distribution center — capturing both transfer header and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: transfer date, transfer type (inter-unit/intra-unit/return-to-vendor/return-to-DC), origin and destination units/locations, transfer status, requested-by/approved-by employees, and total transfer value. Line attributes (one per SKU transferred): stock item reference, transferred quantity, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, lot number, expiration date, and condition code. Maintains inventory accuracy across the restaurant network and supports inter-unit food cost allocation.

attributes.name: stock_transfer_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the stock transfer transaction. Primary key for the stock transfer record.

attributes.name: destination_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit or distribution center receiving the transferred inventory. For intra-unit transfers, this is the same as origin unit.

attributes.name: destination_stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer) within the destination unit where items will be received. Critical for BOH (Back of House) inventory accuracy.

attributes.name: origin_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit or distribution center from which inventory is being transferred. For intra-unit transfers, this is the same as destination unit.

attributes.name: origin_stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in cooler, dry storage, freezer) within the origin unit from which items are being transferred. Critical for BOH (Back of House) inventory accuracy.

attributes.name: primary_stock_requested_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee (typically kitchen manager or shift supervisor) who initiated the stock transfer request. Used for accountability and audit trail.

attributes.name: replenishment_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Inter-unit stock transfers are frequently initiated to fulfill replenishment needs when a supplier order cannot be fulfilled in time. Linking stock_transfer to the replenishment_order that triggered i

attributes.name: tertiary_stock_received_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee at the destination unit who physically received and confirmed the transferred inventory. Null until transfer is received.

attributes.name: cancellation_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock transfer was cancelled. Null if transfer was not cancelled.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation for why the transfer was cancelled. Used for operational analysis and process improvement.

attributes.name: carrier_name
attributes.description: Name of the transportation carrier or delivery service used for inter-unit transfers. Null for intra-unit transfers or self-pickup.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this stock transfer record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: expected_delivery_date
attributes.description: Planned or estimated date when the transferred inventory is expected to arrive at the destination. Used for receiving planning and PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) level management.

attributes.name: external_transfer_reference
attributes.description: Unique identifier from the source operational system (MarketMan, SAP MM). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: Fiscal period (year and period number) to which this transfer is assigned for financial reporting. Format: YYYY-PNN (e.g., 2024-P03 for period 3 of fiscal year 2024).

attributes.name: gl_posting_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory transfer transaction was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting. May differ from physical transfer date due to period-end cutoffs.

attributes.name: haccp_monitoring_required_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Monitoring Required Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this transfer requires continuous temperature monitoring and documentation per HACCP critical control point protocols. True for high-risk perishable items.

tag_set.value: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Monitoring Required Flag

attributes.name: inspection_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes from quality inspection documenting any issues, observations, or conditions. Used for quality tracking and supplier performance evaluation.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this stock transfer record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Urgency classification for the transfer: routine (standard replenishment), high (needed within 24 hours), urgent (needed same day), emergency (critical stockout situation requiring immediate action).

attributes.name: quality_inspection_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether items in this transfer require formal quality inspection upon receipt per GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) or food safety protocols. True for high-value or high-risk items.

attributes.name: quality_inspection_status
attributes.description: Result of quality inspection at receiving: not-required (no inspection needed), pending (awaiting inspection), passed (accepted), failed (rejected), conditional-accept (accepted with notes or partial rejection).

attributes.name: shipping_method
attributes.description: Method used to transport inventory between locations: internal-delivery (company fleet), courier (contracted delivery service), third-party (3PD logistics provider), self-pickup (destination unit retrieves), direct-transfer (hand-carried for intra-unit).

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Code identifying the operational system that originated this stock transfer record: MARKETMAN (MarketMan Inventory Management), SAP-MM (SAP Materials Management), MANUAL (manually entered), LEGACY (migrated from prior system).

attributes.name: temperature_controlled_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this transfer requires temperature-controlled transportation to maintain food safety and quality. True for perishable items requiring refrigeration or freezing per HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) standards.

attributes.name: temperature_zone_required
attributes.description: Required temperature zone for transport: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (33-40°F), frozen (0°F or below), multi-temp (mixed temperature requirements). Critical for HACCP compliance and food safety.

attributes.name: total_item_count
attributes.description: Total number of distinct SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) line items included in this transfer. Used for transfer complexity assessment and receiving workload planning.

attributes.name: total_quantity_transferred
attributes.description: Aggregate quantity of all items transferred, summed across all line items. Unit of measure varies by item (cases, pounds, gallons, each). Used for high-level transfer volume tracking.

attributes.name: total_transfer_value_usd
attributes.description: Total dollar value of all items included in this stock transfer, calculated at standard cost. Critical for COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) tracking and inventory valuation across units.

attributes.name: tracking_number
attributes.description: Shipment tracking number provided by carrier for monitoring in-transit status. Null for internal transfers without formal tracking.

attributes.name: transfer_approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock transfer was approved by authorized manager. Null if not yet approved or if auto-approved (same as request date).

attributes.name: transfer_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique transfer document number used for tracking and reference in operational systems and communications. Format: STR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.

attributes.name: transfer_reason_code
attributes.description: Business reason for initiating the stock transfer: par-replenishment (restore PAR levels), excess-stock (reduce overstock), expiring-soon (move near-expiry items), quality-issue (return defective goods), menu-change (redistribute due to menu update), seasonal-adjustment (rebalance for seasonal demand).

attributes.name: transfer_reason_notes
attributes.description: Free-text explanation providing additional context for the transfer. Used for operational communication and audit documentation.

attributes.name: transfer_received_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory was physically received and confirmed at the destination location. Null until transfer is fully or partially received.

attributes.name: transfer_request_date
attributes.description: Date when the stock transfer was initially requested. Used for tracking lead times and transfer cycle analytics.

attributes.name: transfer_ship_date
attributes.description: Date when the inventory physically left the origin location. Used for in-transit tracking and speed of service metrics.

attributes.name: transfer_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer: requested (initiated but pending approval), approved (authorized for execution), rejected (denied by approver), in-transit (shipped but not yet received), received (fully received and confirmed), cancelled (voided before completion), partially-received (some items received, others pending). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|approved|rejected|in-transit|received|cancelled|partially-received — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: transfer_type
attributes.description: Classification of the transfer based on origin and destination: inter-unit (between restaurant units), intra-unit (between storage locations within same unit), return-to-dc (from unit back to distribution center), return-to-vendor (from unit back to supplier), emergency (urgent replenishment), rebalance (inventory optimization transfer).

attributes.name: variance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified between shipped quantities and received quantities. True if any line item shows variance requiring investigation.

attributes.name: variance_reason
attributes.description: Root cause classification for any quantity variance: damage-in-transit (items damaged during shipment), short-shipment (fewer items sent than documented), overage (more items received than expected), quality-rejection (items rejected at receiving), counting-error (human error in count), none (no variance).

products.name: replenishment_order
products.description: Automated or manually triggered replenishment order generated when stock falls below PAR level or reorder point, capturing both order header and line-level SKU detail in a single consolidated entity. Header attributes: order date, order type (auto-PAR/manual/emergency), restaurant unit reference, supplier reference, requested delivery date, order status, total order value, order source (MarketMan auto-replenishment/manager-initiated), and approval status. Line attributes (one per SKU ordered): stock item reference, ordered quantity, unit of measure, unit cost, extended cost, PAR quantity at time of order, on-hand quantity at time of order, variance-to-PAR quantity, and confirmed delivery quantity. Bridges inventory management and procurement; links to purchase orders in Coupa and SAP MM. Enables food cost forecasting and supplier fill-rate tracking.

attributes.name: replenishment_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the replenishment order. Primary key for the replenishment order entity.

attributes.name: primary_replenishment_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user (manager or approver) who approved the replenishment order. Null if approval not required or still pending.

attributes.name: purchase_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Procurement-to-inventory reconciliation: a replenishment order triggers a supply PO; linking them enables end-to-end order fulfillment tracking, AP matching, and delivery variance reporting. 'purchase

attributes.name: quaternary_replenishment_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user (manager or approver) who approved the replenishment order. Null if approval not required or still pending.

attributes.name: receiving_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user (typically BOH manager or receiving clerk) who received and verified the replenishment order delivery at the restaurant unit.

attributes.name: receiving_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user (typically BOH manager or receiving clerk) who received and verified the replenishment order delivery at the restaurant unit.

attributes.name: restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit placing the replenishment order. Links to the restaurant operations master data.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Replenishment orders target a specific storage location within the restaurant unit (e.g., replenish the walk-in cooler, not just the unit in general). This FK enables location-level replenishment trac

attributes.name: tertiary_replenishment_cancelled_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user who cancelled the replenishment order. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: tertiary_replenishment_created_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the user who created the replenishment order. For auto-PAR orders, may reference system user account.

attributes.name: triggering_stock_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: A replenishment order is triggered when a specific stock item falls below its PAR level or reorder point. Linking replenishment_order to the stock_item that triggered the order enables PAR-level analy

attributes.name: actual_delivery_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the replenishment order was received at the restaurant unit. Used for supplier performance tracking and variance analysis.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval workflow status for the replenishment order. Orders above threshold or emergency orders may require manager or regional approval before submission.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order was approved. Used for audit trail and approval turnaround time analysis.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why the replenishment order was cancelled. Captures business reason such as supplier unavailability, budget constraints, duplicate order, or inventory correction.

attributes.name: cancelled_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order was cancelled. Used for order lifecycle tracking and supplier performance analysis.

attributes.name: carrier_name
attributes.description: Name of the shipping carrier or logistics provider delivering the replenishment order. May be supplier-managed or third-party carrier.

attributes.name: confirmed_delivery_date
attributes.description: Supplier-confirmed date for delivery of the replenishment order. May differ from requested date based on supplier availability.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and order lifecycle analysis.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the replenishment order. Typically USD for US operations, but supports multi-currency for international units.

attributes.name: delivery_instructions
attributes.description: Special instructions for delivery of the replenishment order. May include receiving hours, loading dock location, contact person, or handling requirements.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order record was last updated. Tracks any changes to order details, status, or metadata.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: General free-text notes or comments about the replenishment order. May include special requests, context, or internal communication.

attributes.name: order_date
attributes.description: Date when the replenishment order was created or initiated. Business event date for order placement.

attributes.name: order_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique order number for tracking and reference. Format: RO-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX where YYYYMMDD is order date and XXXXXX is sequence.

attributes.name: order_source
attributes.description: System or channel that originated the replenishment order. Identifies whether order was auto-generated by MarketMan, manually created by manager, or triggered by integrated system.

attributes.name: order_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from creation through fulfillment or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|confirmed|in_transit|delivered|partially_delivered|cancelled|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: order_type
attributes.description: Classification of the replenishment order trigger mechanism. Auto-PAR: triggered automatically when stock falls below PAR level; Manual: manager-initiated; Emergency: urgent out-of-stock order; Scheduled: routine periodic order; Spot: one-time special order.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Payment terms agreed with supplier for this replenishment order. Examples: Net 30, Net 60, COD, 2/10 Net 30. Inherited from supplier master agreement or negotiated per order.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Business priority classification for the replenishment order. Urgent priority for emergency out-of-stock situations; high for items approaching stockout; normal for routine PAR replenishment.

attributes.name: received_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order was physically received and logged into inventory at the restaurant unit. Triggers inventory update and AP invoice matching.

attributes.name: requested_delivery_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the restaurant unit requests delivery of the replenishment order. Used for planning and scheduling.

attributes.name: shipping_fee
attributes.description: Delivery or freight charge for the replenishment order in USD. May be waived based on minimum order value or supplier agreement.

attributes.name: shipping_method
attributes.description: Delivery method for the replenishment order. Determines delivery speed and cost. Will-call indicates restaurant will pick up from supplier.

attributes.name: submitted_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the replenishment order was submitted to the supplier. Marks transition from draft to active order.

attributes.name: supplier_order_reference
attributes.description: Supplier-provided order confirmation number or reference. Used for cross-referencing with supplier systems and delivery documentation.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount applied to the replenishment order in USD. Includes sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.

attributes.name: total_amount_due
attributes.description: Final total amount payable for the replenishment order in USD, including order value, tax, and shipping fees. Used for AP invoice reconciliation.

attributes.name: total_order_value
attributes.description: Total monetary value of the replenishment order in USD, including all line items before tax and fees. Used for budget tracking and COGS calculation.

attributes.name: tracking_number
attributes.description: Shipment tracking number provided by carrier for the replenishment order. Enables real-time delivery status monitoring.

attributes.name: variance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the received replenishment order had discrepancies (quantity, quality, or item substitutions) compared to the original order. True if variance detected.

attributes.name: variance_notes
attributes.description: Detailed notes describing any discrepancies found during receiving. Includes short shipments, damaged goods, wrong items, or quality issues. Used for supplier dispute resolution.

products.name: adjustment
products.description: Records manual adjustments to on-hand inventory balances outside of standard receiving, waste, or count workflows. Captures adjustment date, adjustment type (positive adjustment, negative adjustment, write-off, theft, breakage, system correction), stock item reference, storage location, adjusted quantity, adjustment value ($), reason code, supporting document reference, adjusted-by employee, and manager approval. Provides a complete audit trail for all non-standard inventory movements and supports shrinkage analysis. [SSOT] Authoritative owner for concept 'adjustment'.

attributes.name: adjustment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the inventory adjustment record. Primary key.

attributes.name: adjustment_inventory_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the manager or supervisor who reviewed and approved the inventory adjustment.

attributes.name: adjustment_inventory_related_stock_transfer_id
attributes.description: Reference to the inventory transfer transaction that this adjustment corrects, if the adjustment is a transfer correction.

attributes.name: adjustment_inventory_related_transfer_stock_transfer_id
attributes.description: Reference to the inventory transfer transaction that this adjustment corrects, if the adjustment is a transfer correction.

attributes.name: health_inspection_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to foodsafety.health_inspection. Business justification: Health inspectors frequently mandate disposal of non-compliant inventory items, generating inventory adjustments. Linking the adjustment to the triggering health_inspection provides regulatory traceab

attributes.name: ingredient_lot_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient_lot. Business justification: Lot-level adjustment traceability: expiry write-offs, recall disposals, and shrinkage adjustments must reference the specific ingredient lot for HACCP compliance, recall scope determination, and accur

attributes.name: order_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: When an order item is voided post-prep or returned, an inventory adjustment is required to correct stock levels. Direct FK enables audit trails linking COGS adjustments to specific order lines, suppor

attributes.name: prep_usage_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.prep_usage. Business justification: Prep usage events consume raw ingredients and generate inventory adjustments to reduce on-hand balances of the consumed stock items. Linking inventory_adjustment to the prep_usage record that triggere

attributes.name: primary_inventory_adjusted_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Reference to the employee who initiated and recorded the inventory adjustment transaction.

attributes.name: primary_inventory_physical_count_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical inventory count record that triggered this adjustment, if the adjustment resulted from a count reconciliation.

attributes.name: redemption_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: When a loyalty free-item reward is redeemed, an inventory adjustment must be created to decrement stock. This FK traces which loyalty redemption triggered the adjustment, enabling COGS attribution to

attributes.name: refund_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to order.refund. Business justification: Refunds for food items trigger inventory adjustments (disposal or restock). Direct FK enables financial reconciliation reports matching refund dollar amounts to inventory cost adjustments, supporting

attributes.name: restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the restaurant unit where the inventory adjustment occurred.

attributes.name: reversal_adjustment_inventory_adjustment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the adjustment record that reversed this adjustment, if applicable.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the stock keeping unit (SKU) being adjusted.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific storage location (walk-in, dry storage, freezer) where the adjusted inventory is held.

attributes.name: waste_log_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.waste_log. Business justification: Waste events (spoilage, overproduction, expiration) directly trigger inventory adjustments to reduce on-hand balances. Linking inventory_adjustment to the waste_log record that caused it enables end-t

attributes.name: adjusted_quantity
attributes.description: The quantity of inventory adjusted, expressed in the stock item's unit of measure. Positive values indicate increases to on-hand inventory; negative values indicate decreases.

attributes.name: adjustment_date
attributes.description: The business date on which the inventory adjustment was recorded and applied to on-hand balances.

attributes.name: adjustment_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible unique business identifier for the inventory adjustment transaction. Format: ADJ-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN.

attributes.name: adjustment_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the inventory adjustment transaction was recorded in the system.

attributes.name: adjustment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the inventory adjustment indicating the nature of the non-standard inventory movement (positive adjustment, negative adjustment, write-off, theft, breakage, spoilage, system correction, transfer correction, count reconciliation, damaged goods, expired product, vendor credit). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: positive adjustment|negative adjustment|write-off|theft|breakage|spoilage|system correction|transfer correction|count reconciliation|damaged goods|expired product|vendor credit — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval status of the inventory adjustment (pending, approved, rejected, auto-approved).

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the manager approved the inventory adjustment.

attributes.name: approved_by_manager_name
attributes.description: Full name of the manager who approved the inventory adjustment, captured for audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The batch or lot number of the stock item being adjusted, used for traceability and food safety compliance under Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP).

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: The cost center code associated with the restaurant unit or department responsible for the inventory adjustment, used for Profit and Loss (P&L) allocation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this inventory adjustment record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the adjustment value (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The expiration or best-by date of the stock item being adjusted, relevant for adjustments due to expired product removal.

attributes.name: impacts_cogs
attributes.business_glossary_term: Impacts Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this inventory adjustment impacts the restaurant unit's Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) percentage calculation.

tag_set.value: Impacts Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Flag

attributes.name: is_reversed
attributes.description: Indicates whether this inventory adjustment has been reversed by a subsequent correcting adjustment.

attributes.name: is_shrinkage
attributes.description: Indicates whether this adjustment represents inventory shrinkage (unexplained loss, theft, or discrepancy between physical count and system records).

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this inventory adjustment record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text notes or comments providing context, details, or special instructions related to the inventory adjustment.

attributes.name: on_hand_quantity_after
attributes.description: The on-hand inventory quantity for this stock item at this location immediately after the adjustment was applied.

attributes.name: on_hand_quantity_before
attributes.description: The on-hand inventory quantity for this stock item at this location immediately before the adjustment was applied.

attributes.name: reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the specific business reason for the adjustment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: THEFT|DAMAGE|SPOIL|EXPIRE|RECOUNT|SYSCORR|XFERERR|VNDCRED|BREAK|SPILL|MISREC|OVERPOUR|PORTIONERR|TEMPFAIL|RECALL — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: reason_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative providing detailed explanation of why the inventory adjustment was necessary.

attributes.name: requires_approval
attributes.description: Indicates whether this adjustment type or value threshold requires manager approval before being applied to inventory balances.

attributes.name: supporting_document_reference
attributes.description: Reference number or identifier for supporting documentation (e.g., incident report number, physical count sheet ID, vendor credit memo number, manager approval form) that justifies the adjustment.

attributes.name: temperature_at_adjustment_f
attributes.description: The recorded storage temperature in Fahrenheit at the time of adjustment, relevant for temperature-related spoilage or HACCP compliance documentation.

attributes.name: unit_cost
attributes.description: The cost per unit of the stock item at the time of adjustment, used to calculate the total adjustment value.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure in which the adjusted quantity is expressed (each, case, pound, kilogram, ounce, liter, gallon, box, bag, pallet). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pound|kilogram|ounce|liter|gallon|box|bag|pallet — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The total monetary value of the inventory adjustment, calculated as adjusted quantity multiplied by unit cost. Positive values increase inventory asset value; negative values decrease it.

attributes.name: waste_category
attributes.description: Classification of waste type for adjustments related to inventory loss, used for waste percentage (Waste%) tracking and analysis (operational waste, spoilage, theft, damage, other).

products.name: prep_usage
products.description: Records the consumption of raw ingredients during BOH food preparation activities — the actual ingredient usage pulled from inventory to fulfill prep tasks and production schedules. Captures prep date, shift, station, stock item consumed, quantity used, unit of measure, theoretical usage (from BOM/recipe), actual usage, variance quantity, variance value ($), prep task reference, and recorded-by employee. Bridges inventory depletion with recipe/BOM standards from the menu domain, enabling theoretical vs. actual COGS% comparison.
products.reference: Ingredient Usage / BOH Consumption / Theoretical vs Actual
