attributes.name: kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.kitchen_station. Business justification: Station-level food cost and production analysis: prep usage is recorded at the kitchen station performing the prep task (grill, cold line, prep station). Enables theoretical vs. actual usage variance

attributes.name: menu_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to menu.menu_item. Business justification: Food cost reporting and COGS attribution require linking prep batches directly to the menu item being produced. While recipe_id exists, direct menu_item_id enables menu-item-level theoretical vs. actu

attributes.name: prep_shift_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Restaurant prep work is performed during a specific shift. Linking prep_usage to shift enables labor-cost-per-prep reporting, HACCP traceability by daypart, and variance analysis by shift. shift_code

attributes.name: primary_prep_recorded_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the employee who recorded the prep usage transaction. Supports accountability and audit trails.

attributes.name: recipe_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the recipe or BOM (Bill of Materials) that defines the theoretical ingredient usage. Links to the menu domain recipe master.

attributes.name: restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit where the prep activity occurred. Links to the restaurant operations domain.

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the raw ingredient or stock item consumed during prep. Links to the inventory stock item master.

attributes.name: stock_location_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Prep activities occur at specific BOH stations which correspond to physical storage/prep locations tracked in stock_location. Adding stock_location_id normalizes the prep station reference — stock_loc

attributes.name: actual_cost
attributes.description: The total dollar cost of the actual quantity used (actual_quantity_used multiplied by unit_cost). Contributes to actual COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage).

attributes.name: actual_quantity_used
attributes.description: The actual quantity of the ingredient consumed during prep, as recorded by the BOH (Back of House) team. This is the real-world usage that depletes inventory.

attributes.name: batch_number
attributes.description: The internal batch or production run number assigned to the prep output. Links multiple ingredient pulls to a single production batch.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this prep usage record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. Supports multi-currency operations.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: The expiration or best-by date of the ingredient lot consumed. Ensures FIFO (First In First Out) compliance and food safety.

attributes.name: haccp_compliant
attributes.business_glossary_term: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the prep activity met HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) temperature and handling requirements. True if compliant, False if not.

tag_set.value: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) Compliant

attributes.name: item_description
attributes.description: The description of the stock item consumed. Denormalized to preserve historical context even if the item master changes.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this prep usage record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.

attributes.name: lot_number
attributes.description: The lot or batch number of the ingredient consumed. Critical for traceability and food safety compliance under HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about the prep usage transaction. Captures contextual information for variance investigation or operational insights.

attributes.name: prep_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the prep activity occurred. Used for daily food cost tracking and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) analysis.

attributes.name: prep_task_reference
attributes.description: Reference code or identifier for the specific prep task or production batch. Used to group related ingredient pulls for a single prep activity.

attributes.name: prep_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the prep activity was performed. Enables shift-level and intra-day analysis of BOH (Back of House) production.

attributes.name: prep_type
attributes.description: The type or purpose of the prep activity. Distinguishes between batch production, made-to-order, PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) level restocking, and other prep scenarios.

attributes.name: prep_usage_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of the prep usage record. Supports workflow management and data quality controls.

attributes.name: quality_grade
attributes.description: The quality grade assigned to the prep output. Used to track prep quality and identify training or process improvement needs.

attributes.name: sku_code
attributes.description: The SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) code of the ingredient consumed. Denormalized for reporting convenience and historical accuracy.

attributes.name: temperature_at_prep_f
attributes.description: The temperature in Fahrenheit at which the ingredient was stored or handled during prep. Critical for HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) compliance.

attributes.name: theoretical_cost
attributes.description: The total dollar cost of the theoretical quantity (theoretical_quantity multiplied by unit_cost). Used for theoretical COGS% calculation.

attributes.name: theoretical_quantity
attributes.description: The theoretical quantity of the ingredient that should have been used according to the recipe or BOM (Bill of Materials). Used for variance analysis.

attributes.name: unit_cost
attributes.description: The cost per unit of measure for the ingredient at the time of prep. Used to calculate the dollar value of usage and variance.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the quantity used. Examples include lb, kg, oz, gal, L, ea (each), case, or portion.

attributes.name: variance_cost
attributes.description: The dollar value of the variance (actual_cost minus theoretical_cost). Quantifies the financial impact of over- or under-usage.

attributes.name: variance_percentage
attributes.description: The variance quantity expressed as a percentage of the theoretical quantity. Enables standardized comparison across different ingredients and recipes.

attributes.name: variance_quantity
attributes.description: The difference between actual and theoretical quantity used (actual minus theoretical). Positive values indicate over-usage; negative values indicate under-usage.

attributes.name: waste_reason_code
attributes.description: Code indicating the reason for any waste or over-usage. Used to categorize and analyze sources of food waste and variance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|spillage|over_prep|quality_issue|expiration|contamination|equipment_failure|training_error — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: physical_count_line
products.description: This association product represents the line-level detail record (Count Line Item) between a physical_count event and a stock_item SKU. It captures the operational result of counting one specific SKU during one specific count event — including the quantity physically found, the system book quantity at that moment, the resulting variance, and the per-SKU recount disposition. Each record is actively created by counting staff during the count event and is the atomic unit of variance investigation, food cost reconciliation, and COGS% reporting. This is the source of truth for what was actually on the shelf versus what the system believed was there, at the SKU-event intersection level.. Existence Justification: In restaurant inventory operations, a physical count event covers many SKUs simultaneously, and each SKU participates in many count events over time (daily spot-checks, weekly cycle counts, period-end full counts). The intersection — called a 'physical_count_line' or 'count_detail' — is a real operational record that staff actively create during each count, carrying counted_quantity, system_quantity_at_count, variance_quantity, variance_value, and recount flags that belong exclusively to the specific pairing of a count event and a stock item. This is a textbook M:N with a recognized business concept, rich relationship-specific data, and confirmed reciprocity in both directions.

attributes.name: physical_count_line_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Physical Count Line - Physical Count Line Id
attributes.description: Primary key for the physical_count_line association

tag_set.value: Physical Count Line - Physical Count Line Id

attributes.name: counted_by_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the employee who physically counted this specific SKU line. Supports accountability tracking and variance investigation — different employees may count different sections of the same count event.
attributes.references: Referenced in Product A description as 'counted-by employee reference' line attribute

attributes.name: physical_count_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Physical Count Line - Physical Count Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking this count line to its parent physical count event header.

tag_set.value: Physical Count Line - Physical Count Id

attributes.name: stock_item_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Physical Count Line - Stock Item Id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking this count line to the specific SKU that was counted.

tag_set.value: Physical Count Line - Stock Item Id

attributes.name: count_method
attributes.description: The method used to count this specific SKU line (manual entry, barcode scan, or weight-based). May differ per SKU within the same count event — e.g., produce counted by weight while packaged goods are scanned.
attributes.references: Product A carries a header-level count_method; the line-level equivalent belongs here as it can vary per SKU

attributes.name: counted_quantity
attributes.description: The physical quantity of this SKU found on the shelf during this count event, as recorded by the counting employee. This value belongs exclusively to the SKU-event intersection — it varies per count and per SKU.
attributes.references: Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship_data

attributes.name: line_status
attributes.description: Per-SKU lifecycle status of this count line, independent of the overall count header status. A count event may be submitted while individual lines remain in recount or disputed state.
attributes.references: Derived from count_status_per_sku identified in detection phase relationship_data

attributes.name: recount_required
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this specific SKU line requires a recount due to variance exceeding acceptable thresholds. This is the line-level flag; the header-level recount_required_flag on physical_count is an aggregate. Moving this attribute here resolves the ambiguity between header and line-level recount disposition.
attributes.references: Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship_data as recount_required_flag_per_sku

attributes.name: recount_required_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether a recount is required due to excessive variance or data quality issues. True if recount needed, False otherwise. [Moved from physical_count: The header-level recount_required_flag on physical_count is an aggregate indicator. The per-SKU recount disposition belongs on the count line (recount_required attribute), not on the count header. The header flag can be derived as TRUE if any line has recount_required = TRUE.]

attributes.name: storage_location
attributes.description: The specific storage location within the restaurant unit where this SKU was counted (e.g., walk-in cooler, dry storage shelf 3, freezer). Belongs to the count line because the same SKU may be stored in multiple locations and counted separately per location within one event.
attributes.references: Referenced in Product A description as 'storage location' line attribute

attributes.name: system_quantity_at_count
attributes.description: The book inventory quantity for this SKU as recorded in the inventory management system (MarketMan) at the time this count line was initiated. Snapshotted at line creation to preserve the point-in-time comparison basis. Cannot reside on stock_item (changes continuously) nor on physical_count header (varies per SKU).
attributes.references: Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship_data

attributes.name: unit_cost_at_count
attributes.description: The standard cost per unit of measure for this SKU at the time of the count event, snapshotted for variance_value calculation. Required because standard costs change over time and the historical variance value must be reproducible.
attributes.references: Implied by variance_value calculation — cost varies per count event, cannot reside on stock_item alone

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit of measure used for this count line (e.g., each, case, lb, oz). May differ from the stock_item default UOM if the item was counted in a different unit during this event.
attributes.references: Referenced in Product A description as 'unit of measure' line attribute

attributes.name: variance_quantity
attributes.description: The difference between counted_quantity and system_quantity_at_count for this SKU in this count event (counted minus system). Negative values indicate shrinkage or over-consumption; positive values indicate over-receiving or system errors.
attributes.references: Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship_data

attributes.name: variance_value
attributes.description: The dollar value of the variance for this SKU in this count event, calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the unit cost at count time. Feeds period-end food cost reconciliation and COGS% reporting at the line level.
attributes.references: Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship_data

domains.name: restaurant
domains.description: Master record for every restaurant unit — company-owned and franchised — including location attributes, format (QSR/casual/fine-dining), FOH/BOH configuration, operating hours, daypart schedules, equipment, throughput capacity, speed-of-service (SOS) benchmarks, table turns, cover counts, AUV, SSS, and comp sales. Operational anchor for brand standards and SOPs.

products.name: unit
products.description: Master record for every restaurant unit — company-owned and franchised. The authoritative identity of each physical location including unit number, brand, concept type (QSR/casual/fine-dining), ownership model (company-owned vs. franchised), legal entity name, trade name, opening date, closure date, current operational status, and geographic coordinates. This is the operational anchor for the entire restaurant domain and the primary FK target for all cross-domain joins (order, inventory, workforce, finance, franchise). One row per physical restaurant location. All other restaurant domain products reference this entity.

attributes.name: unit_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the restaurant unit. Primary key for the unit master record.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand. Business justification: Unit should reference its brand via a foreign key rather than a free‑text code; brand_id provides referential integrity.

attributes.name: brand_standard_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand_standard. Business justification: Units must be linked to the applicable brand standard for compliance tracking.

attributes.name: program_id

products.name: format_config
products.description: Defines the operational format, physical configuration, and total capacity of a restaurant unit. Includes dining format (QSR, fast-casual, casual, fine-dining), service model (counter, table, drive-thru, kiosk), FOH layout (total indoor seating capacity, outdoor/patio seating capacity, bar seating count, private dining room capacity, ADA-compliant seating count, table count, cover count), BOH layout (kitchen footprint sq ft, cooking lines, prep stations), drive-thru configuration (lane count, stacking capacity for vehicles), kiosk count, counter service positions, and maximum cover count per daypart. This is the single source of truth for all physical capacity and layout attributes of a unit — governs brand standard compliance, throughput benchmarking, labor staffing ratios, health department permit compliance, and fire marshal occupancy limits.

attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for this format configuration (e.g., 'Standard QSR with DT', 'Urban Fast-Casual').

attributes.name: kds_screen_count
attributes.description: Number of Kitchen Display System (KDS) screens installed in the Back of House (BOH) for order routing and production management.

attributes.name: kiosk_count
attributes.description: Number of self-service digital ordering kiosks installed in the restaurant.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this format configuration record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: last_remodel_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent remodel or renovation that affected the format configuration of the restaurant.

attributes.name: late_night_daypart_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant format operates during late-night hours (typically after 10 PM).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments regarding special considerations, exceptions, or unique characteristics of this format configuration.

attributes.name: olo_enabled_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant format supports Online Ordering (OLO) through web or mobile app channels.

attributes.name: parking_space_count
attributes.description: Number of parking spaces available for guest use at the restaurant location.

attributes.name: pos_terminal_count
attributes.description: Number of Point of Sale (POS) terminals installed for order capture and payment processing.

attributes.name: service_model
attributes.description: Primary service delivery model: counter service, table service, drive-thru, self-service kiosk, or hybrid combination.

attributes.name: sos_benchmark_seconds
attributes.description: Target Speed of Service (SOS) benchmark in seconds from order placement to order delivery, based on format and service model.

attributes.name: table_turn_target_minutes
attributes.description: Target table turn time in minutes for table-service formats, representing the average time from guest seating to table availability.

attributes.name: third_party_delivery_enabled_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant format supports Third-Party Delivery (3PD) integration with external delivery platforms.

attributes.name: throughput_capacity_per_hour
attributes.description: Maximum number of customer transactions the restaurant can process per hour under optimal conditions, based on format configuration.

attributes.name: twenty_four_hour_operation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week.

products.name: operating_hours
products.description: Scheduled operating hours for each restaurant unit by day of week and daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, 24hr). Captures open time, close time, daypart start/end times, holiday schedule overrides, seasonal hour adjustments, drive-thru-specific hours, delivery window hours, and last-order cutoff times. Used for order routing, labor scheduling, and SSS (Same-Store Sales) period alignment.

attributes.name: operating_hours_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the operating hours record. Primary key.

attributes.name: operating_capacity_config_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.capacity_config. Business justification: Operating hours records include expected_cover_count, throughput_capacity_per_hour, and target_speed_of_service_seconds — metrics that are defined authoritatively in capacity_config. Linking operating

attributes.name: operating_format_config_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.format_config. Business justification: Operating hours schedules are governed by the unit's operational format configuration (e.g., a QSR drive-thru format has different daypart windows than a casual dining format). Linking operating_hours

attributes.name: primary_operating_approved_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who approved this operating hours schedule. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: primary_operating_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit to which these operating hours apply. Links to the restaurant master record.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this operating hours schedule was approved, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: close_time
attributes.description: The time the restaurant closes for the specified day and daypart, in 24-hour HH:mm format (e.g., 22:00). May extend past midnight for late-night operations.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this operating hours record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: day_of_week
attributes.description: The day of the week for which these operating hours are defined. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: The daypart segment (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, all-day, overnight) for which these hours apply. Used for menu availability, labor scheduling, and Same-Store Sales (SSS) period alignment.

attributes.name: daypart_end_time
attributes.description: The end time of the specific daypart window (e.g., breakfast ends at 11:00), in 24-hour HH:mm format. Used for menu engineering and Product Mix (PMIX) analysis.

attributes.name: daypart_start_time
attributes.description: The start time of the specific daypart window (e.g., breakfast starts at 06:00), in 24-hour HH:mm format. Used for menu engineering and Product Mix (PMIX) analysis.

attributes.name: delivery_window_close_time
attributes.description: The time the restaurant stops accepting delivery orders (Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD)), in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from dine-in hours.

attributes.name: delivery_window_open_time
attributes.description: The time the restaurant begins accepting delivery orders (Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD)), in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from dine-in hours.

attributes.name: drive_thru_close_time
attributes.description: The time the Drive-Thru (DT) lane closes, in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from Front of House (FOH) close time for QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) units.

attributes.name: drive_thru_open_time
attributes.description: The time the Drive-Thru (DT) lane opens, in 24-hour HH:mm format. May differ from Front of House (FOH) open time for QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) units.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which these operating hours expire, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Null for open-ended schedules. Used for temporary hour changes and seasonal adjustments.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which these operating hours become effective, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for scheduling future hour changes and seasonal adjustments.

attributes.name: expected_cover_count
attributes.description: The expected number of covers (individual diners served) during this daypart for casual and fine-dining establishments. Used for labor scheduling and inventory planning.

attributes.name: expected_table_turn_count
attributes.description: The expected number of table turns (times a table is occupied and vacated) during this daypart for casual and fine-dining establishments. Used for capacity planning and revenue forecasting.

attributes.name: holiday_name
attributes.description: The name of the holiday for which this schedule override applies (e.g., Christmas, Thanksgiving, New Year's Day). Null if not a holiday override.

attributes.name: holiday_schedule_override_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether these operating hours represent a holiday schedule override. True if this is a holiday exception, False for regular schedule.

attributes.name: is_24_hour_operation
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant operates 24 hours a day for the specified day. True if open continuously, False otherwise.

attributes.name: is_closed
attributes.description: Indicates whether the restaurant is closed for the specified day and daypart. True if closed, False if open.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this operating hours record was last modified, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: last_order_cutoff_time
attributes.description: The time by which the last order must be placed before the restaurant stops accepting new orders, in 24-hour HH:mm format. Used for Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) routing.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this operating hours schedule. May include reasons for schedule changes, special instructions, or operational context.

attributes.name: open_time
attributes.description: The time the restaurant opens for the specified day and daypart, in 24-hour HH:mm format (e.g., 06:00).

attributes.name: schedule_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of this operating hours record. Active schedules are in effect; pending schedules are future-dated; expired schedules are past their end date; suspended schedules are temporarily disabled.

attributes.name: schedule_type
attributes.description: The type of operating hours schedule. Regular for standard weekly hours, holiday for holiday overrides, seasonal for seasonal adjustments, temporary for one-time changes, special_event for event-driven hours.

attributes.name: seasonal_adjustment_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether these operating hours represent a seasonal adjustment (e.g., extended summer hours, reduced winter hours). True if seasonal, False for standard schedule.

attributes.name: seasonal_period_name
attributes.description: The name of the seasonal period for which this schedule adjustment applies (e.g., Summer, Winter, Back-to-School). Null if not a seasonal adjustment.

attributes.name: special_event_name
attributes.description: The name of the special event for which these operating hours apply (e.g., Super Bowl Sunday, Local Festival, Grand Opening). Null if not a special event schedule.

attributes.name: target_speed_of_service_seconds
attributes.description: The target Speed of Service (SOS) in seconds for this daypart. Represents the benchmark time from order placement to order fulfillment. Used for operational performance tracking.

attributes.name: target_ticket_time_seconds
attributes.description: The target ticket time in seconds for this daypart. Represents the benchmark time from order entry to Kitchen Display System (KDS) completion. Used for Back of House (BOH) performance tracking.

attributes.name: throughput_capacity_per_hour
attributes.description: The estimated number of customer transactions the restaurant can process per hour during this daypart. Used for labor scheduling and Speed of Service (SOS) benchmarking.

products.name: equipment_asset
products.description: Inventory of all BOH and FOH equipment assets installed at each restaurant unit including all equipment types (fryer, grill, oven, KDS stations, POS terminals, refrigeration units, ice machines, espresso machines, drive-thru timers). Captures make, model, serial number, installation date, warranty expiry, last service date, next scheduled maintenance date, asset condition rating, replacement cost, software version (for digital equipment), and equipment-specific configuration attributes. Supports R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) planning, CapEx forecasting, PCI DSS compliance for payment terminals, and food safety compliance for temperature-critical equipment.

attributes.name: equipment_asset_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the equipment asset record. Primary key.

attributes.name: equipment_kitchen_station_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.kitchen_station. Business justification: Every BOH/FOH equipment asset is physically installed at a specific kitchen or service station. equipment_asset.location_zone (STRING) is a free-text approximation of this placement; the authoritative

attributes.name: primary_equipment_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant unit where this equipment asset is installed.

attributes.name: responsible_employee_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Equipment assets require a designated responsible employee for maintenance accountability, compliance certification tracking, and health inspection readiness. Restaurant operators assign equipment own

attributes.name: acquisition_cost_usd
attributes.description: Original purchase price paid for the equipment asset in US dollars, used for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) tracking and depreciation calculations.

attributes.name: asset_condition_rating
attributes.description: Current physical and operational condition assessment of the equipment asset based on inspection and performance criteria.

attributes.name: asset_tag_number
attributes.description: Externally visible unique asset tag or barcode identifier affixed to the equipment for tracking and inventory purposes.

attributes.name: compliance_certification_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the current compliance certification expires and recertification or inspection is required.

attributes.name: compliance_certification_number
attributes.description: Certification or approval number from regulatory bodies (FDA, NSF, UL, OSHA) confirming the equipment meets safety and sanitation standards.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this equipment asset record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: depreciation_method
attributes.description: Accounting method used to allocate the cost of the equipment asset over its useful life for financial reporting and tax purposes.

attributes.name: disposal_date
attributes.description: Date when the equipment asset was decommissioned and removed from the restaurant, marking the end of its operational lifecycle.

attributes.name: disposal_method
attributes.description: Method by which the equipment was disposed of at end of life, supporting asset lifecycle tracking and environmental compliance.

attributes.name: energy_rating
attributes.description: Energy efficiency rating or certification (e.g., Energy Star rating) indicating the equipment's power consumption performance.

attributes.name: equipment_category
attributes.description: High-level classification grouping equipment by operational domain (cooking, refrigeration, POS systems, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cooking|refrigeration|food_prep|beverage|pos_system|kitchen_display|sanitation|hvac — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: equipment_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or designation of the equipment asset (e.g., Fryer Station 1, POS Terminal 3, Walk-in Cooler).

attributes.name: equipment_type
attributes.description: Category of equipment asset distinguishing its primary function within BOH (Back of House) or FOH (Front of House) operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fryer|grill|oven|refrigeration_unit|freezer|ice_machine|pos_terminal|kds_display|espresso_machine|beverage_dispenser|dishwasher|ventilation_hood|warming_cabinet|prep_table — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date when the equipment asset was installed and commissioned at the restaurant location.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this equipment asset record was most recently updated.

attributes.name: last_service_date
attributes.description: Most recent date when preventive maintenance or repair service was performed on this equipment asset.

attributes.name: last_temperature_check_timestamp
attributes.description: Most recent date and time when temperature was verified for temperature-critical equipment, supporting food safety audit trails.

attributes.name: lease_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the lease or rental agreement for this equipment expires, requiring renewal or return of the asset.

attributes.name: location_zone
attributes.description: Physical zone within the restaurant where the equipment is installed: BOH (Back of House), FOH (Front of House), Drive-Thru, Storage, or Outdoor.

attributes.name: maintenance_frequency_days
attributes.description: Number of days between scheduled preventive maintenance services as defined by manufacturer guidelines or internal SOPs.

attributes.name: manufacturer_name
attributes.description: Name of the company that manufactured the equipment asset.

attributes.name: model_number
attributes.description: Manufacturer-assigned model number or designation for the equipment.

attributes.name: next_scheduled_maintenance_date
attributes.description: Planned date for the next preventive maintenance service visit based on manufacturer recommendations and SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) schedules.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or historical context about the equipment asset.

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status indicating whether the equipment is actively in use, under maintenance, or retired from service.

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the equipment is owned outright by the restaurant, leased under a capital or operating lease, or rented short-term.

attributes.name: power_consumption_watts
attributes.description: Rated electrical power consumption of the equipment in watts under normal operating conditions, used for energy cost forecasting.

attributes.name: replacement_cost_usd
attributes.description: Estimated current market cost to replace this equipment asset with a comparable new unit, used for R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) budgeting and insurance valuation.

attributes.name: serial_number
attributes.description: Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit for warranty and service tracking.

attributes.name: service_contract_number
attributes.description: Reference number of the active service or maintenance contract covering this equipment asset, if applicable.

attributes.name: temperature_critical_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this equipment maintains temperature-sensitive food products and requires continuous monitoring for HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) compliance.

attributes.name: temperature_max_f
attributes.description: Maximum safe operating temperature threshold in Fahrenheit for temperature-critical equipment, above which food safety is compromised.

attributes.name: temperature_min_f
attributes.description: Minimum safe operating temperature threshold in Fahrenheit for temperature-critical equipment, below which food safety is compromised.

attributes.name: useful_life_years
attributes.description: Expected operational lifespan of the equipment asset in years, used for depreciation calculations and replacement planning.

attributes.name: warranty_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage ends, after which repairs and parts are at the restaurant's expense.

attributes.name: warranty_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage begins for this equipment asset.

products.name: brand_standard
products.description: Defines the brand standards and SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) applicable to each restaurant unit by concept type and ownership model. Captures standard code, standard name, standard category (food quality, cleanliness, service, safety, brand presentation), applicable format (QSR/casual/fine-dining), compliance requirement level (mandatory/recommended), effective date, expiry date, governing body reference (NRA, FDA, OSHA), and linked SOP document reference. Operational anchor for audit and compliance workflows.

attributes.name: brand_standard_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the brand standard record. Primary key.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Connect restaurant.brand_standard by adding column brand_id (BIGINT) with FK to restaurant.brand.brand_id because brand standards must explicitly reference which brand they apply to. P10: connect_table: restaurant.brand_standard** - add col

attributes.name: superseded_by_standard_brand_standard_id
attributes.description: Reference to the brand_standard_id of the newer standard that replaces this one. Null if this standard is current and has not been superseded.

attributes.name: applicable_format
attributes.description: Restaurant format(s) to which this brand standard applies. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) for fast food, casual for casual dining, fine_dining for upscale establishments, or combinations/all formats. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|qsr|casual|fine_dining|qsr_casual|qsr_fine_dining|casual_fine_dining — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: applicable_ownership_model
attributes.description: Ownership model(s) to which this standard applies: company-owned units, franchised units, or all units regardless of ownership.

attributes.name: audit_frequency
attributes.description: Expected frequency at which compliance with this standard should be audited or inspected (e.g., daily temperature logs, monthly cleanliness audits, annual safety inspections). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|ad_hoc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: brand_standard_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the brand standard. Active standards are in force. Inactive standards are no longer enforced. Draft standards are under development. Under_review standards are being revised. Superseded standards have been replaced by a newer version.

attributes.name: certification_body
attributes.description: Name of the external organization or authority that issues the required certification (e.g., National Restaurant Association ServSafe, OSHA, Local Health Department). Null if no certification is required.

attributes.name: certification_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether staff must obtain a formal certification to perform tasks related to this standard (e.g., ServSafe Food Handler certification for food safety standards). True if certification is mandatory, False otherwise.

attributes.name: compliance_requirement_level
attributes.description: Enforcement level of the standard. Mandatory standards must be met for operational compliance and are subject to audit consequences. Recommended standards are best practices encouraged but not enforced. Aspirational standards represent future-state goals.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this brand standard record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date on which this brand standard becomes active and enforceable. Standards are not applicable before this date.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which this brand standard is no longer active or enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended standard with no planned expiration.

attributes.name: governing_body_reference
attributes.description: External regulatory or industry body that mandates or influences this standard (e.g., FDA, USDA, OSHA, NRA ServSafe, Local Health Department, ISO 22000). Null if the standard is purely internal.

attributes.name: guest_facing_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this standard directly impacts the guest experience and is visible or perceptible to customers (e.g., FOH cleanliness, service speed, food presentation). True if guest-facing, False if back-of-house or internal only.

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date on which this brand standard was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and compliance with current regulations. Used to track review cycles and ensure standards remain current.

attributes.name: measurement_method
attributes.description: Method by which compliance with this standard is assessed: direct observation (visual inspection), checklist (structured audit form), measurement (thermometer, timer), testing (lab analysis, swab test), documentation review (log verification), or guest feedback (CSAT, NPS).

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next formal review of this brand standard. Ensures periodic reassessment to maintain relevance and regulatory alignment.

attributes.name: non_compliance_consequence
attributes.description: Consequence or remediation action triggered when a unit fails to meet this standard. Ranges from warnings and corrective action plans to financial penalties, operational suspension, or franchise termination for critical violations.

attributes.name: owner_department
attributes.description: Internal department or functional area responsible for defining, maintaining, and enforcing this brand standard (e.g., Food Safety, Operations, Quality Assurance, Franchise Development, Marketing).

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Business priority level of the standard. Critical standards directly impact guest safety or regulatory compliance. High standards affect brand reputation or operational efficiency. Medium and low standards are important but less urgent.

attributes.name: sop_document_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or URI to the detailed SOP document that operationalizes this brand standard. May be a document management system ID, SharePoint link, or file path.

attributes.name: standard_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the brand standard into operational domains: food quality (taste, temperature, freshness), food safety (HACCP compliance, contamination prevention), cleanliness (sanitation, hygiene), service excellence (guest interaction, speed of service), brand presentation (signage, uniforms, decor), workplace safety (OSHA compliance, injury prevention), or equipment maintenance (preventive maintenance, calibration). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_quality|food_safety|cleanliness|service_excellence|brand_presentation|workplace_safety|equipment_maintenance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: standard_code
attributes.description: Unique business identifier code for the brand standard, typically following a category-number pattern (e.g., FS-001 for Food Safety standard 001, CL-042 for Cleanliness standard 042).

attributes.name: standard_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the brand standard, including the specific requirement, rationale, and expected outcome. May reference specific procedures, thresholds, or quality criteria.

attributes.name: standard_name
attributes.description: Full descriptive name of the brand standard (e.g., 'Proper Handwashing Protocol', 'Drive-Thru Speed of Service Target', 'Uniform and Appearance Guidelines').

attributes.name: target_metric_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the target metric value (e.g., degrees Fahrenheit, seconds, percentage, count). Null if the standard does not have a quantitative metric.

attributes.name: target_metric_value
attributes.description: Specific quantitative or qualitative target that defines compliance with this standard (e.g., 'Food temperature >= 165°F', 'Speed of Service <= 90 seconds', 'Cleanliness score >= 95%', 'Zero critical violations'). Null if the standard is qualitative without a numeric threshold.

attributes.name: training_module_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier to the training module or course that covers this brand standard. May be an LMS (Learning Management System) course ID or training document reference. Null if no formal training module exists.

attributes.name: training_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether formal training is required for staff to understand and comply with this standard. True if training is mandatory, False otherwise.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this brand standard record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for the standard, following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0). Incremented when the standard is revised or updated.

products.name: capacity_config
products.description: Defines the seating and service capacity configuration for each restaurant unit including total indoor seating capacity, outdoor/patio seating capacity, bar seating count, private dining room capacity, drive-thru stacking capacity (number of vehicles), kiosk count, counter service positions, maximum cover count per daypart, and ADA-compliant seating count. Used for throughput planning, labor staffing ratios, and health department permit compliance.

attributes.name: capacity_config_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the capacity configuration record. Primary key.

attributes.name: capacity_brand_standard_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.brand_standard. Business justification: Capacity configuration targets (sos_target_seconds, table_turn_target_minutes, drive_thru_sos_target_seconds) are set in accordance with brand standards. Adding capacity_brand_standard_id → brand_stan

attributes.name: capacity_format_config_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.format_config. Business justification: A capacity configuration is derived from and constrained by the unit's physical format configuration (e.g., foh_seating_capacity in format_config informs total_seating_capacity in capacity_config). Ad

attributes.name: ada_compliant_seating_count
attributes.business_glossary_term: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Compliant Seating Count
attributes.description: Number of seating positions that meet ADA accessibility requirements for guests with disabilities.

tag_set.value: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Compliant Seating Count

attributes.name: avg_party_size
attributes.description: Average number of guests per dining party used for capacity planning and labor forecasting.

attributes.name: bar_seating_count
attributes.description: Number of seats available at the bar or counter service area.

attributes.name: config_effective_date
attributes.description: The date from which this capacity configuration becomes effective for the restaurant unit.

attributes.name: config_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this capacity configuration ceases to be effective. Null indicates the configuration is currently active.

attributes.name: config_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes documenting special considerations, constraints, or context for this capacity configuration.

attributes.name: config_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the capacity configuration record.

attributes.name: counter_service_positions
attributes.description: Number of staffed counter service positions available for order taking and guest interaction.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: curbside_pickup_spaces

attributes.name: delivery_staging_capacity
attributes.description: Number of completed delivery orders that can be staged simultaneously in the designated pickup area for third-party delivery (3PD) drivers.

attributes.name: drive_thru_sos_target_seconds
attributes.description: Target time in seconds for drive-thru order fulfillment from order point to pickup window.

attributes.name: drive_thru_stacking_capacity

attributes.name: max_cover_count_breakfast

attributes.name: max_cover_count_dinner
attributes.description: Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the dinner daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.

attributes.name: max_cover_count_late_night
attributes.business_glossary_term: Maximum Cover Count - Late Night Daypart
attributes.description: Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the late night daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.

tag_set.value: Maximum Cover Count - Late Night Daypart

attributes.name: max_cover_count_lunch
attributes.description: Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the lunch daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.

attributes.name: outdoor_seating_capacity
attributes.description: Total number of outdoor or patio seats available for guest dining.

attributes.name: peak_hour_throughput_capacity
attributes.description: Maximum number of guest transactions or covers that can be processed during the busiest hour of operation.

attributes.name: private_dining_capacity
attributes.description: Total seating capacity in private dining rooms or event spaces within the restaurant.

attributes.name: sos_target_seconds
attributes.description: Target time in seconds from order placement to order delivery, used as a key operational performance metric.

attributes.name: table_turn_target_minutes
attributes.description: Target time in minutes for a complete table turn cycle from guest seating to table reset for the next guest.

attributes.name: total_seating_capacity

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was last modified.

products.name: pos_terminal
products.description: Master reference table for pos_terminal. Referenced by: loyalty.offer_redemption.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.payment_method_link.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.redemption.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.visit.pos_terminal_id, order.drive_thru_event.pos_terminal_id

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the POS terminal. Primary key.

domains.name: workforce
domains.description: Manages employee lifecycle including recruiting, onboarding, scheduling, time and attendance, labor forecasting, Labor% optimization, FTE tracking, certifications (ServSafe), performance management, and payroll integration via Workday HCM and Planday. Optimizes labor deployment across dayparts, BOH/FOH staffing ratios, and restaurant locations.

products.name: employee
products.description: Master record for every restaurant employee across company-owned and franchised locations. Captures full employee lifecycle data including personal details, employment type (FTE/PTE), BOH/FOH role classification, hire date, termination date, employment status, pay grade, home restaurant assignment, Workday HCM employee ID, declared availability windows (preferred dayparts, max weekly hours, blackout dates, cross-location availability), and current benefit enrollment summary. Single source of truth for workforce identity and worker profile across the enterprise.

products.name: position
products.description: Defines authorized job positions within the restaurant organization, including role title (e.g., Crew Member, Shift Lead, Kitchen Manager, FOH Supervisor), BOH/FOH classification, pay band, FLSA exemption status, required certifications (e.g., ServSafe), FTE equivalency factor, and whether the position is eligible for overtime. Serves as the job catalog for workforce planning and scheduling.

products.name: shift
products.description: Represents a scheduled work shift for an employee at a specific restaurant location, daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night), and station assignment (grill, fry, drive-thru, expo, host, bar, dish). Captures planned start/end times, actual clock-in/clock-out times, assigned BOH/FOH station, shift type (regular, overtime, on-call, training), break duration, scheduling source (Planday), and swap/coverage details (original assignee, covering employee, swap request reason, swap approval status, approval timestamp) when shift reassignment occurs. Core operational record for labor deployment, station coverage planning, Speed of Service (SOS) staffing optimization, and Planday shift coverage workflows.

products.name: schedule
products.description: Weekly or period-level labor schedule published for a restaurant location, representing the planned staffing plan across all dayparts. Captures schedule period (start/end dates), restaurant unit, total scheduled hours, scheduled Labor%, FTE count by daypart, publication status (draft/published/locked), and the manager who approved the schedule. Links to individual shifts for granular staffing detail.

products.name: time_entry
products.description: Captures actual clock-in and clock-out events for each employee per shift, sourced from Workday HCM time tracking or POS-integrated time clocks. Records regular hours, overtime hours, break time, missed punch flags, and manager approval status. Foundation for payroll processing, Labor% calculation, and compliance with FLSA/OSHA labor regulations.

products.name: certification
products.description: Single source of truth for all employee learning credentials, training completions, and regulatory certifications required for foodservice operations. Covers external certifications (ServSafe Food Handler, ServSafe Manager, allergen awareness, HACCP, alcohol service permits, OSHA safety) and internal training completions (new hire orientation, food safety modules, POS/KDS operation, BOH/FOH SOPs, LTO product training, management development). Captures credential type, issuing body (NRA ServSafe, local health department, internal L&D), delivery method (in-person, e-learning, OJT), issue/completion date, expiration date, assessment score, trainer/facilitator, and compliance status. Critical for food safety regulatory compliance, health department inspections, scheduling eligibility validation, and employee development tracking.

Captures request type, requested dates, approved dates, leave balance consumed, approval status, approving manager, and return-to-work date. domains.name: foodsafety
domains.description: Governs HACCP plan management, food safety audit results, health inspection records, corrective action tracking, temperature monitoring logs, sanitation schedules, allergen management, and SOP compliance via Zenput. Ensures adherence to FDA FSMA, local health department requirements, ISO 22000, and ServSafe standards across all restaurant units.

products.name: haccp_plan
products.description: Master record for each restaurant units Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) plan, including plan version, scope, approval status, regulatory framework alignment (FDA FSMA, ISO 22000, Codex Alimentarius), effective and expiration dates, responsible food safety manager, team members, and prerequisite program references. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for HACCP program governance across all company-owned and franchised units. Each plan undergoes annual review and revalidation.
products.reference: HACCP Plan — FDA FSMA / ISO 22000 / Codex Alimentarius

products.name: critical_control_point
products.description: Defines each Critical Control Point (CCP) within a HACCP plan, including the hazard type (biological, chemical, physical), critical limits (min/max temperature, pH, time), monitoring method, corrective action procedure, and verification frequency. Each CCP is tied to a specific process step (e.g., cooking, cooling, receiving) and HACCP plan version.
products.reference: CCP — HACCP Principle 2 / Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969

products.name: food_safety_audit
products.description: Header-and-line transactional record of each food safety audit conducted at a restaurant unit, including audit metadata (type: internal/third-party/health-department, date, auditor, overall score, pass/fail) and individual findings (category: critical/major/minor, regulatory reference, corrective action required, responsible party, due date, resolution status). Managed via Zenput. Supports QA compliance tracking, trend analysis, and regulatory reporting. Each audit contains zero-to-many findings as line items.
products.reference: Food Safety Audit — Zenput / ServSafe / ISO 22000 Clause 9.2

attributes.description: Timestamp when the loyalty member record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: account_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the loyalty member record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and change tracking.

attributes.name: badges_earned
attributes.description: Total number of achievement badges earned by the member through gamification activities, challenges, and milestones.

attributes.name: birthday_month
attributes.description: Month of the member's birthday (1-12). Stored without year or day for privacy. Used for birthday reward campaigns and personalized offers.

attributes.name: current_points_balance
attributes.description: Current available points balance that the member can redeem. Calculated as lifetime earned minus lifetime redeemed minus expired points.

attributes.name: data_privacy_consent_date
attributes.description: Date when the member provided explicit consent for data collection, processing, and storage under applicable privacy regulations.

attributes.name: direct_mail_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive physical direct mail offers and communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: email_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional emails. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: enrollment_channel
attributes.description: Channel through which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. OLO (Online Ordering), POS (Point of Sale), mobile app, website, in-restaurant kiosk, or call center.

attributes.name: enrollment_date
attributes.description: Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the start of the member lifecycle.

attributes.name: gamification_level
attributes.description: Current level in the loyalty program gamification system. Members progress through levels by completing challenges, earning badges, and accumulating points.

attributes.name: last_activity_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent loyalty program activity by the member, including points earned, points redeemed, or profile updates. Used for churn prediction and reactivation campaigns.

attributes.name: last_transaction_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent purchase transaction where the member earned or redeemed points. Subset of last_activity_date focused on revenue-generating activity.

attributes.name: lifetime_points_earned
attributes.description: Total cumulative loyalty points earned by the member since enrollment. Never decreases; used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.

attributes.name: lifetime_points_redeemed
attributes.description: Total cumulative loyalty points redeemed by the member for rewards, discounts, or offers since enrollment.

attributes.name: membership_number
attributes.description: Externally-facing unique membership identifier displayed on loyalty cards, mobile app, and customer communications. Human-readable alphanumeric code.

attributes.name: next_expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the next batch of points will expire. Null if no points are scheduled to expire.

attributes.name: nps_score
attributes.description: Most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Measures member satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.

attributes.name: nps_survey_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent NPS survey was completed by the member.

attributes.name: points_expiring_soon
attributes.description: Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for proactive member engagement and retention campaigns.

attributes.name: preferred_language
attributes.description: Member's preferred language for communications and app interface. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.

attributes.name: program_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership. Active: member in good standing. Suspended: temporarily blocked due to policy violation. Churned: member opted out or account closed. Inactive: no activity for extended period. Pending: enrollment initiated but not completed.

attributes.name: push_notification_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive push notifications through the mobile app. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: referral_code
attributes.description: Unique referral code assigned to this member for member-get-member campaigns. When shared and used by new enrollees, both parties receive bonus points.

attributes.name: sms_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional SMS text messages. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: status_reason
attributes.description: Business reason or explanation for the current program status. Examples: fraud detection, member request, inactivity, policy violation, system migration.

attributes.name: terms_accepted_date
attributes.description: Date when the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions. Required for legal compliance and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: terms_version
attributes.description: Version number of the loyalty program terms and conditions that the member accepted. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc.

attributes.name: third_party_sharing_opt_in
attributes.description: Indicates whether the member has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing purposes. True if opted in, False if opted out.

attributes.name: tier_effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the member achieved or was assigned their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and renewal cycles.

attributes.name: tier_expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the current tier status expires and member will be re-evaluated for tier qualification. Null for lifetime tiers.

attributes.name: total_visits
attributes.description: Total number of restaurant visits or orders placed by the member since enrollment. Includes dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, and 3PD orders where loyalty was applied.

products.name: tier
products.description: Reference catalog of all loyalty membership tiers (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) defined in the Restaurants loyalty program. Stores tier name, tier code, qualification threshold (points or visit count), tier benefits summary, tier multiplier for points accrual, tier validity period, and sort order. Drives tier assignment logic and benefit entitlement across the program.

attributes.name: tier_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the loyalty membership tier. Primary key.

attributes.name: program_id
attributes.description: P3: Connect loyalty.tier by adding column program_id (BIGINT) with FK to loyalty.program.program_id. P3: connect_table: loyalty.tier** - add column program_id (BIGINT) with FK to loyalty.program.program_id - tiers are meaningless without as

attributes.name: annual_fee_amount
attributes.description: Annual membership fee charged to maintain this tier status, if applicable. Null or zero indicates no fee. Used for premium tier monetization.

attributes.name: benefits_summary
attributes.description: Comma-separated or structured text summary of key benefits and perks associated with this tier (e.g., free delivery, birthday rewards, exclusive offers, priority support). Used for member communications and benefit entitlement logic.

attributes.name: birthday_reward_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive a special birthday reward (e.g., free item, bonus points, discount). True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in operational systems and reporting.

attributes.name: color_code
attributes.description: Hexadecimal color code used for tier branding in digital channels (e.g., #CD7F32 for bronze, #C0C0C0 for silver, #FFD700 for gold). Used in mobile app, website, and marketing materials.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the loyalty system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the tier, including value proposition, benefits summary, and marketing messaging. Used in loyalty program communications and member portal.

attributes.name: downgrade_threshold
attributes.description: Minimum threshold value required to maintain this tier status during re-evaluation period. Falling below this value triggers downgrade to lower tier. Null indicates no downgrade policy.

attributes.name: early_access_lto
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive early access to new Limited Time Offers (LTO) before general availability. True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this tier configuration was retired or replaced. Null indicates the tier is currently active. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this tier configuration became active and available for member assignment. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.

attributes.name: exclusive_offers_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive access to exclusive promotional offers and Limited Time Offers (LTO) not available to lower tiers. True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: free_delivery_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier are eligible for free delivery on Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) orders. True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: grace_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days after tier expiration during which members retain tier benefits before downgrade. Used to provide buffer period for re-qualification.

attributes.name: icon_url
attributes.description: URL path to the tier badge or icon image asset used in member portal, mobile app, and digital communications. Supports tier visual identity and gamification.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.

attributes.name: launch_date
attributes.description: Date when this tier was officially launched to the market and communicated to guests. May differ from effective_start_date for soft launches or pilot programs.

attributes.name: max_redemption_discount_pct
attributes.description: Maximum percentage discount that can be applied when redeeming points for rewards in this tier (e.g., 20.00 for 20% max discount). Used to control reward economics and prevent abuse.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this tier configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Bronze Member, Silver Member, Gold Member, Platinum Elite). Used in guest-facing communications and marketing materials.

attributes.name: points_multiplier
attributes.description: Multiplier applied to base points accrual for members in this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base, 1.5 for 50% bonus, 2.0 for double points). Used to calculate accelerated points earning.

attributes.name: priority_support_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether members in this tier receive priority customer support (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if eligible, false otherwise.

attributes.name: qualification_metric
attributes.description: The metric used to determine tier qualification (points accrued, visit count, total spend amount, or transaction count). Defines how qualification_threshold is interpreted.

attributes.name: qualification_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days over which the qualification threshold must be met (e.g., 365 for annual qualification, 90 for quarterly). Null indicates lifetime accumulation.

attributes.name: qualification_threshold
attributes.description: Numeric threshold value required to qualify for this tier (e.g., 1000 points, 25 visits, $500 spend). Interpretation depends on qualification_metric.

attributes.name: referral_bonus_points
attributes.description: Bonus points awarded to members in this tier for each successful referral of a new loyalty program member. Used to incentivize member acquisition through word-of-mouth.

attributes.name: rollover_points_allowed
attributes.description: Indicates whether unused points can roll over to the next qualification period for members in this tier. True if rollover is allowed, false if points expire at period end.

attributes.name: sort_order
attributes.description: Integer defining the hierarchical display order of tiers (e.g., 1 for Bronze, 2 for Silver, 3 for Gold, 4 for Platinum). Lower values represent entry-level tiers; higher values represent premium tiers.

attributes.name: target_member_segment
attributes.description: Description of the target guest segment this tier is designed to attract and retain (e.g., frequent visitors, high spenders, digital-first guests, family diners). Used for marketing strategy and program design.

attributes.name: tier_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily disabled; retired tiers are no longer offered but may have legacy members; pending tiers are configured but not yet launched.

attributes.name: upgrade_notification
attributes.description: Indicates whether members should receive automated notification when they qualify for upgrade to this tier. True if notification is enabled, false otherwise.

attributes.name: validity_days
attributes.description: Number of days a member retains this tier status after qualification before re-evaluation (e.g., 365 for annual renewal). Null indicates permanent tier assignment.

attributes.name: welcome_bonus_points
attributes.description: One-time bonus points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier. Used to incentivize tier progression and enhance member engagement.

products.name: points_ledger
products.description: Immutable financial-grade ledger recording every points movement for a loyalty member — accruals, redemptions, expirations, adjustments, bonus awards, goodwill credits, promotional credits, dispute resolutions, system error corrections, and fraud reversals. Each row captures transaction type (earn, redeem, expire, adjust, bonus, goodwill, reversal, correction), points delta, running balance, source channel (POS, OLO, 3PD), source order reference, campaign reference, expiry date of earned points, adjustment reason code, adjustment category (goodwill, dispute, error_correction, promotional, fraud), authorizing agent (for manual adjustments), approval status (auto_approved, pending_review, approved, rejected), processing timestamp, and related original transaction reference (for reversals/corrections). This is the single SSOT for ALL member point balance movements — no other product in this domain records points changes of any kind.

attributes.name: points_ledger_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for each points transaction record in the loyalty ledger. Serves as the immutable primary key for this financial-grade system of record.

attributes.name: accrual_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.accrual_rule. Business justification: points_ledger is the financial-grade immutable record of every points movement. For accrual entries, the specific accrual_rule that governed the points calculation (rate, multiplier, eligibility) shou

attributes.name: loyalty_member_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier of the loyalty program member whose points balance is affected by this transaction. Links to the member master record.

attributes.name: offer_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.offer. Business justification: points_ledger records every points movement including bonus points triggered by offer redemptions (e.g., double-points offers, bonus point campaigns). Without an offer_id FK, there is no way to trace

attributes.name: points_restaurant_unit_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the restaurant location where this points transaction originated. Populated for POS and kiosk transactions. Null for OLO, mobile app, or admin transactions not tied to a specific physical location.

attributes.name: pos_terminal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to restaurant.pos_terminal. Business justification: Points are earned at a specific POS terminal during a transaction. POS-level points accrual auditing, fraud detection, and terminal reconciliation reports require knowing which terminal processed each

attributes.name: primary_points_adjusted_by_user_employee_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the system user or customer service agent who authorized and executed the manual adjustment or reversal. Populated for adjust and reversal transactions. Critical for SOX compliance and fraud prevention.

attributes.name: primary_points_guest_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the originating order transaction that triggered this points movement. Populated for earn and redeem transactions tied to guest purchases. Null for non-order events such as bonus awards, expirations, or manual adjustments.

attributes.name: reversal_of_transaction_points_ledger_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original points_ledger_id that this reversal transaction is canceling. Populated only for reversal transaction types. Creates an audit chain linking reversals to their source transactions.

attributes.name: reward_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific reward item redeemed by the member. Populated only for redeem transaction types. Links to the rewards catalog to identify what was claimed (free item, discount, experience).

attributes.name: source_transaction_guest_order_id
attributes.description: The unique transaction identifier from the originating source system. Enables end-to-end traceability and reconciliation between the loyalty ledger and upstream systems. Format varies by source system.

attributes.name: tier_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the loyalty membership tier the member held at the time of this transaction. Used to determine earn rates, bonus multipliers, and redemption privileges. Links to tier master data.

attributes.name: adjustment_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code explaining why a manual adjustment or reversal was made. Populated only for adjust and reversal transaction types. Supports audit compliance and customer service quality tracking.

attributes.name: adjustment_reason_notes
attributes.description: Free-text explanation provided by the customer service representative or system administrator who initiated the adjustment. Populated for adjust and reversal transactions. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: batch_reference
attributes.description: Identifier of the processing batch or job that created this ledger entry. Used for bulk operations such as nightly expiration runs, mass bonus awards, or system migrations. Null for real-time individual transactions.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month or accounting period number) within the fiscal year when this transaction occurred. Supports monthly financial close, P&L reporting, and loyalty liability accrual tracking.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this points transaction occurred, derived from transaction_timestamp. Supports financial reporting, GAAP revenue recognition for breakage, and year-over-year loyalty program analytics.

attributes.name: is_voided
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this transaction has been voided or reversed by a subsequent transaction. True if a reversal exists, false otherwise. Immutable ledger entries are never deleted, only marked as voided.

attributes.name: order_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order total amount. Supports multi-currency loyalty programs for international operations. Null for non-order transactions.

attributes.name: order_total_amount
attributes.description: The total monetary value of the order that triggered this points transaction, in the local currency. Used to calculate points earn rate and for financial reconciliation. Null for non-order transactions.

attributes.name: points_balance_after
attributes.description: The cumulative running balance of points for this member immediately after this transaction was applied. Provides audit trail and reconciliation capability. Must equal prior balance plus points_delta.

attributes.name: points_delta
attributes.description: The signed integer change in points balance for this transaction. Positive values represent accruals (earn, bonus), negative values represent deductions (redeem, expire, adjust downward). Zero-delta entries are not permitted.

attributes.name: points_earn_rate
